Delta Fair Sharing is a family of algorithms designed for performance isolation in multi-tenant storage systems, addressing high preemption delays that lead to spikes in client tail latencies.
An open-source storage layer that brings ACID transactions to Apache Spark and big data workloads. It provides reliability, performance, and data quality guarantees on top of data lakes.

D.term: Demand- and Priority-Aware Adaptive Congestion Control
D.definition: A congestion control technique for Vehicle-to-Everything (V2X) communications that adapts based on varying service demands and priorities, improving traffic efficiency and road safety.
A dataset used for analyzing bias in acoustic representations for cognitive impairment detection, facilitating the study of performance disparities across demographic groups.
A physical or logical subnetwork that separates an internal network from an untrusted external network like the internet. DMZs host public-facing services while protecting internal resources.

A tree-like diagram used to visualize the results of hierarchical clustering. Dendrograms show the arrangement of clusters and the distances at which clusters merge, helping determine optimal cluster counts.

An attack that aims to make a computer resource unavailable to its intended users by overwhelming it with traffic or exploiting vulnerabilities. DoS attacks disrupt services and can cause significant business impact.

A runtime for JavaScript and TypeScript built on the V8 engine that aims to be a more secure and modern alternative to Node.js. Deno supports TypeScript natively and has a permissions-based security model.
A method for correcting click-through rate (CTR) and conversion rate (CVR) estimates in autobidding algorithms, addressing uncertainty in predictions to enhance performance.
The process of adding redundant data to a normalized database to improve read performance. Denormalization trades storage space and write complexity for faster queries.
A formal semantics framework for ODRL (Open Digital Rights Language) that facilitates knowledge-based constraint conflict detection through denotation intersection.
A family of reductions that increase the number of observed entries in problems like $k$-XOR while controlling the decrease in signal, facilitating connections to Tensor PCA.
Density-Guided Response Optimization is an approach for aligning language models with community norms by leveraging implicit acceptance signals rather than explicit preference supervision.
A supervised dimensionality reduction technique for categorical data based on density-matrix construction, which utilizes class-conditional frequencies to create low-dimensional spectral embeddings.
A software component, library, or module that another piece of software requires to function. Managing dependencies involves tracking versions, resolving conflicts, and ensuring compatibility.
A directed graph representing dependencies between modules, packages, or tasks. Dependency graphs are used for build ordering, package management, and understanding system architecture.

A design pattern where an object receives its dependencies from external sources rather than creating them internally. DI promotes loose coupling, testability, and modularity by separating object creation from business logic.
The process of tracking, resolving, and maintaining the external libraries and packages that a software project relies on. Tools like npm, pip, and Maven automate dependency management.

DEpiABS is a scalable, differentiable agent-based model that captures individual-level heterogeneity in health status and behavior while modeling epidemic processes. It aims to provide a balance between mechanistic detail, computational efficiency, and interpretability in epidemic simulations.
A bio-inspired leg design for quadruped robots that enhances leaping capabilities by mimicking the energy-storage mechanisms found in natural organisms.
The process of releasing software to a production environment where it becomes available to users. Deployment strategies include rolling updates, blue-green deployments, and canary releases.
An automated sequence of stages that code changes pass through from development to production. Pipelines typically include building, testing, staging, and deploying phases.
The process of marking a feature, API, or component as outdated and scheduled for removal in future versions. Deprecation notices give users time to migrate to recommended alternatives.

A model of arithmetic circuits that generalizes Waring decompositions and diagonal circuits, computing sums of powers of low-degree sparse polynomials. It is particularly relevant in polynomial identity testing and reconstruction tasks.
An efficient adaptation technique for multilingual speech recognition models that focuses on selectively tuning early and late layers to improve performance in low-resource languages.
A graph traversal algorithm that explores as far as possible along each branch before backtracking. DFS is used for topological sorting, cycle detection, and pathfinding.
DSMR is a training design that employs stateful recurrent attention and layer-wise memory distribution to enable long-context modeling for symbolic music generation.
A class of computational circuits characterized by a depth of three layers and a bottom fan-in of two, used to compute functions like Inner Product efficiently.
A framework for precise camera-controlled video generation that leverages 3D priors to avoid inconsistencies and degradation in video content during trajectory alterations.
This concept introduces an information-theoretic framework for caching in reasoning engines, focusing on the implications of premise erasure on capacity and coding efficiency.
An approach to scalable cooperative multi-agent learning that reduces variance in gradient estimates by leveraging differentiable analytical models of system states.
The process of converting data from a serialized format like JSON, XML, or binary back into an in-memory object or data structure. Insecure deserialization is a common security vulnerability.
The process of planning and creating the visual appearance, user interface, and user experience of a product. Design encompasses research, wireframing, prototyping, and visual design.
A reusable solution to a commonly occurring problem in software design. The Gang of Four book documented 23 classic design patterns categorized as creational, structural, and behavioral.

A comprehensive collection of reusable components, guidelines, and standards that ensure consistency across a product or brand. Design systems include UI components, typography scales, color palettes, spacing rules, and interaction patterns.
A human-centered approach to innovation that draws from the designer's toolkit to integrate the needs of people, technology possibilities, and business requirements.
Named entities that store visual design attributes such as colors, typography, and spacing as platform-agnostic values. Design tokens bridge the gap between design tools and code implementations.
An econometric framework for event-study analysis that incorporates staggered adoption diagnostics and sensitivity-robust inference under heterogeneous effects.

A novel framework that integrates structural editability with visual fidelity in graphic design generation, allowing for high-quality visual content while maintaining the ability to edit layouts effectively.
An interaction approach that distributes control across intent, input, and process in generative AI, aligning AI output more closely with designers' creative intent.
An interaction approach that allows designers to iteratively refine ideas in generative AI systems, improving alignment with their creative processes.

A personal computer designed for regular use at a single location, or the primary screen of a graphical user interface operating system. Desktop computing remains essential for professional and creative work.
A software application that runs locally on a computer's operating system rather than in a web browser. Desktop apps can offer better performance and deeper OS integration than web apps.

A graphical user interface implementation for operating systems that provides a complete workspace including a window manager, file manager, and system utilities. GNOME, KDE, and macOS are popular desktop environments.
A programming style that allows the passing of memory cell addresses as parameters, enabling more efficient memory management in both systems and pure functional programming.

A syntax feature in languages like JavaScript and Python that allows extracting values from arrays or objects into distinct variables. Destructuring simplifies code by reducing verbose property access.
A task that maps natural language phrases to 3D instances, enhancing fine-grained understanding in 3D visual grounding by leveraging detailed descriptions.

A workflow for managing security in software generated by large language models, involving the detection of issues, application of fixes, and verification of results, aimed at improving project-level artifact security.
A detection-gated pipeline that combines a YOLOv8-based detector with a U-Net segmenter for accurate glottal segmentation in high-speed videoendoscopy, ensuring robustness against false positives.
An algorithm that, given a particular input, will always produce the same output and follow the same sequence of steps. Determinism is important for reproducibility and debugging.
An algorithm for edge coloring in the CONGEST model that uses a reduced number of colors based on the maximum degree of the graph, achieving a polynomially close upper bound to known lower bounds.
A framework that enhances communication efficiency by analyzing the rate-reliability tradeoff in identification tasks over linear Gaussian channels.

Deterministic Parsing is a method introduced to reduce content effects in reasoning tasks for LLMs by transforming syllogisms into canonical logical representations, enhancing validity assessment in multilingual contexts.

The overall experience and satisfaction developers have when using tools, APIs, frameworks, and platforms. Good developer experience includes clear documentation, intuitive design, and helpful error messages.

This concept refers to the unique efficiencies that different developers bring to the training of large language models, which can significantly impact performance, especially at the frontier of model capabilities.

A local server environment used during software development that provides features like hot reloading, debugging, and mock services. Development servers simulate production environments without affecting live systems.
A method that allows devices to measure their distance from the user's body to adjust transmit power dynamically, enhancing throughput while complying with exposure regulations.
A technique utilizing 5G signals for monitoring human physiological activities, such as sleep, without the need for wearable devices, leveraging radio-frequency propagation sensitivity.

A set of practices that combines software development (Dev) and IT operations (Ops) to shorten the development lifecycle while delivering high-quality software. DevOps emphasizes automation, collaboration, and continuous improvement.
An approach that integrates security practices within the DevOps process, making security a shared responsibility throughout the software development lifecycle.

Browser Developer Tools — built-in debugging and profiling tools in web browsers that allow developers to inspect HTML, CSS, JavaScript, network requests, and performance. Chrome DevTools and Firefox DevTools are widely used.

DexterCap is an affordable optical capture system designed for accurately tracking dexterous hand-object manipulation, utilizing dense character-coded marker patches to overcome challenges posed by self-occlusion.
Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol — a network management protocol that automatically assigns IP addresses and other network configuration parameters to devices on a network.

DHEA-MECD is a deep reinforcement learning algorithm tailored for autonomous underwater vehicles (AUVs) that utilizes embodied intelligence to enhance tracking capabilities in complex underwater environments.
An optimization technique for Linear Echo State Networks that reduces computational complexity from O(N^2) to O(N) by reformulating reservoir dynamics in the eigenbasis of the recurrent matrix.

DIAL (Decentralized I/O AutoTuning) is a framework designed to optimize client-side I/O performance in parallel file systems by utilizing learned local metrics, reducing the reliance on extensive global runtime metrics.
A modeling approach that accounts for dialectal variability in low-resource languages, enhancing automatic speech recognition performance by addressing phonological and lexical differences.
A unified framework for automatic speech recognition in low-resource Taiwanese Hakka, addressing high dialectal variability and the challenges posed by distinct writing systems.
A state-of-the-art hybrid end-to-end neural diarization system built on a pre-trained WavLM model, designed for speaker diarization in challenging audio environments.
A method that employs deepfake-informed double machine learning to estimate causal effects of visual attributes in advertising, addressing biases from confounding variables.
A comparison showing the differences between two files or versions of a file, typically displayed as added, removed, and modified lines. Diff tools are essential for code review and version control.
A novel two-stage framework for reliable 3D reconstruction of occluded surgical scenes, utilizing 2D Gaussian Splatting for real-time performance and improved depth accuracy.

A scalable and efficient differentiable rendering engine designed specifically for bitmap images, enabling optimization of various bitmap primitives using a highly parallelized rendering pipeline with a custom CUDA implementation.

Differentiable CBDs integrate causal modeling with differentiable programming, allowing for modular system design that supports gradient-based optimization while ensuring correctness in cyber-physical systems.

A framework that integrates modal logic with differentiable programming, allowing systems to learn complex relationships such as trust networks and causal chains from behavioral data without manual specification.
A novel model for simulating sound scattering in synthetic spatial hearing systems, particularly effective in underwater environments, improving 3D spatial hearing accuracy.
A system designed to optimize parametric designs in spacecraft by modeling the effects of radiation pressure, enabling efficient design and optimization processes.
D-REX is a differentiable engine that facilitates learning dexterous grasping by bridging the gap between real-world dynamics and simulation, optimizing object mass identification.
A novel differentiable approximation to the zero-one loss function, allowing for gradient-based optimization in classification tasks by projecting onto a hypersimplex.
An inversion-free grid deformation method that optimizes differential weights to adaptively compress space, allowing for dynamic resolution allocation in grid-based representations.
A rapid technology for analyzing gas phase samples, particularly useful for extracting chemical information from complex volatile organic compounds, while addressing environmental dependencies.
A novel approach to vertex coloring in graphs that ensures differential privacy for edges, utilizing random initial coloring and the exponential mechanism for resampling colors.
A coreset that preserves the k-means objective under differential privacy constraints, with established computational lower bounds for its computation.
A method for generating Markov chain models that protects user data through differential privacy, ensuring that sensitive information is not disclosed while maintaining model accuracy.

DPPS is a lightweight differential privacy protocol for decentralized communication that adds calibrated random noise to shared information, enhancing privacy in decentralized networks while maintaining communication efficiency.

A framework for the continual release of distinct item counts in turnstile streams while maintaining differential privacy, allowing both additive and multiplicative error estimates to improve accuracy.
A method of securely exchanging cryptographic keys over a public channel, named after Whitfield Diffie and Martin Hellman. It is one of the earliest practical examples of public key exchange.
A regime-conditioned diffusion model for generating counterfactual limit order book trajectories, enabling stress testing and scenario analysis in financial markets.
A differentiable rendering framework designed for reconstructing transparent objects from multi-view images, allowing for efficient decomposition of geometry and materials.
A reinforcement learning approach that combines flow matching with an entropy regulator to generate competitive actions in real-time decision-making for autonomous driving.

D.term: Diffusion Actor-Critic with Entropy Regulator via Flow Matching (DACER-F)
D.definition: DACER-F integrates flow matching into online reinforcement learning to enhance the efficiency of generative policies, enabling competitive action generation in real-time decision-making.

A model designed to translate music from various instruments into piano versions using a diffusion architecture, incorporating pitch and loudness encoders to extract relevant features for high-quality timbre generation.
An innovative framework that integrates Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models with Deep Reinforcement Learning for robust portfolio optimization under stress scenarios.
A model predictive control approach that utilizes diffusion-based sampling methods in discrete combinatorial domains, focusing on feasibility constraints and planning horizon effects.
An anthropomorphic 3D-printed prosthetic hand designed for enhanced dexterity in typing tasks, aiming to replicate the intuitive control of human hands for precise manipulation.
An electronic document that uses a digital signature to bind a public key with an identity. Digital certificates are used to verify the ownership of public keys in SSL/TLS connections.
An electronic circuit that operates on digital signals using discrete voltage levels representing binary 0 and 1. Digital circuits form the basis of all modern computers and digital devices.
A proposed central bank digital currency for the Euro area, aimed at providing a secure and efficient means of digital payment while addressing privacy and technical feasibility concerns.
A computing paradigm that integrates computation directly within memory arrays to reduce data movement and enhance energy efficiency, particularly beneficial for deep learning applications.
A conceptual framework that views large language models as a distinct asset class, emphasizing the economic implications of data-compute complementarities and innovation pressures in the AI industry.

The promotion of products or services through digital channels including search engines, social media, email, and websites. Digital marketing uses data analytics to target and measure campaign effectiveness.
A mathematical scheme for verifying the authenticity and integrity of digital messages or documents. Digital signatures provide authentication, non-repudiation, and integrity guarantees.
The process of using digital technologies to fundamentally change how an organization operates and delivers value to customers. It involves cultural change as well as technology adoption.
A virtual representation of a physical object, process, or system that serves as its real-time digital counterpart. Digital twins are used in manufacturing, urban planning, and healthcare.
A digital replica of a physical system that protects private information while optimizing mobile transmission scheduling in energy-constrained environments.
A novel pipeline for generating synthetic images from high-quality 3D models and programmatically generated camera poses, enabling repeatable and quantifiable digital twin creation.
A digital representation of physical systems used for real-time monitoring and reliability management, particularly in the context of 3D integrated circuit packaging.
A virtual representation of a physical environment that integrates real-world data for accurate simulation and testing of wireless signal propagation using ray tracing.
A graph algorithm that finds the shortest path between nodes in a weighted graph with non-negative edge weights. It is fundamental to routing, navigation systems, and network optimization.

DIMAFx is an explainable multimodal framework for cancer survival prediction that produces disentangled representations from various data sources, enhancing interpretability while maintaining high prediction accuracy. It integrates histopathology images and transcriptomics data to provide insights into survival outcomes.
An architecture for multi-modal stance detection that separates stance information into textual-dominant, visual-dominant, and cross-modal shared components to improve performance.
A framework that guides the design of context-aware, scalable AI interfaces based on workflow complexity, AI autonomy, and AI reasoning, facilitating dynamic human-AI collaboration.
Techniques for reducing the number of input variables in a dataset while preserving important information. PCA, t-SNE, and autoencoders are common dimensionality reduction methods.
A previously unidentified issue in real-time operating systems where the maximum bandwidth of peripheral devices decreases as more tasks are added, potentially leading to data loss.

This framework proposes a method for distributed stochastic optimization under delayed gradient models, demonstrating that a pre-chosen diminishing step size is sufficient for effective optimization in federated learning scenarios.

D.term: Diminishing Step Size in Federated Learning
D.definition: A framework for distributed stochastic optimization that utilizes a pre-chosen diminishing step size to effectively manage delayed gradient updates from local agents.
A conceptual framework that explores the tension between parental care for children's prospects and the principle of equal opportunity, highlighting the complexities of managed inequality in society.
A method for processing sensory data on neuromorphic hardware that allows for the direct injection of analog signals, eliminating the need for power-intensive analog-to-digital conversions.
A technique for accelerating first-order primal-dual methods specifically for convex-concave saddle point problems, enhancing convergence rates through spectral properties of the coupling matrix.

A sparse subgraph that certifies connectivity in directed graphs, ensuring a minimum number of internally node-disjoint paths between any two nodes, crucial for understanding directed connectivity problems.
A graph in which edges have a direction, going from one vertex to another. Directed graphs model relationships like dependencies, workflows, and web page links.

D.term: Directed Graph Model for Text Visualization
D.definition: A framework for designing and studying time-dependent visualizations of textual data, focusing on the relationships between evolving narratives in digital news and social media.

A method that utilizes reinforcement learning to steer generative diffusion models for neural architecture generation, specifically tailored for directed acyclic graphs representing neural architectures.
An optimization technique for bilevel programs that does not require the convexity of the lower level program, allowing for more flexible problem-solving in hierarchical optimization scenarios.
A novel approach to enhance large language models with capabilities to understand speech from multiple talkers by integrating directivity, particularly in applications like smart glasses.

A target-specific condition that allows for efficient inference on scalar-valued targets after adaptive data collection, ensuring that estimators remain asymptotically normal and semiparametrically efficient.
A novel framework for designing continuously steerable differential beamformers using uniform circular arrays, incorporating directional derivative constraints to enhance target signal acquisition.

A hierarchical database that stores and provides access to information about network resources including users, computers, and services. LDAP and Active Directory are common directory service implementations.
A novel class of prior distributions for Bayesian Neural Networks that addresses limitations in interpretability and overconfidence by introducing structured sparsity.
A key parameter in active learning that governs label complexity for decision trees, analyzed under specific assumptions to achieve polylogarithmic label complexity.
A set of policies, tools, and procedures to enable the recovery of vital technology infrastructure and systems following a natural or human-induced disaster.

A generative AI pipeline that facilitates semantic communication by transmitting the meaning of physical signals over channels, adapting dynamically to channel impairments through autoregressive and diffusion-based algorithms.

This technique involves incorporating a fixed share of discounted sales of expiring perishables into demand forecasts to improve accuracy, addressing the challenges faced by grocery retailers in predicting actual demand.

A theoretical limit in fair division problems that constrains the value guarantees for agents in the allocation of indivisible items, specifically indicating that every partition yields high value asymptotically limited by Θ(√n).
A framework for adaptive hyperparameter selection in ill-posed linear inverse problems that balances bias and variance without prior knowledge of the nuisance function's smoothness.

A statistical technique used to model consumer choice behavior, employing methods such as multinomial logit (MNL) and mixed logit (MMNL) models to analyze preferences and intentions in various contexts, including drone delivery services.
A framework for sampling efficiency in score-based discrete diffusion models, focusing on convergence guarantees under continuous-time Markov chain formulations.
A novel mutation operator that enhances Quality-Diversity algorithms by incorporating discrete, gene-level crossover to improve solution discovery across diverse behavioral niches.
A proposed model that maps the structural phase transitions and computational complexity of random 3-SAT instances directly to the combinatorial topology of an N-dimensional Boolean hypercube.
A model that maps the structural phase transitions of random 3-SAT instances to the combinatorial topology of Boolean hypercubes, providing a discrete perspective on algorithmic complexity.
A representation framework that converts continuous surface electromyography (sEMG) signals into interpretable discrete tokens, facilitating robust muscle state analysis.
A framework for probabilistic programming that focuses on discrete probability distributions, enabling intuitive modeling of complex probabilistic scenarios.

A discrete-event simulation model is a computational approach used to analyze complex systems by simulating the operation of a process as a discrete sequence of events over time. This model is particularly useful for examining supply chain dynamics and disruptions in vaccine manufacturing.

A concept that establishes a connection between the stability properties of discrete-time algorithms and their corresponding continuous-time dynamical systems, ensuring that exponential stability in continuous time implies stability in discrete time.
A type of machine learning model that learns the boundary between classes directly from the data. Examples include logistic regression, SVMs, and neural networks used for classification.

DiSE is a self-evaluation confidence quantification method for diffusion large language models that assesses the quality of generated sequences by calculating the probability of regenerating tokens given the full context.
A pretraining approach for 3D CT vision-language models that uses contrastive learning and prompt-based disease supervision to improve alignment between medical reports and imaging data.
DERL is a framework for robust multimodal sentiment analysis that employs hybrid experts to adaptively disentangle multimodal inputs, addressing the challenges of missing or corrupted data.

A method in music generation that separates different aspects of audio, such as structure and timbre, to allow for controllable synthesis. This approach enhances the ability to manipulate audio characteristics during generation.
A compositional approach in Behavior-Driven Development (BDD) that combines transition systems representing alternative system behaviors, preserving the testing power of original scenarios.

Disk-Resident Graph Approximate Nearest Neighbor (ANN) methods are designed for efficient search in large datasets where memory is limited, focusing on performance trade-offs related to storage, layout, and execution paradigms.
A benchmark challenge aimed at evaluating conversational AI systems in medical dialogues, focusing on understanding multi-speaker interactions in noisy environments.
An output device that visually presents information to users. Display technologies include LCD, OLED, LED, and e-ink, varying in resolution, refresh rate, color accuracy, and power consumption.

A process by which a smaller company with fewer resources successfully challenges established businesses by addressing overlooked segments. Disruptive innovation transforms markets and creates new value networks.

D.term: Disruption Tolerant Networking for UxV Control
D.definition: A network control system that leverages disruption-tolerant networking principles to enhance the flexibility and reliability of unmanned vehicle (UxV) operations in challenging environments.
A general speech restoration model that utilizes disentangled speech representation and degradation-prior guidance to improve performance across diverse speech restoration tasks.
A fitness function introduced for the $(1+1)$ evolutionary algorithm, characterized by large plateaus and valleys, demonstrating the density of optimal mutation rates.

A lightweight model for phishing email detection that combines distilled MobileBERT with a BiLSTM architecture enhanced by multi-head attention, achieving semantic discrimination with fewer parameters.

DistillER is a knowledge distillation approach for Entity Resolution (ER) that transfers knowledge from large language models (Teachers) to smaller models (Students), optimizing performance while reducing computational costs.
A design approach for UI components that emphasizes creating variations that are both mimetic and distinct, facilitating easier instantiation and exploration of design spaces.

A measure evaluating the performance of mechanisms that partition items among agents based on their ordinal preferences, focusing on expected performance rather than worst-case scenarios to provide a more optimistic analysis.
A protocol designed for smart grid IoT networks that enhances reliability and energy efficiency by integrating chaotic frequency-hopping spread spectrum and link-adaptive quality power control.
A recording scheme that enables efficient record-and-replay of multi-threaded programs by synchronizing distributed threads without excessive synchronization overhead.

A model where components located on networked computers communicate and coordinate their actions by passing messages. Distributed computing enables processing large workloads across multiple machines.
A system architecture that supports simultaneous wireless information and power transfer (SWIPT) through continuous aperture arrays, enabling efficient energy and data transmission to multiple users.
A method for finding balanced separators in planar graphs that can be computed in a distributed manner, improving efficiency in graph optimization problems.
A novel technique for recording the execution of multi-threaded programs that minimizes synchronization requirements, facilitating efficient debugging and testing.
A configuration of multiple IMUs used for full-body shape sensing in vine robots, enhancing localization and configuration determination in complex environments.
A technique that partitions model execution across multiple edge devices to meet strict throughput and latency requirements for AI-driven workloads in extreme edge computing environments.
A consensus of replicated, shared, and synchronized digital data geographically spread across multiple sites, countries, or institutions. Blockchain is the most well-known type of distributed ledger.

D.term: Distributed LLM Pretraining During Renewable Curtailment
D.definition: This approach aligns the pretraining of large language models with renewable energy curtailment windows, utilizing excess clean energy for training across geo-distributed GPU clusters, thus optimizing energy consumption.

Distributed prompt caching is a technique that enhances local large language model inference on resource-constrained edge devices by sharing intermediate processing states across multiple devices, improving performance through cooperative caching.
DQGP is a method that leverages quantum computing to enhance the modeling capabilities of Gaussian Processes in multi-agent systems, allowing for better scalability and complex correlation capture.

D.term: Distributed Semi-Speculative Parallel Anisotropic Mesh Adaptation
D.definition: A distributed memory method for anisotropic mesh adaptation that separates meshing functionality from performance aspects, avoiding global synchronization techniques to enhance efficiency in high-performance computing.
An optimization technique that employs a distributed semismooth Newton approach combined with augmented Lagrangian methods to solve optimization problems across networks with local cost functions.
A systematic study and algorithmic approach for detecting and enumerating sub-hypergraphs in distributed settings, addressing theoretical and practical challenges in graph algorithms.

A system whose components are located on different networked computers that communicate and coordinate their actions by passing messages. Distributed systems face challenges like network partitions and consistency.

Systems whose components are located on different networked computers that communicate and coordinate to achieve a common goal. Distributed systems face challenges like consistency, partition tolerance, and latency.
A method of tracking application requests as they flow through distributed systems. Tools like Jaeger, Zipkin, and AWS X-Ray help developers identify performance bottlenecks.
A novel unmasking schedule for diffusion language models that adapts to the unknown dependence structure of the target data distribution, enhancing generation quality without prior knowledge.

This technique involves training large language models to predict multiple future tokens simultaneously, enhancing sample efficiency without increasing training time, thereby improving downstream capabilities.
A sequential prediction model that allows learners to abstain from making predictions when faced with potentially corrupted instances, balancing between stochastic and adversarial scenarios.

A technique that investigates the ability to recover predictive distributions over numerical targets from Large Language Models (LLMs) without relying on autoregressive generation, aiming to reduce computational costs.

Distributional Treatment Effects refer to the impact of interventions on different segments of a population, particularly in contexts where outcomes are not uniformly distributed, such as user engagement in streaming platforms.
A forecasting framework that minimizes worst-case expected loss by integrating parametric and nonparametric models under distributional uncertainty.

DrIGM is a principle in cooperative multi-agent reinforcement learning that ensures each agent's robust greedy actions align with team-optimal joint actions, addressing environmental uncertainties and enhancing performance in real-world applications.
A control method for three-phase AC-DC rectifiers that achieves rapid voltage regulation and current tracking under load and grid disturbances, utilizing a transformed error-state dynamics model.

This concept focuses on generating multiple (near-)optimal yet diverse median strings from a set of input strings, enhancing flexibility and robustness in decision-making across various applications, including bioinformatics and pattern recognition.
A high-level Python web framework that encourages rapid development and clean, pragmatic design. Django includes an ORM, authentication, admin interface, and many other features out of the box.
A competition framework focused on Bengali long-form speech recognition and speaker diarization, addressing challenges in low-resource language processing.
A Large Language Model-augmented system designed for real-time interactive enhancements in live streaming environments, featuring a three-layer architecture for rendering various visual effects.
Dynamic Link Library — a file containing code and data that can be used by more than one program at the same time. DLLs promote modularization and reduce memory usage.

Diffusion Model-based Generative Image Steganography (DM-GIS) is a technique that conceals secret messages within generated images using diffusion models, enhancing security against traditional steganalysis methods.

DMart is a decentralized scheduling framework for datacenter networks that allows applications to independently encode urgency, treating network bandwidth as a competitive marketplace to optimize diverse performance goals.

Domain Name System — the internet's phone book that translates human-readable domain names into IP addresses. When you type a URL, DNS servers resolve it to the correct server address, enabling web navigation.

Doc2Spec is a multi-agent framework that utilizes large language models to automatically generate formal programming specifications from natural language descriptions, streamlining the process of ensuring API compliance with programming rules.

A platform for building, running, and managing containerized applications. Docker packages software into standardized units called containers that include everything needed to run — code, runtime, libraries, and system tools.
A taxonomy of document chunking methods critical for dense retrieval systems, including LLM-guided and contextualized strategies that aim to preserve contextual information during segmentation.
A type of NoSQL database that stores data as semi-structured documents, typically in JSON or BSON format. MongoDB and CouchDB are popular document stores that offer flexible schemas.
Written text that explains how software works, how to use it, and how to develop with it. Good documentation includes API references, tutorials, guides, and architecture overviews.
Document Object Model — a programming interface for web documents that represents the page as a tree of nodes. JavaScript interacts with the DOM to dynamically update content, structure, and styling.
A specific area of knowledge or activity that a software system is designed to address. In Domain-Driven Design, the domain model represents business concepts and rules.
A human-readable address used to identify a location on the internet, such as example.com. Domain names are translated to IP addresses by the Domain Name System.
A technique that reduces the complexity of logical constraints in neural networks by slicing the domain, which accelerates the generation of explanations for model predictions.
A novel approach for enhancing Physics-Informed Neural Networks by utilizing domain-specific Fourier features for positional encoding, improving training and interpretability.

A method for multi-domain all-in-one image restoration that constructs a task prompt pool to facilitate the handling of various restoration tasks within a single model, enhancing adaptability across different image domains.

A software development approach that focuses on modeling software to match the business domain it serves. DDD introduces concepts like bounded contexts, aggregates, and ubiquitous language to bridge the gap between technical and business teams.
An efficient interior-point method for solving hyperbolic programming problems, featuring a new straight-line program representation for compact and efficient computation.

A computational problem that involves determining the minimum size of a dominating set in a graph, where a dominating set is a subset of vertices such that every vertex not in the set is adjacent to at least one vertex in the set.
A modulation technique designed for uplink multiple access over doubly-dispersive channels, addressing challenges related to high peak-to-average power ratio and detection complexity.
Denial of Service — an attack that disrupts normal functioning of a targeted server, service, or network by overwhelming it with a flood of traffic or exploiting vulnerabilities to crash the system.
A technique that allows a function to be dynamically dispatched based on the runtime types of two objects rather than one. The visitor pattern is the most common implementation of double dispatch.

Double-BD-RIS (Beyond Diagonal Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface) refers to a multi-user MIMO communication setup that enhances performance through inter-element connections and cooperative beamforming among multiple BD-RISs.
A novel approach in radio SLAM that utilizes double-bounce non-line-of-sight paths to enhance localization and mapping accuracy, deriving bounds and algorithms for improved performance.
A potential flaw in digital cash schemes where the same single digital token can be spent more than once. Blockchain technology was designed specifically to solve the double-spending problem.
The transfer of data from a remote server to a local device over a network. Download speeds are measured in bits per second and depend on network bandwidth and server capacity.

The process of reducing the number of samples in a dataset, either by reducing the resolution of signals or by selecting a subset of data points. Used to handle class imbalance and reduce dataset size.

D.term: Downstream Task Inspired Underwater Image Enhancement
D.definition: This approach focuses on enhancing underwater images specifically for downstream tasks like semantic segmentation and object detection, addressing challenges such as blurring and color inconsistencies to improve target recognizability.
A period when a system, server, or service is unavailable or not operational. Minimizing downtime is a primary goal of high availability architectures and disaster recovery planning.

Differentially Private Stochastic Gradient Descent (DP-SGD) is an optimization algorithm that incorporates differential privacy into the training process, achieving a Privacy-Utility Trade-Off of O(1/ε²).

Data Plane Development Kit — a set of libraries and drivers for fast packet processing that bypasses the kernel network stack. DPDK enables high-performance networking applications running in user space.

DPSQL+ is a privacy-preserving SQL library that enforces differential privacy while adhering to governance requirements such as the minimum frequency rule, ensuring that released query results do not expose sensitive information.
A framework for optimizing machine learning inference on FPGA-based systems using reinforcement learning to efficiently allocate computational resources for deep learning applications.
A novel approach to enhance reasoning in long chain-of-thought models by addressing systematic overthinking and optimizing reasoning budgets without post hoc techniques.

The TREC 2025 DRAGUN Track focuses on the development and evaluation of assistive Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems aimed at helping readers assess the trustworthiness of news articles by generating well-attributed reports.
DRAMA (Domain Retrieval using Adaptive Module Allocation) is a framework designed to enhance the scalability and efficiency of neural information retrieval models across multiple domains.

DRAMatic is a Processing-in-Memory (PIM) architecture designed to accelerate homomorphic encryption operations by integrating processing units and memory on the same chip, enhancing memory bandwidth and reducing computational overhead.
A formal description framework for DRAM protocols using timed Petri nets, providing an expressive model to understand complex memory bank operations and protocol specifications.
A QA benchmark that evaluates vision-language models on real students' handwritten responses to math problems, highlighting models' weaknesses in understanding and diagnosing student errors.

DREAM is a unified framework that integrates visual representation learning and text-to-image generation, optimizing both discriminative and generative objectives through techniques like Masking Warmup.

Dream2Learn is a framework that enables continual learning by allowing models to autonomously generate structured synthetic experiences from internal representations, facilitating self-improvement without reconstructing past data.

DreamFlow is a deep reinforcement learning-based local navigation framework that enhances a robot's perceptual horizon through conditional flow matching, allowing for improved navigation in cluttered environments.

A continuous framework for structural graph refinement that generalizes the Weisfeiler-Lehman hierarchy, enabling the distinction of graphs that 1-WL cannot separate, thus enhancing graph isomorphism testing.

DReX is an explainable multimodal recommendation framework that processes diverse data sources while addressing challenges such as cold-start and data sparsity by aligning user and item representations across different modalities.
A conditional diffusion framework for trajectory generation in mobile robots, designed to balance kinematic smoothness and terminal precision in unstructured environments.

A method for generating counterfactual explanations in autonomous driving scenarios, which identifies minimal semantic changes required to alter a model's decision using gradient-based optimization on continuous embeddings.

A novel numerical encoding method for large language models in autonomous driving, aimed at improving numerical reasoning and precision in sensor measurement processing and control command generation.

Software that allows an operating system to communicate with hardware devices. Device drivers translate generic OS commands into device-specific instructions, enabling hardware like printers, GPUs, and network cards to function.
A system designed for automated trains to detect collisions and driving-over events, integrating new technological components for enhanced safety and automation.
This approach utilizes deep reinforcement learning to enhance user throughput in multi-panel mmWave radio access networks by optimizing dynamic beam selection and management strategies.

An algorithm that integrates deep reinforcement learning with block coordinate descent techniques to solve nonconvex optimization problems in wireless networks, enhancing decision-making capabilities.
Digital Rights Management — technologies that control access to copyrighted digital content. DRM systems restrict copying, sharing, and modification of digital media like music, video, and software.

DrPose is a Direct Reward fine-tuning algorithm that enhances the pose accuracy of single-view 3D human reconstruction models by enabling post-training on diverse poses without the need for extensive 3D human datasets.
Don't Repeat Yourself — a software development principle aimed at reducing repetition of information. DRY promotes the creation of abstractions and the elimination of redundant code.
A modular distillation framework for one-step flow matching in speech synthesis, designed to reduce computational costs and improve efficiency in generating high-quality speech outputs.

DSLean is a framework designed for bidirectional translation between expressions in the Lean proof assistant and external domain-specific languages (DSLs), simplifying the interaction between proof assistants and automation tools.

Digital Signal Processing — the manipulation of signals such as audio, video, and sensor data using mathematical algorithms. DSP is used in telecommunications, audio engineering, and image processing.

The DSPy optimization framework provides a systematic approach to instruction optimization for large language models, enhancing reasoning performance through various prompting techniques for tasks like tabular fact verification.

DTAPP-IICR is a Delivery-Time Aware Prioritized Planning method designed for preflight planning of UAV fleets in dynamic airspace. It incorporates incremental and iterative conflict resolution to enhance scalability and flexibility in Unmanned Traffic Management.

DTBench is a synthetic benchmark designed for evaluating document-to-table extraction capabilities, focusing on the ability to derive structured tables from unstructured documents while addressing reasoning and conflict resolution challenges.
DTT-BSR is a hybrid generative adversarial network that integrates rotary positional embeddings with a dual-path band-split recurrent neural network for effective music source restoration.
A benchmark specifically designed for type inhabitation in dependent type theory, providing a standard for evaluating implementations and performance in type-checking scenarios.

A concept in multi-period multi-asset martingale optimal transport that establishes the existence of dual optimizers under specific conditions, extending previous duality results to more complex financial scenarios.
A framework that integrates liquid-capacitance-extended models to enhance the interpretability of recurrent neural networks (RNNs), particularly in bio-inspired models.

D.term: Dual-Hop Joint VLC and Backscatter Communication
D.definition: A communication framework that combines visible light communication (VLC) with backscatter communication (BC) to enable energy-neutral data transmission in Ambient Internet of Things (A-IoT) deployments.
An algorithm that incorporates momentum into single-pass stochastic gradient descent for generalized linear prediction, achieving improved optimization performance in streaming settings.
A framework for audio-visual video highlight detection that utilizes two pathways in audio encoding: one for semantic understanding and another for capturing dynamic audio characteristics.

D.term: Dual-Pathway Audio Encoders for Video Highlight Detection (DAViHD)
D.definition: DAViHD is a framework that employs dual-pathway audio encoders to enhance audio-visual video highlight detection by utilizing both semantic and dynamic pathways.
A phenomenon observed in large language models where early memories are prioritized over recent ones during conflicting information processing, contrasting with human memory patterns.
A novel memory wear-leveling method inspired by the golden ratio, designed to extend the lifespan of computer memory by ensuring even wear across memory cells.

A Bayesian inference technique for constrained inverse problems that utilizes dual-space sampling to characterize the posterior distribution, particularly effective in scenarios with noisy and incomplete data.

A novel negative sampling technique for implicit collaborative filtering that utilizes large language models without relying on textual information or fine-tuning, enhancing the sampling process for better recommendation performance.

Dual-View Predictive Diffusion (DVPD) is a lightweight model for speech enhancement that exploits the dual nature of spectrograms, treating them as both visual textures and frequency-domain representations to improve efficiency.

An inference system designed to alleviate storage bandwidth bottlenecks in agentic LLM inference by introducing dual-path KV-Cache loading, optimizing the flow of data between storage and processing units.

A lightweight framework designed to detect targeted attacks on black-box Large Language Models via a dual entropy lull pattern, enabling effective defense without high access rights or significant performance costs.
A style of dynamic typing where an object's suitability is determined by the presence of certain methods and properties rather than its actual type. The name comes from the duck test.

An in-process SQL OLAP database management system designed for analytical query workloads. DuckDB runs embedded within applications like SQLite but is optimized for columnar storage and vectorized execution.

Duel-Evolve is an evolutionary optimization algorithm designed for large language models that utilizes self-preferences through pairwise comparisons to optimize outputs at test time without relying on external scalar evaluators.
DuoMo is a generative method that reconstructs human motion in world-space coordinates from noisy video inputs by factorizing motion learning into camera-space and world-space diffusion models.

A design and fabrication method that integrates Fused Deposition Modeling (FDM) printing with pneumatic actuation to create shape-changing interfaces, allowing for complex functionalities through a single fabrication process.
In database systems, the guarantee that once a transaction has been committed, it will remain so even in the event of power loss, crashes, or errors. Durability is the D in ACID properties.

A dynamic weighted graph structure embedding technique that integrates attention mechanisms and frequency-domain MLPs to enhance traffic forecasting by capturing evolving spatial-temporal dependencies.

Developer Experience — the overall experience developers have when using tools, APIs, SDKs, and platforms. Good DX includes clear documentation, intuitive APIs, helpful error messages, and efficient workflows.

D.term: Dynamic Access Point Selection and Pilot Power Allocation (DAPPA)
D.definition: A framework for dynamic access point selection and pilot power allocation in cell-free massive MIMO systems, aimed at mitigating inter-user interference and enhancing spectral efficiency through hierarchical correlation-based clustering.
A technique for analyzing Android apps that addresses the challenges posed by packers, enabling runtime behavior capture while mitigating anti-analysis techniques.

D.term: Dynamic Attention with Global Registers for Video Quality Assessment (DAGR-VQA)
D.definition: DAGR-VQA is a novel framework that integrates register-tokens directly into a convolutional backbone for spatio-temporal dynamic saliency in no-reference video quality assessment, enhancing the contextual understanding of video signals.

DBNs are a class of probabilistic graphical models that represent a set of variables and their conditional dependencies via a directed acyclic graph. They are extended in this context to estimate market risk measures like expected shortfall.

A technique used in speech enhancement networks to reduce computational load by adaptively disabling specific channels based on current signal properties, enhancing efficiency without compromising performance.

DCDP is a framework that integrates chunk-based action generation with real-time corrections for robotic manipulation tasks, enhancing adaptability in dynamic environments through a self-supervised dynamic feature encoder.

A concept introduced to enhance situation awareness and decision-making in Uninhabited Ground Vehicles (UGVs) by creating an ontology-driven dynamic knowledge base that adapts to complex environments.
A novel approach to dense retrieval that adapts to new domains without the need for extensive retraining, addressing limitations of traditional fine-tuning methods.
DDFKs are used in fluid simulations to maintain divergence-free velocity fields while leveraging memory-efficient spatial representations, enhancing the simulation of incompressible fluid dynamics.
A technique for wearable navigation that utilizes a fingertip stimulation prototype with a grid of electrodes to render spatiotemporal patterns, enhancing interaction during walking.

D.term: Dynamic Enumeration of Regular Language Infixes
D.definition: A technique for dynamically enumerating infixes of a given word that belong to a fixed regular language, allowing for efficient updates through letter substitutions.
A novel framework for reconstructing speech audio envelopes from EEG data that utilizes dynamic temporal priors to improve fidelity and reduce noise in the reconstruction process.
A component of a semiparametric joint VaRES framework that captures time-varying tail severity in risk assessments, enhancing the understanding of extreme risk factors in financial contexts.

A novel architecture that enhances the performance of domain-specific accelerators by dynamically orchestrating data paths at execution time, addressing performance fragility with irregular input patterns.

D.term: Dynamic Flexible Assembly Flow Shop Scheduling
D.definition: A formal definition of a scheduling problem that addresses the coordination of part completion and product-level kitting in dynamic multi-product delivery environments.
A nonparametric revealed preference framework that allows for the valuation of goods considering habit formation, separating the effects of hedonic pricing from intertemporal choice.
A neuromorphic approach to solving the Edge User Allocation problem using an attractor network with a Winner-Takes-All mechanism and dynamic heuristic biasing.

This decomposition technique addresses all-to-all GPU communication bottlenecks in large-scale training clusters by optimizing scheduling and traffic management, improving overall communication efficiency in GPU-based systems.

A parallelism strategy that adaptively reconfigures communication groups and degrees of parallelism during the training of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs), addressing issues of load imbalance and hardware utilization.
Dynamic Importance Estimation is a technique used to enhance persona following in role-playing language agents by adapting character behaviors based on situational context.
A technique in Text-to-SQL systems that dynamically associates user queries with database values, improving the accuracy of schema and value identification in ambiguous queries.

The Dynamic Kernel is a component of a proposed quantum-native organizational principle that allows nodes in a Quantum Internet to construct and execute a local Plan of Action (PoA) for service intents through dynamic composition of micro-protocols.
A new mathematical concept distinct from traditional dynamical systems, which may provide insights into Turing incomputable computations and has implications in various fields of mathematics.
A parameterized dynamic logic designed for program verification that simplifies reasoning by directly applying operational semantics without the need for extensive rule redesign.

A novel speech enhancement strategy that employs a robotic arm to dynamically adjust the geometry of a microphone array in response to varying acoustic conditions, improving speech recognition in noisy environments.

D.term: Dynamic Model of Purpose at Work
D.definition: A theoretical framework that describes how firms can invest in creating purpose and meaning in the workplace to enhance employee motivation and productivity. This model integrates worker preferences for meaning with firm profit objectives.
A method used for modeling forward rates in bond pricing that dynamically adjusts parameters based on market conditions, integrated within a no-arbitrage framework.

A model-agnostic framework that utilizes state machines to dynamically adjust the personality expression of large language models in response to conversational context, enhancing interactive performance.
A methodology for making longitudinal and time-to-event predictions in clinical settings, utilizing joint modeling and landmarking techniques to inform treatment decisions.
A pricing strategy where prices are adjusted in real time based on supply, demand, competition, and other market factors. Used in e-commerce, ride-sharing, airlines, and hospitality industries.

An algorithmic technique that solves complex problems by breaking them down into simpler overlapping subproblems and storing their solutions. It optimizes recursive algorithms by eliminating redundant calculations.
A method that assembles modular components of small defender teams into larger forces efficiently in polynomial time, enabling scalable drone defense strategies against adversarial swarms.

An optimization algorithm designed for Schatten-$p$ quasi-norm regularized problems that dynamically selects step sizes and avoids costly singular value decompositions, improving computational efficiency.
A method for optimizing spatio-temporal forecasting by selectively training on a dynamic subset of samples, reducing computational inefficiencies associated with redundant data in large datasets.
A variable scoping mechanism used in SAS that resolves variable references at runtime by searching through active macro layers.
A method for optimizing sensor network performance by dynamically partitioning nodes into informative subsets to minimize battery consumption and adapt to changing conditions.
An innovative technique for optimizing FP8 computation in digital compute-in-memory architectures by dynamically predicting and adjusting bitwidths for aligned-mantissa representations.
A statistical modeling technique used to capture network spillovers in the context of Generative AI adoption and its impact on productivity and systemic risk in the banking sector.

An advanced neural network architecture that integrates Phase Lag Index-based Graph Attention Network for spatial modeling and Bidirectional Gated Recurrent Unit for capturing temporal dynamics, aimed at early detection of pornography addiction in adolescents.
A collaborative optimization technique for executing backdoor attacks in self-supervised learning environments, designed to enhance stealthiness and reduce detectability of triggers.
A machine learning-based approach that recommends stocks from the S&P 500 by dynamically selecting the top 20% based on selected stock indicators with good explanatory power.

A technique for addressing non-ideal reference issues in analog in-memory computing, focusing on mitigating systematic drift in weight updates during training by dynamically tracking the symmetric point.
A type system where type checking occurs at runtime rather than at compile time. Languages like Python, JavaScript, and Ruby use dynamic typing, offering flexibility at the cost of some type safety.
A method for analyzing articulated objects by separating dynamic and static components without prior knowledge of part mobility, enhancing segmentation and reconstruction quality.

DynamicAdaptiveClimb is an advanced cache replacement policy that dynamically adjusts the promotion distance of cached objects based on recent access patterns, outperforming traditional caching strategies like LRU.
An approach that enhances Vision-Language Models (VLMs) by leveraging graph topology representation preferences to improve their performance on graph-related question answering tasks.
A quantization framework designed to optimize multi-hop all-reduce operations in large model training by bridging the gap between quantization best practices and multi-hop aggregation.
A framework for generating first-order ambisonics from dynamic and acoustically complex 360-degree videos, addressing challenges related to dynamic sound sources and environmental effects.

A rethinking of Transformer architectures specifically designed for solving partial differential equations (PDEs), addressing the computational inefficiencies of traditional numerical solvers in high-dimensional settings.

E.definition: The buying and selling of goods and services over the internet, including online retail, electronic payments, and digital marketplaces. E-commerce platforms handle product catalogs, shopping carts, and payment processing.

A persistent compiler abstraction that utilizes e-graphs to represent equalities natively in intermediate representations, facilitating program optimization without losing discovered equalities during transformations.

A unified multimodal knowledge graph framework tailored for e-commerce applications, which constructs a specific knowledge graph and learns item representations through graph neural network-based propagation.

E-variable-approximability is a method for constructing e-variables for various distribution classes, enhancing statistical hypothesis testing. This technique builds on the concept of average-bounded randomness tests from Algorithmic Information Theory.

E2E-GNet is an end-to-end geometric deep neural network designed for skeleton-based human motion recognition, incorporating a geometric transformation layer to optimize motion sequences in non-Euclidean space.
A framework aimed at enhancing emotional expressiveness in virtual conversational agents, focusing on the integration of emotional context into responses to improve user engagement.
An evaluation strategy where expressions are evaluated as soon as they are bound to variables, as opposed to lazy evaluation where evaluation is deferred until the value is needed.
A data loading strategy where related data is fetched at the same time as the primary data, typically through JOIN queries. It avoids the N+1 query problem but may retrieve unnecessary data.
Enterprise Application Integration — the use of middleware and software technologies to integrate a set of enterprise computer applications, enabling them to share data and business processes.
An energy-aware adaptive antenna control framework utilizing reinforcement learning to minimize energy consumption in cell-free massive MIMO networks while ensuring user spectral efficiency.
The gathering of information about Earth's physical, chemical, and biological systems using remote sensing technologies including satellites, aircraft, and ground-based sensors.

Extended Berkeley Packet Filter — a technology that allows running sandboxed programs in the Linux kernel without changing kernel source code. eBPF is used for networking, security, and observability.

eBPF-PATROL is a protective agent that enhances runtime security in containerized and virtualized environments by providing context-aware syscall argument filtering and adaptive enforcement based on application behavior.
Elastic Block Store — a block-level storage service provided by AWS designed for use with EC2 instances. EBS volumes persist independently from the running life of an EC2 instance.
Elastic Compute Cloud — a web service from AWS that provides resizable compute capacity in the cloud. EC2 allows users to launch virtual servers with various configurations on demand.

Error-Correcting Code memory — a type of computer memory that can detect and correct common types of internal data corruption. ECC memory is used in servers and workstations where data integrity is critical.

ECCO is a framework for compiler optimization that combines interpretable reasoning with combinatorial search, enabling the construction of a Chain-of-Thought dataset to map code features to performance evidence.
A situation where users primarily encounter information and opinions that reinforce their existing beliefs, often amplified by algorithmic content recommendation systems in social media.
EchoVLA is a user-aware Vision Language Action model that integrates real-time user audio instructions with visual data to enhance decision-making in autonomous driving scenarios.

ECLD, or Edge Compact LLM Deployment, is a framework that combines structured pruning, low-bit quantization, and knowledge distillation to create deployable large language models for mobile edge computing environments.
A framework for detecting hallucinations in Large Language Models by assessing the mismatch between a model's semantic entropy and the evidence capacity, significantly reducing hallucination rates.

An open-source integrated development environment primarily used for Java development but extensible to other languages through plugins. Eclipse provides code editing, debugging, and project management.
The standardized specification for the scripting language that forms the basis of JavaScript. ECMAScript defines the core language features, with new editions adding capabilities annually.
An enriched dataset that augments traditional Amazon datasets with Product Carbon Footprint metadata, aimed at enhancing sustainability metrics in recommender systems.
The network of organizations, technologies, and tools that interact to create and deliver products or services. Technology ecosystems include developers, platforms, APIs, and user communities.
An annotated dataset of historical geographic coordinates extracted from 18th-century texts, aimed at improving the retrieval of geographic information from digitized early modern literature.
The deployment of artificial intelligence algorithms directly on edge devices such as smartphones, IoT sensors, and embedded systems rather than relying on cloud-based processing.
A problem or situation that occurs at the extreme ends of operating parameters or under unusual conditions. Handling edge cases properly is essential for robust software.

A distributed computing paradigm that processes data closer to where it's generated rather than in a centralized data center. Edge computing reduces latency, saves bandwidth, and enables real-time processing for IoT devices and applications.
A problem in software testing focused on achieving white-box coverage of control flow graphs while adhering to specific constraints between vertices, impacting feasibility and complexity.

A method for designing test sets that achieve edge coverage in control flow graphs while respecting additional constraints between vertices, enhancing the feasibility and computational complexity of software testing.
A serverless function that runs at the edge of a content delivery network, close to the end user. Edge functions reduce latency by processing requests near the user rather than at a central server.

A combinatorial algorithm for clustering datasets modeled as edge-colored hypergraphs, aiming to match node colors with edge colors. This approach presents an approximation factor better than 2, improving upon previous methods.

EdgeFLow is a federated learning framework that utilizes sequential model migration between edge base stations, eliminating the need for traditional cloud servers and reducing communication bottlenecks in distributed learning systems.
A compact graph representation that allows for efficient analysis of massive graph streams, providing unbiased estimators for key graph properties with controllable variance.
This framework allows for imperceptible text-based speech editing by decoupling content editing from acoustic fidelity, ensuring that modified speech segments blend seamlessly with their context.

EditEmoTalk is a framework for controllable speech-driven 3D facial animation that enables continuous emotion editing through boundary-aware semantic embeddings, allowing for smooth emotional transitions in facial expressions.
EditFlow is a benchmarking and optimization framework for code edit recommendation systems that reconstructs developer flows to improve technical accuracy and productivity.
A DiT-based framework for audio-driven video-to-video editing that allows transcript-based modifications of talking head videos, focusing on maintaining motion and lip synchronization.
A society-centric generative cognitive digital twin architecture that simulates social spaces in educational contexts, allowing for dynamic rule injection and complex social dynamics.

EduAIGV-1k is a benchmark dataset and evaluation framework for assessing the quality of AI-generated videos aimed at teaching foundational math concepts, comprising 1,130 videos generated from pedagogy-oriented prompts.

Educational assortative mating refers to the pattern where individuals with similar educational backgrounds tend to marry each other, influenced by factors such as geographic mobility and migration, particularly observed in urban-to-urban migrant populations in India.

EduEVAL-DB is a role-based dataset designed for evaluating and training automatic pedagogical evaluators and AI tutors, comprising 854 explanations for 139 questions across various subjects in K-12 education.

E.term: EEG-Based Hybrid Visual and Motor Imagery
E.definition: This framework integrates EEG-based visual and motor imagery to enable intention-driven robotic control, allowing real-time translation of neural signals into robotic actions for grasping and placement.

E.term: EEG-Based Hybrid Visual and Motor Imagery Control
E.definition: A framework that integrates EEG signals for real-time control of robotic grasping and placement, translating visual intent and motor imagery into robotic actions.
An extension of effective toposes that incorporates effectful computation, providing a framework to study computational processes with associated Lawvere-Tierney topologies.
A congestion-adjusted metric for two-sided platforms that discounts match probabilities based on user attention constraints, enhancing recommendation efficiency.
A measure of the number of independent spatial channels available in a near-field MIMO system, which increases due to the spherical wavefronts present in the near field.
An index introduced for model selection in nested models, providing a measure similar to effective sample size (ESS) to guide decisions on model complexity and variable selection.
Alternative metrics for assessing effective sample size in importance sampling algorithms, linked to Rényi and Tsallis entropy, enhancing the understanding of sample efficiency.
A measure of the efficiency of converting fundamental frequency light into its second harmonic, computable through optimization models with geometric constraints.
A method that utilizes the triangle inequality to optimize suffix comparisons in prescriptive process monitoring, significantly reducing computational costs while ensuring exact retrieval.
A novel framework for single-image super-resolution that optimizes visual quality towards human preferences by avoiding extensive patch sampling and enabling efficient image-level perception.

An end-to-end framework for automated academic poster generation that utilizes semantic-aware retrieval and token-efficient multimodal generation to enhance information density and layout verification.
A web-native neuro-symbolic framework for smart glasses that utilizes a Large Language Model (LLM) to provide assistive AI capabilities, enhancing user interaction with the web in real-time.
An end-to-end framework for accurate egocentric full-body pose estimation in virtual reality, integrating headset motion cues with RGB-D observations for improved tracking performance.
Outbound data transfer from a cloud service or network to an external destination. Egress fees are a significant cost factor in cloud computing and can influence architecture decisions.

A network security practice of monitoring and restricting outbound traffic from an internal network, preventing data exfiltration, blocking command-and-control communications, and stopping IP spoofing of external addresses.
A scalar associated with a linear transformation that indicates the factor by which a corresponding eigenvector is scaled. Eigenvalues are central to PCA, spectral clustering, and quantum computing.
A complete classification of the computational complexity of the planar eight-vertex model, identifying conditions under which the partition function is computable in polynomial time.

EIMC (Efficient Instance-aware Multi-modal Collaborative perception) introduces an early collaborative paradigm for multi-modal data fusion in autonomous driving, enhancing communication efficiency by integrating lightweight collaborative voxels into local modality-fusion steps.
Elastic Kubernetes Service — a managed Kubernetes service by AWS that simplifies running Kubernetes clusters without needing to install and operate the control plane.

A structured execution graph framework that integrates large language models with dynamic knowledge graphs to enhance decision-making in scientific workflows while maintaining context and auditability.
A static IPv4 address designed for dynamic cloud computing in AWS. Elastic IPs can be rapidly remapped to different instances, enabling quick failover and flexible architecture.
An AWS service that automatically distributes incoming application traffic across multiple targets such as EC2 instances, containers, and IP addresses across availability zones.
The ability of a cloud system to automatically scale resources up or down based on demand. Elasticity differs from scalability in that it implies automatic and dynamic resource adjustment.

A distributed, open-source search and analytics engine built on Apache Lucene. Elasticsearch enables fast full-text search, structured search, and analytics across large volumes of data, commonly used in logging, monitoring, and site search.
A JSON-based query language for Elasticsearch that provides a full range of search capabilities including full-text search, structured search, geo queries, and aggregations.
A simulation framework used to evaluate the benefits of malleable job scheduling in high-performance computing clusters, allowing dynamic resource allocation at runtime.

A framework that formalizes the heuristic elbow method for cluster selection as a rigorous inferential problem, using a normalized discrete curvature statistic to evaluate cluster heterogeneity against a null distribution.
Disturbance generated by an external source that affects an electrical circuit through electromagnetic induction, electrostatic coupling, or conduction. EMI can degrade electronic device performance.
A framework for building cross-platform desktop applications using web technologies like JavaScript, HTML, and CSS. Apps like VS Code, Slack, and Discord are built with Electron.

A fast, multiplication-free algorithm for dimension reduction that selects a subset of elements from input data, providing an efficient alternative to traditional methods like PCA, especially in resource-constrained environments.
A novel method for optimizing handover processes in direct-to-device satellite networks by dynamically adjusting the elevation angle threshold to mitigate co-channel interference.
A technology designed to enhance the reliability of direct satellite-to-device communications by integrating supplementary uplink mechanisms to mitigate challenges posed by low elevation angles.

Executable and Linkable Format — the standard binary file format for executables, object code, shared libraries, and core dumps on Linux and Unix systems, defining sections for code, data, symbols, and relocation entries.
A novel approach that utilizes elicitability to derive path-wise loss functions for training neural networks, enabling efficient approximation of stochastic processes without expensive simulations.
