A Generic Reconfigurable Activation Unit designed for neural network hardware accelerators, which supports mixed-precision quantization and nonlinear functions while minimizing hardware costs through piecewise linear fitting.
AWS custom ARM-based processors designed for cloud workloads. Graviton instances offer better price-performance compared to x86 instances for many cloud computing workloads.

A semi-synthetic dataset designed to evaluate Domain Generation Algorithm (DGA) detection methods specifically in the context of mobile device spearphishing, addressing the gap in existing DGA research.
A semi-synthetic dataset derived from smishing links used to evaluate domain-generation algorithm (DGA) detection methods, specifically targeting mobile device spearphishing.
A binary numeral system where two successive values differ in only one bit. Gray codes are used in error correction, rotary encoders, and minimizing switching errors in digital circuits.
Algorithms that enumerate binary words with constant delay and auxiliary space, independent of the length of the words, implemented in computational models such as tape machines and deque machines.
An open-source log management platform for collecting, indexing, and analyzing both structured and unstructured data from various sources. It provides real-time search and analysis capabilities.
A structured reflection-correction framework for generative recommendation systems that enhances decoding trajectories by incorporating a supervised reflection-correction template.
A manifold-aware framework for speech enhancement that enforces Global Rotation Equivariance to effectively model the circular topology of phase information.

An algorithmic paradigm that makes the locally optimal choice at each stage with the hope of finding a global optimum. Greedy algorithms are used for optimization problems like minimum spanning trees.
An algorithm that accelerates autoregressive decoding by using a verification process that accepts prefixes of candidate sequences based on probabilities from target and draft models.
An optimization problem that aims to minimize the number of bins used and total energy consumption when packing items into bins, factoring in energy costs based on item sizes.
A method for reconnecting fragmented habitats in a graph by optimally placing 'green bridges' at minimum cost, ensuring robust connectivity even with patch failures.

The practice of designing, manufacturing, using, and disposing of computing resources in an environmentally sustainable way. Green computing focuses on energy efficiency, reducing e-waste, and minimizing the carbon footprint of data centers and devices.

Software engineering practices aimed at reducing the carbon footprint and energy consumption of applications. The Green Software Foundation promotes patterns like carbon-aware computing and energy-efficient code.

A thread that is scheduled by a runtime library or virtual machine instead of the operating system kernel. Green threads enable lightweight concurrency but cannot take advantage of multiple CPU cores.

A software project built from scratch without constraints from prior work or legacy systems. Greenfield projects offer freedom in choosing technologies and architecture but lack existing infrastructure.

A project that is developed from scratch without the need to consider any existing code or infrastructure. Greenfield projects offer the freedom to choose optimal technologies but lack existing foundations.
A command-line utility for searching plain-text data for lines that match a regular expression. Grep is one of the most frequently used Unix tools for text processing and log analysis.
A distributed computing architecture that pools computing resources from multiple locations to reach a common goal. Grid computing is used for large-scale scientific computations and data processing.

The Grift compiler is a close-to-the-metal implementation of gradual typing that utilizes coercions for runtime checks and is extended with monotonic references to efficiently access statically-typed data structures.

GrIT (Group Informed Transformer) is a sequential recommendation framework that enhances user interest predictions by incorporating temporally evolving group features alongside individual user histories, addressing the limitations of existing transformer-based methods.
A dynamic, optionally typed programming language for the Java Virtual Machine that combines features of Python, Ruby, and Smalltalk. Groovy is used for scripting, testing, and building DSLs.
A structural reformulation of cryptographic security that uses Grothendieck topologies to model attacker observations, framing security properties as geometric conditions within sheaf theory.
The accurate, verified data used as a reference for evaluating machine learning model predictions. Ground truth data is essential for training supervised models and measuring model accuracy.

Grounded Laplacians are mathematical constructs used to analyze dynamics on directed signed matrix-weighted networks, ensuring global asymptotic convergence of system states under specific conditions.
An approach to emotional support where multiple AI agents collaborate to provide assistance, leveraging social support theories to enhance user experience.
An SQL clause that groups rows sharing common values into summary rows, often combined with aggregate functions like COUNT, SUM, and AVG. It is fundamental for data aggregation and reporting.
A formalization of spatial understanding in Vision Transformers, where image sequences are mapped to a latent space preserving the algebraic structure of transformation groups.
An alignment algorithm for large language models that optimizes policy in a Hilbert space, transforming the simplex constraint into a linear orthogonality condition to enhance model performance.
A feature of Microsoft Windows that provides centralized management and configuration of operating systems, applications, and user settings. Group policies are applied through Active Directory.
Group Relative Policy Optimization is a technique applied in diffusion transformer architectures for enhancing text-to-audio generation quality through reinforcement learning.
A novel reinforcement learning objective designed to enhance reasoning capabilities in multimodal large language models, particularly for tasks like hateful meme detection.

A MySQL plugin that enables creating elastic, highly available database clusters with built-in conflict detection and resolution. It provides fault tolerance through automatic group membership management.

GroupEnsemble is an efficient method for uncertainty estimation in DETR-based object detection, addressing the challenge of capturing spatial uncertainty while minimizing memory consumption compared to traditional Deep Ensembles.
A token-efficient and privacy-preserving framework for multi-user chat assistants that enhances scalability and reduces privacy risks in group chat environments.

A marketing strategy focused on rapid experimentation across channels and product development to identify the most effective ways to grow a business. It combines marketing, data analysis, and engineering.

A high-performance, open-source remote procedure call framework developed by Google. gRPC uses Protocol Buffers for serialization and HTTP/2 for transport, enabling efficient communication between microservices with strong typing and streaming support.

A feature of gRPC that allows sending and receiving multiple messages over a single connection. gRPC supports server streaming, client streaming, and bidirectional streaming for real-time communication.
A JavaScript client library that enables browser applications to communicate with gRPC services through an intermediary proxy. gRPC-Web brings the benefits of gRPC to web clients.
A set of software and hardware optimizations for efficient ray tracing of 3D Gaussian-based rendering, addressing inefficiencies in existing Gaussian ray tracing methods.

A JavaScript task runner that automates repetitive tasks like minification, compilation, unit testing, and linting. While largely superseded by newer tools like Webpack, Grunt established the concept of front-end build automation.
GSICO is a novel codec for Gaussian Splatting that efficiently compresses pre-trained models into structured images, significantly reducing file sizes while maintaining perceptual fidelity.

GS-SBL (Greedy Sequential Sparse Bayesian Learning) is a framework that combines greedy pursuit and sparse Bayesian learning to efficiently model 3D wireless channels by identifying a discrete set of virtual signal sources.

Global System for Mobile Communications — a standard for digital cellular networks used by mobile phones worldwide. GSM defines protocols for second-generation digital cellular networks and has evolved through 3G, 4G, and 5G.

A synergistic optimization framework for 3D Gaussian Splatting that integrates single-view adaptive sub-region weighting constraints and multi-view spatial structure to enhance surface reconstruction accuracy.

A benchmark designed to evaluate AI safety risks in multi-agent environments, featuring high-stakes scenarios derived from game-theoretic structures to assess coordination failures and conflicts among agents.

GTATrack is a hierarchical tracking framework that integrates Deep Expansion IoU for motion-agnostic online association and Global Tracklet Association for trajectory-level refinement, specifically designed for multi-object tracking in sports.

GTDoctor is an expert model designed for the intelligent pathological diagnosis of gestational trophoblastic diseases, capable of performing pixel-based lesion segmentation and providing personalized diagnostic analysis.

A conditional statement at the beginning of a function that checks for invalid inputs and returns early, reducing nesting and improving readability. Guard clauses simplify control flow and make code easier to understand.

A programming pattern where a function returns early if certain preconditions are not met, avoiding deep nesting. Guard patterns improve code readability by handling edge cases at the beginning of functions.
GuardAlign is a training-free defense framework for large vision-language models that integrates optimal transport for safety detection and enhances the robustness of safety signals during decoding.

Graphical User Interface — a visual way of interacting with a computer using icons, buttons, windows, and other graphical elements rather than text commands. GUIs make software accessible to non-technical users.

GGO is a method for optimizing a bidirected weighted graph used in Lifelong Multi-Agent Path Finding (LMAPF), where directed edges represent actions and their weights indicate action costs, enhancing agent movement efficiency.
A method in pattern mining that enhances the discovery of sequential rules by focusing on user-relevant results, thereby reducing computational overhead and improving the relevance of mined patterns.
A technique for nonlinear state-space identification that improves parameter estimation by decomposing the optimization problem into manageable subproblems, enhancing reliability and efficiency.

A search interface paradigm that helps users refine their queries through facets, suggestions, and filters. Guided search reduces zero-result pages and helps users find relevant content more efficiently.
An innovative communication approach that combines guided waves and wireless signals to reduce interference and enhance data rates over longer distances.
A method for covering a set of points in a plane using straight cuts that can only be made vertically or horizontally, which has been shown to be NP-complete.

A streaming build system and task runner for JavaScript that automates time-consuming tasks in the development workflow. Gulp uses Node.js streams for fast builds without writing intermediate files to disk.

A Node.js stream implementation used by Gulp to pipe file contents through transformations without writing intermediate files to disk. Streams make Gulp builds faster than task runners that use temporary files.
Green Unicorn — a Python WSGI HTTP server for Unix that serves Python web applications. Gunicorn is commonly used to deploy Django and Flask applications behind a reverse proxy like Nginx.
The block-based editor introduced in WordPress 5.0 that replaces the classic TinyMCE editor. Gutenberg uses a modular block approach for content creation and full-site editing.
Generative Visual Chain-of-Thought, a framework that combines visual reasoning with image editing by generating spatial cues for target localization and executing edits in an end-to-end manner.
A file compression program and format based on the DEFLATE algorithm that reduces file sizes for storage and transmission. Gzip is widely used for compressing web content and software distributions.

H.definition: The Hardware-Aware Stochastic Simulated Annealing (HA-SSA) algorithm optimizes memory usage for FPGA implementations of simulated annealing, maintaining computational speed while reducing memory overhead.
A social news website focused on computer science and entrepreneurship run by Y Combinator. Hacker News serves as a prominent community for discussing technology, startups, and programming.

An open-source framework for distributed storage and processing of large datasets across clusters of computers. Hadoop's HDFS file system and MapReduce processing model pioneered big data processing, though newer tools like Spark have largely superseded it.

The primary distributed storage system used by Hadoop applications that provides high-throughput access to data across clusters. HDFS splits files into blocks and replicates them across multiple nodes for fault tolerance.

H.term: HADUA (Hierarchical Attention and Dynamic Uniform Alignment)
H.definition: An adaptive learning framework for robust cross-subject emotion recognition that models intra-modal temporal dynamics and inter-modal semantic interactions.
Hypertext Application Language — a standard format for representing API resources as JSON or XML with hypermedia links. HAL provides a simple and consistent way to hyperlink between API resources.

A modification of the 1 Euro Filter designed for real-time animation blending, which includes algorithms for automatic data-driven tuning and filter triggering based on motion derivative boundary checks.

A 16-bit floating-point number format (FP16) that uses less memory and computation than standard 32-bit floats. Half-precision training accelerates deep learning while maintaining acceptable model accuracy.
A phenomenon where AI language models generate plausible-sounding but factually incorrect or fabricated information. Addressing hallucinations is a major challenge in deploying reliable AI systems.

A fine-grained resource sharing quantum operating system that addresses the challenges of multiplexing quantum processors among multiple users, aiming to enhance resource utilization and reduce job wait times.

An innovative architecture for large language models that utilizes rational arithmetic to improve numerical stability and inference accuracy, moving away from traditional fuzzy floating-point methods.
A novel architecture that represents relations like part-whole intrinsically within its components, utilizing a graph-Hamiltonian operator to calculate energies among encoded data.
A path in a graph that visits each vertex exactly once. The Hamiltonian path problem is NP-complete and has applications in logistics, circuit design, and genome sequencing.

A framework that utilizes multimodal large language models for intention-driven 3D affordance grounding, aggregating interaction intentions into contact-aware embeddings for improved object interaction understanding.
The number of positions at which corresponding symbols in two strings of equal length are different. Hamming distance is used in error detection, error correction, and information theory.

A metric introduced to quantify the proximity of learners' actions to valid grammatical constructions during interactive gameplay, providing insights into cognitive strategies in language acquisition.

A one-player game involving colored blocks and flowers, where the objective is to make blocks contact with flowers of the corresponding color. The solvability of its levels is PSPACE-complete under certain restrictions.
An abstract reference to a resource such as a file, socket, or window managed by the operating system. Handles provide an indirection layer that allows the OS to manage resource lifecycle.
A process of negotiation between two communicating parties to establish parameters of a connection before data transfer begins. The TCP three-way handshake and TLS handshake are common examples.
The default scenario in a use case where everything proceeds without errors or exceptions. Testing the happy path verifies that the system works correctly under ideal conditions.

A free, open-source software that provides high availability load balancing and proxying for TCP and HTTP-based applications. HAProxy is widely used as a reverse proxy and load balancer in production environments.
Technology that creates tactile sensations through vibrations, forces, or motions applied to the user. Haptic feedback enhances user interaction with touchscreens, game controllers, and VR devices.
Innovative use of weight and pressure as haptic feedback mechanisms in virtual reality user interfaces, enhancing user experience by conveying information through tactile sensations.

The practice of embedding fixed values directly into source code rather than obtaining them from configuration files, databases, or user input. Hard coding reduces flexibility and is generally considered a bad practice.

The process of securing a system by reducing its attack surface through disabling unnecessary services, applying patches, and configuring security settings. System hardening is a fundamental security practice.

The physical components of a computer system, including processors, memory, storage devices, input/output devices, and networking equipment. Hardware provides the foundation on which software operates.

A layer of programming that allows an operating system to interact with hardware devices at a general level rather than at a detailed hardware level. HAL provides portability across different hardware platforms.

The protection of physical computing devices and their components from tampering, theft, and unauthorized access. Hardware security includes secure boot, trusted platform modules, and physical tamper resistance.

A physical computing device that safeguards and manages digital keys, performs encryption and decryption functions, and provides strong authentication. HSMs are used in banking, PKI, and critical infrastructure.

A technique for generating uniformly random polynomials in Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) schemes, aimed at reducing communication overhead by deriving random values from a small seed on the client side.

Harmonia is an algorithm-hardware co-design framework that enables the use of block floating point (BFP) activations across all layers of large language models, enhancing memory and compute efficiency.

A deep learning architecture designed to compute the Harmonic Beltrami Signature from binary-like images, providing a shape representation invariant to transformations such as translation and rotation.
In signal processing, frequencies that are integer multiples of a fundamental frequency. Understanding harmonics is essential in audio engineering, telecommunications, and power system analysis.
A systematic approach for synthesizing topology reconfiguration schedules in chip-to-chip silicon photonics to minimize collective completion time during communication.
A situation where two different inputs produce the same output from a hash function. Handling collisions efficiently is critical for hash table performance and cryptographic security.

A mathematical function that converts an input of arbitrary size into a fixed-size output. Cryptographic hash functions like SHA-256 are used for data integrity verification, password storage, and digital signatures.

A data structure that implements an associative array by using a hash function to compute an index into an array of buckets from which the desired value can be found. Hash maps provide average constant-time lookups.
A data structure used in consistent hashing that maps both data and nodes onto a circular ring. Hash rings enable distributed systems to add or remove nodes with minimal data redistribution.
A data structure that maps keys to values using a hash function for fast data retrieval. Hash tables provide O(1) average-case time complexity for insertions, deletions, and lookups.

A software company that provides tools for cloud infrastructure automation, including Terraform for infrastructure as code, Vault for secrets management, Consul for service mesh, and Nomad for orchestration.
A tool for securely storing and accessing secrets such as API keys, passwords, and certificates. Vault provides a unified interface for secret management with tight access control and audit logging.

The process of converting data of any size into a fixed-length string of characters using a mathematical function. Hash functions are one-way operations used for password storage, data integrity verification, and digital signatures.

A purely functional programming language known for its strong static type system, type inference, and lazy evaluation. Haskell emphasizes correctness and is widely used in academia and financial systems.

HateMirage is a novel dataset designed to decode faux hate and subtle online abuse, focusing on the nuanced ways misinformation can incite or normalize hate, thus advancing reasoning and explainability in online safety research.

Hypermedia as the Engine of Application State — a constraint of REST architecture where clients interact with a server entirely through hypermedia provided dynamically by the server. It enables self-discoverable APIs.
A high-bandwidth flash-augmented vector engine aimed at accelerating large-scale Approximate Nearest Neighbor Search (ANNS) for retrieval-augmented generation tasks.
A storage management system that integrates confidential computing principles with disaggregated storage to enhance performance and security in modern data centers.
An open-source, non-relational distributed database modeled after Google's Bigtable and written in Java. HBase runs on top of HDFS and provides real-time read/write access to large datasets.

A method for generating high compatibility virtual reality environments that enhance redirected walking techniques, addressing challenges posed by geometric divergence between physical and virtual spaces.
HDINO is an efficient open-vocabulary object detector that employs a two-stage training strategy to reduce reliance on curated datasets and enhance detection capabilities using noisy samples.

High-Definition Multimedia Interface — a proprietary audio/video interface for transmitting uncompressed digital data from a source device to a display. HDMI supports high-resolution video and multi-channel audio.

A performance-limiting phenomenon that occurs when the first packet in a queue cannot be processed, blocking all subsequent packets. HTTP/2 and QUIC address head-of-line blocking at different protocol layers.

An approach where the front-end presentation layer is decoupled from the back-end content management system. Headless CMS solutions deliver content via APIs, allowing any front-end technology to consume it.
A web browser without a graphical user interface that can be controlled programmatically. Headless browsers are used for automated testing, web scraping, and server-side rendering.

A content management system that provides content through APIs without a built-in frontend presentation layer. Headless CMS separates content creation from display, allowing developers to deliver content to any platform — web, mobile, IoT, or AR.

An endpoint or mechanism in a service that reports whether the service is functioning correctly. Health checks are used by load balancers and orchestrators to route traffic and manage service instances.

A composite metric that indicates the overall well-being of a customer account based on usage patterns, support tickets, and engagement. Health scores help predict churn and guide customer success efforts.

A user-centric health data management system that empowers individuals to control, upload, query, and share their health data across various modalities, addressing fragmentation in healthcare ecosystems.

A specialized tree-based data structure that satisfies the heap property, where parent nodes are ordered relative to their children. Heaps are used to implement priority queues and for efficient sorting.
A snapshot of the memory of a Java or similar application at a certain point in time, showing all objects and their references. Heap dumps are used to diagnose memory leaks and performance issues.

A region of memory used for dynamic memory allocation where blocks of memory can be allocated and freed in any order. Unlike stack memory, heap memory requires explicit management in languages without garbage collection.
A periodic signal sent between systems to indicate normal operation and detect failures. Heartbeat mechanisms are used in distributed systems, clusters, and high-availability configurations.
The process of transferring thermal energy away from electronic components to maintain safe operating temperatures. Effective heat dissipation is critical for CPU, GPU, and data center design.
A data visualization technique that uses color gradients to represent the magnitude of values in a matrix or geographic area. Heat maps are used in web analytics, genomics, and spatial data analysis.
A statistical finding that correlates high temperatures with an increase in school shootings, suggesting a link between heat exposure and aggressive behavior.
A heatmap-guided decoding framework for large-scale Travelling Salesman Problems that treats heatmaps as soft edge priors, enabling probabilistic tour construction under feasibility constraints.

Software that visualizes user behavior on web pages by tracking and displaying where users click, scroll, and move their mouse. Tools like Hotjar and Crazy Egg help optimize page layouts and user flows.
A simulation framework designed to analyze the impact of urban materials on the Urban Heat Island effect, providing high-resolution city-scale simulations to study temperature variations.
A classification framework that utilizes Heaviside loss and low-rank constraints to robustly handle matrix-structured observations, improving noise sensitivity compared to traditional methods.

H.term: Height Profile of Analog Error-Correcting Codes
H.definition: A concept that models the reliability of analog vector-matrix multipliers as a coding problem, determining the trade-off between error tolerance and the number of detectable outlying errors based on the height profile of the code.

This model incorporates vertical variations in signal behavior for spectrum sharing in aerial communications, validated through extensive measurements across various environments using a helikite-mounted software-defined radio.
A software bug that seems to disappear or alter its behavior when one attempts to study or debug it. The name references the Heisenberg uncertainty principle from quantum mechanics.
Architected soft materials designed for soft continuum robots, providing tunable stiffness and strength while enabling the embedding of sensors for enhanced control and sensing capabilities.
A framework that harmonizes early and late fusion techniques along with large language model reasoning for improved multi-granular table-text retrieval in open-domain question answering.

An end-to-end audio language model designed for realistic social interactions, leveraging a massive dataset of real-life conversations and a modality-interleaved training strategy to enhance emotional resonance in speech.

A package manager for Kubernetes that simplifies deploying and managing applications on Kubernetes clusters. Helm uses charts — pre-configured packages of Kubernetes resources — to define and install applications.
A 72-parameter analytical color space designed for UI systems that maps CIE XYZ to a perceptually-organized Lab representation, enhancing color distance metrics for design applications.

A centralized resource for providing technical support and troubleshooting assistance to users. Help desk systems track issues through ticketing, manage knowledge bases, and measure support performance.
A utility function that performs a common task used by other functions in a program. Helper functions promote code reuse and keep the main logic clean by abstracting repetitive operations.
A foundational logic for labelled transition systems that provides a framework for reasoning about system behaviors and equivalences in formal verification.

A formalization of Hennessy-Milner Logic for labelled transition systems, providing a syntax, satisfaction relation, and metatheory that emphasizes generality and reusability within the Lean Computer Science Library.
A Meta-RL approach that leverages task symmetries to enhance generalization by transforming states and actions through actions of a Lie group, enabling richer task space exploration.
A unified high-performance architecture for Number Theoretic Transform (NTT) that supports Hybrid Homomorphic Encryption (HHE) by exploiting both temporal and spatial parallelism.
A build process that is isolated from external dependencies and the host environment, producing identical output regardless of when or where it is executed. Hermetic builds improve reproducibility.

HeRo is a heterogeneous-aware framework for adaptive orchestration of retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) on mobile System-on-Chips (SoCs), optimizing low-latency execution across diverse hardware accelerators.

A cloud platform as a service that supports several programming languages and enables developers to build, run, and operate applications entirely in the cloud. Heroku abstracts away infrastructure management.
A hyperthread-level scheduling technique that addresses performance interference in microservices by modeling SMT contention dynamics for optimal resource allocation.
An AI/ML-based beam selection algorithm designed to function effectively across heterogeneous antenna hardware configurations, minimizing beam training overhead while maintaining performance.

A methodology for selecting clients in federated learning that accounts for statistical heterogeneity across clients, aiming to improve the accuracy of the global machine learning model without compromising local data privacy.
A learning paradigm that facilitates collaborative optimization among heterogeneous agents by sharing verified rollouts during training, allowing independent execution during inference.

A retraining-free framework designed for Sparse Mixture-of-Experts models that utilizes analog in-memory computing to improve memory and energy efficiency during inference without requiring noise-aware retraining.
A computing approach that uses more than one kind of processor or core to gain performance and energy efficiency. Modern systems combine CPUs, GPUs, and specialized accelerators.
Data that comes in multiple formats, structures, or types from diverse sources. Handling heterogeneous data is a key challenge in data integration, requiring schema alignment and transformation.

A model for recovering hyperedges from projected graphs, focusing on the reconstruction of original hyperedges based on observed projections. The approach utilizes maximal cliques to achieve recovery under specific conditions.
A deep learning-based antenna coding optimization algorithm that trains multiple neural networks on various coding schemes and selects the optimal one for enhanced wireless communication performance.
An algorithmic strategy that routes simpler queries to lower-cost models and complex queries to higher-cost models, enhancing the cost-efficiency of large language model deployments.
This model describes a random hypergraph structure where hyperedges can vary in degree, facilitating the recovery of hyperedges from observed projections of the graph.

A problem-solving approach that uses practical methods or rules of thumb to find good-enough solutions quickly when finding the optimal answer is impractical. Heuristics trade optimality for speed and are widely used in AI, search algorithms, and UX design.
A usability inspection method where evaluators examine a user interface against a set of recognized usability principles. It is a fast and cost-effective way to identify usability issues in a design.
A framework developed under the Hexa-X-II project aimed at addressing security, privacy, and resilience in the sixth generation (6G) mobile networks, focusing on end-to-end system controls.
A base-16 number system that uses digits 0-9 and letters A-F to represent values. Hexadecimal notation is widely used in computing for memory addresses, color codes, and binary data representation.
An open-source compilation stack targeting Qualcomm's Neural Processing Units (NPUs), built on the MLIR framework, which optimizes AI workloads through structured compilation passes.
An architectural pattern that isolates the core business logic from external concerns like databases, UIs, and frameworks through ports and adapters. Also known as ports and adapters architecture.
A dataset comprising subject-rated 1080p videos encoded at various bitrates to study the perceptual effects of frame rate and compression strength in bitrate-constrained live streaming.

A modular, reconfigurable neuromorphic computing platform designed for executing large spiking neural networks with high efficiency, utilizing hierarchical address-event routing for optimized spike processing.
A simultaneous speech translation model that eliminates the need for word-level alignments, simplifying the training process and enabling scalability across diverse languages.
Human Interface Device — a type of computer device that takes input from or provides output to humans. Examples include keyboards, mice, game controllers, and touchscreens.
A layer of neurons in an artificial neural network between the input and output layers. Hidden layers enable the network to learn complex nonlinear representations of data.
A statistical model where the system being modeled is assumed to follow a Markov process with hidden states. HMMs are used in speech recognition, bioinformatics, and natural language processing.
A hierarchical collaboration framework for multi-agent systems that optimizes intra-node LLM mixtures and inter-node communication topology to enhance agent performance.

An approach to classification that leverages hierarchical structures in data, particularly for tasks like vehicle make and model classification, allowing models to exploit semantic relationships through multi-task learning.
A decoding framework that assesses EEG signals to capture object representations across hierarchical abstraction levels, addressing challenges in recognizing fine-grained representations in EEG data.
A method for urban geospatial modification that utilizes agentic systems to iteratively adjust existing urban plans based on dependency awareness, facilitating efficient urban renewal.
A method of cluster analysis that builds a hierarchy of clusters by either successively merging or dividing groups. The result is visualized as a dendrogram showing relationships between data points.

H.term: Hierarchical Diffusion Models for Micro-Video Recommendation
H.definition: A modeling approach that utilizes hierarchical diffusion techniques to capture user preferences in micro-video recommendation systems, addressing challenges posed by noise and implicit feedback.
A framework for characterizing defect sensitivity in Binary Search ADCs, particularly in thin-film technologies, to improve fault tolerance in flexible electronics.
A methodological approach that models decision-making in repeated public goods games as discrete switches, addressing the volatility in contributions through behavioral trajectory clustering.

A novel architecture for Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning that employs a sequential training scheme to learn from multiple perspectives across different hierarchy levels, integrating high-level objectives with low-level execution.

A multi-agent framework that utilizes large language models to decompose natural-language instructions into executable actions for heterogeneous robot teams, featuring prompt optimization for improved task planning.

HRA is a multimodal universal attack framework for vision-language models that optimizes the perturbation learning process by leveraging a temporal hierarchy of gradients, reducing computational overhead in adversarial attacks.

A differentiable architecture that selects spectral coefficients from a Boolean Fourier basis and composes them using Sinkhorn-constrained routing, aimed at improving the learning of precise Boolean logic.
This approach utilizes multiple pre-trained visual encoders to decode visual representations from brain signals, enhancing the understanding of human visual processing.
An evolutionary search approach that utilizes biological knowledge to optimize whole-brain models, improving predictive power and reducing overfitting in biophysical brain modeling.
A hierarchical layer attention framework for audio deepfake detection that utilizes margin-based contrastive learning to model dependencies across temporal frames and layers.

A hierarchical reinforcement learning framework for soccer robot control that integrates dual-frequency policies for long-term tactical planning and short-term motion execution, utilizing real-time vision detection.
A reference-based inpainting method aimed at generating high-fidelity human-product images, addressing challenges in detail preservation and training data diversity.
A method for generating high-fidelity 3D meshes using compact autoregressive dependencies, significantly improving the maximum generatable sequence length for detailed structural representation.
A block floating-point data format designed for deep learning that packs 64 4-bit elements with shared scaling metadata, enhancing representational efficiency and reducing hardware requirements.
Non-terrestrial networks that provide broad coverage and serve as a vital link between satellite systems and terrestrial networks, with applications in sensing, computing, and communication.

A system design approach that ensures a specified level of operational performance and uptime, typically 99.9% or higher. HA architectures use redundancy, load balancing, and failover mechanisms to minimize downtime and service disruption.
A characteristic of a data column that has a very large number of distinct values relative to the number of rows. High cardinality can impact indexing strategy, query performance, and storage.
A design principle where the elements within a module are closely related and focused on a single purpose. High cohesion leads to more maintainable, understandable, and reusable code.
The use of supercomputers and parallel processing techniques to solve complex computational problems. HPC is used in scientific research, weather forecasting, and molecular modeling.
A methodological approach that uncovers complex networks of influence among entities directly from data, facilitating the analysis of spillover effects in corporate emissions.

H.term: High-Order Markov Chain Task for Transformers
H.definition: A task designed to study how transformers learn to integrate information from multiple past positions, revealing dynamics of competitive and cooperative attention patterns.

A set of bounds for universal prediction of stochastic sequences that guarantees vanishing regret with high probability, enhancing the understanding of prediction performance over finite time horizons.
A type that takes other types as parameters and itself takes type parameters. Higher kinded types enable powerful abstractions in languages like Haskell and Scala for generic programming.

This framework extends the bialgebraic abstract GSOS approach to higher-order languages, preserving compositionality of bisimilarity and offering a structured method for modeling operational and denotational semantics.

Higher-Order Circuit Theories define laws for strict higher-order circuit diagrams, incorporating concepts such as nesting, temporal and spatial composition, and equivalence between lower-order bipartite processes and higher-order bipartite states.

A function that takes one or more functions as arguments or returns a function as its result. Higher-order functions are fundamental to functional programming and enable powerful abstractions like map, filter, and reduce.
HOMs refer to the underlying dynamical rules that govern collective behaviors in complex systems characterized by higher-order interactions, identified through signatures of higher-order behaviors.

A weighted graph representation of the Hilbert space of a Heisenberg antiferromagnet, where vertices correspond to spin configurations and edges represent off-diagonal spin-flip terms of the Heisenberg Hamiltonian, weighted by wavefunction amplitudes.
An approach that integrates Hilbert space-filling priors into decentralized multi-robot learning, enhancing exploration efficiency and coverage in sparse-reward environments.

The problem of determining the solvability of multivariate polynomial equations is shown to lie within the counting hierarchy, allowing for polynomial-time computation of the number of solutions with oracle access.
A method for efficient long-context inference of Large Language Models (LLMs) on edge devices, utilizing hierarchical key-value eviction strategies to manage memory constraints effectively.

HiLoRA is a hierarchical low-rank adaptation framework for personalized federated learning, which introduces adapters at multiple levels to enhance shared representation learning and improve adaptation to unseen clients.
A technique in the VeriFast verification tool that allows for pragmatic certification of verification results by mirroring user-annotated properties to enhance correctness assurance.
A conjecture that generalizes the original matrix case to tensors, proposing new insights into the complexity of matrix and tensor operations.

A CLEF evaluation lab focused on person-place relation extraction from multilingual historical texts, targeting the classification of relations such as 'at' and 'isAt' based on temporal and geographical cues.

Hippasus is an automatic feature augmentation technique that enhances machine learning tasks on relational data by discovering and integrating useful features from related tables through efficient join operations.

A high-performance solver for trajectory optimization that employs an interior-point method with an adaptive barrier update strategy, capable of handling both inequality and hard equality constraints efficiently.
A ray tracing framework that utilizes the hardware ray tracing capabilities of AMD GPUs to accelerate rendering computations, enhancing performance in graphics applications.
A framework for automated C-to-Rust migration that combines a build-aware skeleton with historical retrieval to address dependency issues and improve translation accuracy.

Hierarchical Sparse Activation Compression is a framework designed for ultra-long sequence modeling in recommender systems, optimizing user behavior summarization and reducing latency and memory usage.

A graphical representation of the distribution of numerical data that groups values into bins and displays their frequencies. Histograms are fundamental for understanding data distributions in statistical analysis.

An image processing technique that adjusts the contrast of an image by modifying its intensity distribution. Histogram equalization spreads out the most frequent intensity values to improve visual quality.

A dynamic algorithm that supports insertions and deletions in a balls-and-bins model while maintaining a maximum load of m/n + O(1) with high probability, achieving efficient recourse independent of the history of operations.
The ratio of successful cache lookups to total lookups, indicating cache effectiveness. A high hit rate means the cache is serving most requests without accessing the slower backing store.
HTTP Live Streaming — an adaptive bitrate streaming protocol developed by Apple for delivering audio and video content over HTTP. HLS dynamically adjusts quality based on network conditions.
A newly recorded dataset for cardiorespiratory sounds, utilized for separation, clustering, and anomaly detection in AI-driven signal processing.
Hash-based Message Authentication Code — a specific type of message authentication code that uses a cryptographic hash function combined with a secret key to verify data integrity and authenticity.
Hierarchical Multi-Agent System for Payments, a framework that automates end-to-end payment workflows using large language models, addressing challenges in agentic payment tasks.
Hot Module Replacement — a development feature that updates modules in a running application without a full page reload. HMR speeds up development by preserving application state during code changes.

A JavaScript behavior where variable and function declarations are moved to the top of their containing scope during compilation. Understanding hoisting is essential for avoiding common JavaScript pitfalls.

A three-dimensional image created using laser light, photographic techniques, or computational methods. Holographic displays project images that can be viewed from different angles without special glasses.

A data storage technology that uses laser beams to store data in three dimensions within a photosensitive medium. Holographic storage has the potential for extremely high data density and fast read speeds.

Holographic-Pattern Division Multiple Access (HDMA) is a technique for multi-user communication that utilizes holographic surfaces to manage beamforming and user access, optimizing data transmission in virtual reality applications.

HomeAdam is a variant of the Adam and AdamW optimizers that aims to improve generalization by leveraging algorithmic stability, addressing the limitations of traditional adaptive algorithms in deep learning.
An optimization algorithm that enhances the generalization of Adam and AdamW by incorporating algorithmic stability, aiming to improve convergence rates in deep learning.
A free and open-source package manager for macOS and Linux that simplifies the installation of software. Homebrew installs packages to their own directory and symlinks them into standard locations.

HoMMI (Whole-Body Mobile Manipulation Interface) is a data collection and policy learning framework that captures human demonstrations using egocentric sensing, enabling scalable mobile manipulation learning while addressing the human-to-robot embodiment gap.

A form of encryption that allows computations to be performed on encrypted data without decrypting it first. The result of the computation, when decrypted, matches the result of operations on the plaintext.
An algorithmic challenge that involves reconstructing a graph based on homomorphism counts from given graphs, with complexity varying based on the representation of counts.
A security mechanism that creates a decoy system or resource designed to attract and detect unauthorized access attempts. Honeypots help security teams study attack techniques and improve defenses.

A mechanism that allows code to intercept function calls, messages, or events at specific points in execution. In React, hooks are functions that let developers use state and lifecycle features in functional components.

The hooking period analysis framework focuses on evaluating the initial three seconds of video ads, leveraging a multimodal approach to understand the interplay of visual, auditory, and textual elements that capture viewer attention.

HookLens is an interactive visual analytics system that assists developers in understanding the dependencies and data flows defined by React Hooks, enhancing maintainability and reducing complexity in React applications.
A React feature introduced in version 16.8 that lets developers use state and other React features in functional components. Common hooks include useState, useEffect, useContext, and custom hooks.

A Kubernetes resource that automatically scales the number of pods in a deployment based on observed CPU utilization or other custom metrics. HPA ensures applications handle varying workloads efficiently.

The process of adding more machines or nodes to a system to handle increased load, as opposed to vertical scaling which adds resources to existing machines. Horizontal scaling provides better fault tolerance.
A database scaling technique that distributes rows of a table across multiple database instances based on a shard key. Horizontal sharding enables handling datasets too large for a single server.
A container for DNS records that defines how traffic is routed for a domain and its subdomains. In AWS Route 53, a hosted zone stores the routing information for a registered domain.

A development feature that updates modules in a running application without requiring a full page reload. HMR significantly speeds up the development workflow by preserving application state during code changes.

A development feature that applies code changes to a running application instantly without requiring a full restart. Hot reload preserves application state, dramatically speeding up the development feedback loop in frameworks like React, Flutter, and Vite.
A section of code or a node in a system that receives a disproportionately high amount of traffic or computation. Identifying hot spots is essential for performance optimization.
A redundancy approach where the backup system runs simultaneously with the primary system and takes over immediately upon failure. Hot standby minimizes downtime compared to cold or warm standby.
A benchmark designed to evaluate agentic search systems by balancing quality and efficiency, focusing on real-world user queries with underspecified preferences.

An urgent software update applied to fix a critical bug or security vulnerability in a production system. Hotfixes bypass the normal release cycle to address issues that cannot wait for the next scheduled release.
A telemetry system that monitors memory access patterns at runtime to optimize data placement and migration in memory tiering solutions, enhancing performance without interfering with workloads.
High-order wavefront sensing and control algorithms designed for maintaining wavefront stability in astronomical observatories, addressing computational demands with advanced hardware solutions.

Hardware Security Module — a physical computing device that safeguards and manages digital keys, performs encryption and decryption, and provides strong authentication. HSMs are used to protect cryptographic keys in enterprise environments.

HTTP Strict Transport Security — a web security policy mechanism that forces browsers to interact with websites only over HTTPS connections. HSTS prevents protocol downgrade attacks and cookie hijacking.

The Hierarchical Sequential Transduction Unit, a generative recommender architecture that captures the temporal hierarchy of user interactions, improving the modeling of user behavior in recommendation systems.

HyperText Markup Language — the standard markup language for creating web pages and web applications. HTML provides the structural foundation of web content using elements like headings, paragraphs, links, images, and semantic tags.
The practice of using HTML elements according to their intended meaning rather than their visual appearance. Semantic HTML improves accessibility, SEO, and code maintainability.
The fifth major revision of HTML that introduces new elements, attributes, and APIs for building modern web applications. HTML5 includes native support for audio, video, canvas, and local storage.

A JavaScript library that allows access to AJAX, CSS transitions, WebSockets, and server-sent events directly in HTML using attributes. HTMX enables building modern user interfaces with minimal JavaScript.

HyperText Transfer Protocol — the foundation of data communication on the World Wide Web. HTTP defines how messages are formatted and transmitted, with methods like GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE for interacting with web resources.
A mechanism for storing copies of web resources to reduce server load and improve response times. HTTP caching uses headers like Cache-Control, ETag, and Last-Modified to manage cached content.
A small piece of data stored on the user's computer by a web browser while browsing a website. Cookies are used for session management, personalization, and tracking user behavior across pages.
A component of an HTTP request or response that passes additional information between the client and server. Headers carry metadata about the request, response, or the data being transferred.

Metadata fields sent with HTTP requests and responses that provide information about the data being transferred. Headers control caching, authentication, content types, security policies, and connection management.
The second major version of HTTP that introduces multiplexing, header compression, and server push to improve web performance. HTTP/2 allows multiple requests and responses over a single connection.
The third major version of HTTP built on the QUIC transport protocol instead of TCP. HTTP/3 eliminates head-of-line blocking at the transport layer and provides faster connection establishment.
HTTP Secure — an extension of HTTP that uses TLS encryption to secure communication between a web browser and a server. HTTPS protects data integrity and confidentiality during transmission.
A network topology where all traffic passes through a central hub. This architecture simplifies management but creates a single point of failure and potential bottleneck at the hub.

An open-source security scanner designed to detect hubness poisoning in Retrieval-Augmented Generation systems, which can exploit frequent retrieval items to introduce harmful content and degrade system performance.
The huff Python package provides a comprehensive workflow for market area analysis, including data import, interaction matrix construction, parameter estimation, and visualization of market dynamics.

A lossless data compression algorithm that assigns variable-length codes to characters based on their frequency of occurrence. More frequent characters receive shorter codes, reducing overall data size.

A company and open-source platform that provides tools, models, and datasets for natural language processing and machine learning. The Hugging Face Hub hosts thousands of pre-trained models and datasets.
A fast open-source static site generator written in Go that builds websites from content files and templates. Hugo is known for its build speed and is used for blogs, documentation, and portfolios.

The process of having humans assess the quality, relevance, or correctness of AI system outputs. Human evaluation remains the gold standard for judging natural language generation and creative AI tasks.
A study assessing the reasoning capabilities of large language models on digital logic circuit problems, evaluating their effectiveness as educational tools for undergraduate students.

A probabilistic model that assigns a likelihood to each unit being human in a population of interacting humans and AI agents, allowing for the estimation of average human composition in subpopulations.
HBI refers to the interaction between humans and architectural spaces, enhanced by advancements in robotics and sensing technologies that allow for adaptive and responsive environments.
An approach to human-AI collaboration that balances AI complementarity and trust-building, aiming to enhance decision-making without eroding human trust.
An approach that integrates physiological data (like eye-tracking and EEG) with content analysis to enhance the detection of nuanced social phenomena, such as sexism in memes.

Human-Certified Module Repositories (HCMRs) are architectural models aimed at constructing trustworthy software by ensuring the reliability of AI-generated components through rigorous provenance and review processes.
The study and design of the interaction between people and computers. HCI encompasses usability, user experience, accessibility, and the design of interfaces that are effective and satisfying to use.
A machine learning approach that incorporates human feedback into the training or decision-making process. Human-in-the-loop systems combine the strengths of human judgment with machine efficiency.
A human-inspired framework that models phonetic perception through adaptive inference, achieving state-of-the-art results on phonetic benchmarks with minimal training data.

HVP-Net is a framework for video-text retrieval that improves matching accuracy by extracting and refining features from multiple intermediate layers of a vision encoder, rather than relying solely on final-layer features.

A trading strategy that integrates technical analysis, machine learning, and financial sentiment to adapt to market regimes, utilizing methods like trend-following, mean-reversion, and sentiment analysis.
A mobile application built using web technologies like HTML, CSS, and JavaScript but wrapped in a native container. Hybrid apps can access device features and are distributed through app stores.

H.term: Hybrid Approach for Data Breach Reporting
H.definition: A proposed methodology to streamline data breach notifications under GDPR by automating the translation of forensic artifacts into structured reports, reducing cognitive burden on analysts.

H.term: Hybrid Architecture for Options Wheel Strategy
H.definition: A model-first hybrid architecture that integrates Large Language Models (LLMs) with Bayesian Networks to enhance decision-making in financial trading, particularly for the options wheel strategy.
An advanced deep learning architecture designed for edge-preserving denoising in cone-beam computed tomography, effectively reducing noise while maintaining fine anatomical details.

A computing environment that combines public cloud services with private cloud or on-premises infrastructure. Hybrid cloud allows organizations to place workloads in the optimal location based on requirements for performance, security, compliance, and cost.

H.term: Hybrid Consensus with Quantum Sybil Resistance
H.definition: This consensus protocol integrates classical and quantum techniques to achieve Sybil resistance by leveraging the uncloneability of quantum states as a scarce resource, enhancing the security of decentralized systems.
A learning-based approach for matching systems that adaptively combines immediate and delayed matching strategies to improve market efficiency and handle user departure dynamics.

This framework automates the extraction of personal names from broadcast news videos, leveraging a curated corpus of annotated frames to address variability in graphical layouts and typographic conventions.
An approach for optimizing the delivery of panoramic scenes in immersive applications by utilizing two types of feedback signals to enhance user experience while adhering to bandwidth constraints.
An innovative rendering method that combines volumetric path tracing with Gaussian splatting to enhance interactive and immersive anatomical visualizations in medical imaging.
HyFi is a framework that combines decentralized finance (DeFi) with institutional backing to enhance market stability, transparency, and governance in cryptocurrency markets.

H.term: Hybrid Genetic Algorithm with Graph Neural Networks
H.definition: An optimization approach that combines a multi-modal Genetic Algorithm with a Graph Neural Network to enhance timetabling solutions by integrating domain knowledge into the search process.
A two-step procedure that combines the HAR (Heterogeneous Autoregressive) model with ElasticNet regularization to analyze volatility spillovers across multiple financial markets.

A framework that integrates insights from fine-tuned time-series large language models (TSLMs) into general reasoning large language models (GRLMs) to enhance diagnostic reasoning for time-series data.
A theoretical framework that characterizes the expressive power of various attention mechanisms, including linear and hybrid attention, in relation to standard full attention in transformers.

H.term: Hybrid LLM and Higher-Order Quantum Approximate Optimization (HO-QAOA)
H.definition: A hybrid pipeline for collateral optimization under ISDA Credit Support Annexes, integrating a language model for term extraction and a quantum-inspired optimization method for complex search spaces.
A dialogue system that combines the responsiveness of large language models with a theory-aligned, rule-based framework to support learner reflections in educational contexts.
A systematic trading framework that combines neural networks with tree-based voting models to forecast market risk and generate alpha, utilizing a diverse feature set across multiple asset classes.

H.term: Hybrid Model for Misophonia Trigger Sound Detection
H.definition: A sound event detection model that combines a frozen pre-trained CNN with a time-series module to identify trigger sounds in synthetic soundscapes, aimed at assistive technologies for misophonia.
An automatic glossing pipeline that combines neural sequence labeling with large language model post-correction, aimed at improving interlinear glossed text creation for low-resource languages.

A sensorless control framework that integrates physics-consistent modeling with hybrid reinforcement learning to achieve precise force regulation in surgical robotic grasping, minimizing hardware complexity.
An architecture that combines analog processing-using-memory with CMOS-based fixed-function hardware to perform a wider range of matrix-based operations while maintaining energy efficiency.

A framework that integrates quantum computing with classical methods to optimize database problems in real-time, allowing for fine-grained control over solving procedures to balance accuracy and efficiency.

A novel approach that combines quantum computing techniques with classical methods to optimize flow scheduling in multi-beam digital satellites by reformulating scheduling problems as Quadratic Unconstrained Binary Optimization (QUBO).
A proposed framework that integrates a lightweight neural network with the Rao detection method to enhance the detection of weak signals in complex, non-Gaussian noise environments.
This framework integrates responsible AI principles with stochastic optimization to ensure fairness, robustness, and auditability in multivendor 6G network management systems.
An architecture that combines Transformers and State Space Models (SSMs) to optimize in-context retrieval tasks, achieving linear-time processing while maintaining effective retrieval capabilities.

Hybrid TD3 is an extension of the Twin Delayed Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient algorithm that effectively manages hybrid action spaces, allowing for simultaneous optimization of high-level decisions and low-level execution in robotic manipulation tasks.

This technique enhances continual learning for software vulnerability prediction by addressing catastrophic forgetting through a selective replay mechanism that prioritizes samples based on confidence levels during temporal fine-tuning.

A hybrid modeling framework that integrates physics-based and data-driven models through mutual regularization, treating both components as co-trained agents to enhance robustness against sparse and noisy data.
Hybrid-Gym is a training framework for coding agents that focuses on generalizing across diverse tasks by decomposing trajectories into fine-grained components and designing auxiliary training tasks.
A generative-based video system that combines traditional codecs with neural approaches to enhance video quality during mobile streaming under low bandwidth conditions.
An open-source network login cracker that supports numerous protocols for authorized penetration testing. Hydra can test password strength against services like SSH, FTP, HTTP, and databases.

The process of attaching event handlers and making a server-rendered HTML page interactive on the client side. Hydration bridges the gap between server-side rendering and client-side interactivity in frameworks like React and Next.js.
HydroShear is a hydroelastic tactile simulator designed for sim-to-real reinforcement learning, focusing on complex force and shear interactions in contact-rich tasks to bridge the sim-to-real gap.
A novel model for quantifying and detecting financial bubbles, integrating Log-Periodic Power Law (LPPL) with sentiment scores and a hype index to generate bubble labels and confidence scores.

Hyper-KGGen is a skill-driven framework for generating high-quality knowledge hypergraphs, addressing the scenario gap in knowledge extraction by adapting to diverse domains and balancing structural and fine-grained details.
A class of matrix-valued rational functions associated with absolute stability, characterized by their matrix-convex structure and closed under inversion, relevant to control theory.
A method for carrier phase positioning in distributed MIMO systems that achieves high accuracy despite phase synchronization errors, leveraging deep learning for training.

HOSC is an activation function designed for implicit neural representations that utilizes periodic activations to maintain high-frequency information while allowing control over gradient stability through a parameter that adjusts the Lipschitz bound.

HPR is a signal processing technique that utilizes hypercomplex numbers, such as quaternions and octonions, to recover complex-valued signals from intensity-only measurements, enhancing applications in optical imaging and computational sensing.

A software-defined IT infrastructure that virtualizes all elements of conventional hardware-defined systems including compute, storage, and networking. HCI combines these components into a single, easily managed platform.
A computational paradigm that represents data using high-dimensional, low-precision vectors called hypervectors, enabling lightweight and noise-tolerant operations for learning and inference.
A method for modeling and mapping Spiking Neural Networks on neuromorphic hardware using hypergraphs, addressing the NP-hard problems of neuron partitioning and placement.

A hypergraph of concepts is a model that embeds research pathways within a multi-dimensional framework, allowing for the identification of leadership in emerging scientific areas by analyzing the connections between concepts and researchers.
A method for error reduction in randomized algorithms using $k$-uniform hypergraphs, which involves sampling hyperedges and utilizing their vertices as seeds for multiple algorithm runs.

HyperLTL is a logic framework that enables the expression of temporal hyperproperties in reactive systems, allowing for the explicit quantification over execution traces to reason about system properties related to information flow and security.
An extension of hypertext that includes graphics, audio, video, and interactive elements linked together in a non-linear web of information. REST APIs that use hypermedia controls are called HATEOAS.
A memory management technique for large language models that optimally utilizes hierarchical memory in supernode architectures to alleviate memory constraints and improve computational efficiency.

A parameter whose value is set before the machine learning training process begins, as opposed to model parameters that are learned during training. Examples include learning rate, batch size, and number of hidden layers.
The process of finding the optimal set of hyperparameters for a machine learning model. Techniques include grid search, random search, and Bayesian optimization.
A hypergraph-based framework for audio deepfake detection that models high-order interactions among features, capturing complex patterns beyond local temporal and spectral features.

A company that provides cloud computing infrastructure at massive scale, typically operating millions of servers across global data centers. AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud are the three major hyperscalers.
A formal proof system that extends traditional sequent calculi by allowing hypersequents, which are sets of sequents, leading to a complexity classification that includes hyper-Ackermannian bounds.
Text displayed on a computer that contains links to other text, documents, or resources that the reader can immediately access. Hypertext is the foundational concept of the World Wide Web.

Software that creates and manages virtual machines by abstracting physical hardware resources. Type 1 hypervisors run directly on hardware (VMware ESXi, KVM), while Type 2 run on a host operating system (VirtualBox, Parallels).

A symbolic formalism for hypothesis refinement where LLM agents collaboratively operate on a shared hypothesis state using a structured grammar of reasoning moves, emphasizing incremental scientific reasoning.
A statistical method for making decisions about population parameters based on sample data. Hypothesis testing uses null and alternative hypotheses, test statistics, and p-values to draw conclusions.

HyRA is a hybrid resource allocation framework for Radio Access Network (RAN) slicing that combines dedicated resource allocations with shared resource pooling to enhance efficiency and performance under varying traffic conditions.

A Netflix library that implements the circuit breaker pattern for latency and fault tolerance in distributed systems. Hystrix isolates points of access to remote systems and prevents cascading failures.

A hybrid temporal-aware attention architecture designed for long behavior sequential recommendation, which decouples long-term stable preferences from short-term intent spikes to improve retrieval precision and efficiency.

I.definition: A causal discovery method that integrates causal graphs over non-identical variable sets using causal additive models, addressing the limitations of traditional approaches in identifying causal relationships from multiple datasets.

I-Perceive is a foundation model designed for active perception, enabling robots to proactively adjust their viewpoint based on language instructions to acquire task-relevant information in large-scale environments.

Input/Output — the communication between a computer system and the outside world, including reading from and writing to files, network sockets, and peripheral devices. I/O operations are often a performance bottleneck.

Infrastructure as a Service — a cloud computing model that provides virtualized computing resources over the internet. Users manage operating systems, storage, and applications while the provider manages the underlying infrastructure.

Identity and Access Management — a framework of policies and technologies for ensuring that the right people have appropriate access to technology resources. Cloud IAM services manage user identities, roles, and permissions.
A document in AWS Identity and Access Management that defines permissions for actions on resources. IAM policies are attached to users, groups, or roles to control access to cloud services.
An Interactive Connectivity Establishment candidate representing a potential network path for WebRTC peer-to-peer communication. ICE candidates include host, server-reflexive, and relay addresses.
An analysis of the cross-border shifts in initial coin offerings (ICOs) following regulatory clarifications, revealing significant changes in entrepreneurial finance activity across regions.

ICON is a probing-to-mitigation framework that defends against indirect prompt injection attacks in LLM agents by identifying over-focusing signatures in the latent space, allowing for task continuity while neutralizing malicious instructions.
A font that contains symbols and glyphs instead of letters and numbers, used to display scalable vector icons on web pages. Font Awesome and Material Icons are popular icon font libraries.
A human-AI co-creative system for generating stylistically consistent icons, organizing generation along axes of semantic richness and visual complexity based on user-specified concepts.

This system automates the classification and recommendation of digitized artworks using the Iconclass vocabulary, integrating YOLOv8 object detection and rule-based inference to enhance the understanding of abstract meanings in art.

ICPNS (In-Community Popularity Negative Sampling) is a strategy for generating reliable negative samples in recommendation systems by utilizing the popularity of items within specific communities to enhance model training.

ICSSPulse is an open-source, modular platform for penetration testing of industrial control systems, integrating network scanning and protocol-aware interactions to assess security vulnerabilities in real-time operational conditions.

An intent-driven diffusion-based path planning framework for mobile data collectors in IoT-enabled dense wireless sensor networks, focusing on optimizing rendezvous point selection and data collection tours.

Integrated Development Environment — a software application that provides comprehensive tools for software development including a code editor, debugger, compiler, and build automation. Popular IDEs include VS Code, IntelliJ IDEA, and Xcode.
An algebraic proof system that allows for the verification of mathematical statements through algebraic techniques. This system has been studied for its separation theorems in complexity theory.

The creative process of generating, developing, and communicating new ideas for solving problems. In design thinking, ideation sessions use brainstorming, sketching, and prototyping to explore solution spaces.

A property of certain operations whereby they can be applied multiple times without changing the result beyond the initial application. Idempotent APIs ensure that duplicate requests do not cause unintended side effects.
A unique key sent with an API request to ensure that retrying the same operation produces the same result. Idempotent keys prevent duplicate actions when network issues cause request retries.
A name given to a program element such as a variable, function, class, or module. Identifiers follow naming rules specific to each programming language and should be descriptive and consistent.

A system of trust between multiple security domains that enables users to authenticate once and access resources across those domains. Federation eliminates the need for separate credentials per system.

The organizational process for managing digital identities throughout their lifecycle, including creation, maintenance, and deactivation. Identity management systems handle user provisioning, authentication, and access control.
A system that creates, maintains, and manages identity information and provides authentication services to relying applications. Examples include Okta, Auth0, and Azure Active Directory.

A method for cold-start click-through rate (CTR) prediction that utilizes multimodal large language models (MLLMs) to generate proxy embeddings from rich content signals, enabling effective prediction for new items without prior usage data.
Intrusion Detection System — a device or software application that monitors a network or systems for malicious activity or policy violations. IDS types include network-based and host-based systems.

The IEEE standard for floating-point arithmetic that defines formats for representing real numbers in binary, including single and double precision. It specifies rounding rules, special values like NaN and infinity, and exception handling.

An inline frame HTML element that embeds another HTML page within the current page. Iframes are used for embedding third-party content, ads, and sandboxed widgets while maintaining isolation from the parent page.

iGVLM is an instruction-guided visual modulation framework that employs a decoupled dual-branch architecture to enhance task-specific visual reasoning in large vision-language models by utilizing dynamic visual representations.
Immediately Invoked Function Expression — a JavaScript design pattern that executes a function as soon as it is defined. IIFEs create a private scope and avoid polluting the global namespace.
A general framework designed to implement Inference-time Language Acquisition (ILA) by equipping LLMs with a set of behavioral primitives to facilitate learning new programming languages.
A novel preference fine-tuning framework for LLM-based recommendation systems that utilizes self-hard negatives from intermediate layers to improve the discriminative power of negative samples.

A visual representation of data stored in digital format, using formats like JPEG, PNG, GIF, and WebP. Digital images consist of pixels and are used throughout computing for interfaces, media, and communication.

The task of automatically generating a textual description of an image using computer vision and natural language processing. Image captioning models combine CNNs for visual features with RNNs or transformers for text.

A computer vision task that involves categorizing images into predefined classes based on their visual content. Convolutional neural networks have achieved superhuman performance on many image classification benchmarks.
Techniques for reducing the file size of digital images while maintaining acceptable visual quality. Compression can be lossless (PNG) or lossy (JPEG), trading file size for image fidelity.

A standardized way to organize and store digital image data. Common formats include JPEG for photographs, PNG for lossless graphics, SVG for vectors, WebP for web optimization, and GIF for animations.

The manipulation and analysis of digital images using algorithms for tasks like filtering, enhancement, segmentation, and recognition. Image processing is fundamental to computer vision and medical imaging.
The ability of software to identify objects, places, people, or other elements within digital images. It uses deep learning models trained on large datasets to recognize visual patterns.
A storage and distribution system for container images, allowing developers to push, pull, and manage versioned container images. Docker Hub and Amazon ECR are popular image registries.
A computer vision technique that partitions an image into multiple segments or regions, each corresponding to different objects or parts of objects. It provides pixel-level understanding of images.
A single image file containing multiple smaller images arranged in a grid, used to reduce the number of HTTP requests. CSS sprites improve web page loading performance by batching image downloads.
An extension of the iMiGUE dataset that provides a spontaneous affective corpus for studying emotional states in speech, enriched with metadata such as transcripts and speaker-role separation.
A tool designed for model checking and synthesis of memoryless imperfect information strategies for Strategic Timed CTL, providing significant performance improvements over previous methods.
