A multi-objective evolutionary algorithm designed for neural architecture search that utilizes bi-population strategies and uniform sampling to improve exploration and diversity in the search space.

MoECLIP is a Mixture-of-Experts architecture tailored for zero-shot anomaly detection, which allows for patch-specialized processing of input data, thereby improving the model's ability to detect anomalies in unseen categories.

Motion Group-aware Gaussian Forecasting is a framework for long-term scene extrapolation that employs motion-aware Gaussian grouping and optimization to maintain physically consistent motion in dynamic scenes.
Mogan STEM is a WYSIWYG structured editor that addresses the limitations of TeX in compilation efficiency and user experience, offering features like fast rendering and on-demand plugin loading.

A communication framework that employs non-linear, cross-reactive sensors for detecting and transmitting information about molecule mixtures, addressing limitations of traditional molecular communication systems.

A genetic algorithm-based framework for molecular optimization that utilizes multiple critics, including an ensemble of Gaussian process surrogates and a pretrained text-molecule aligned encoder, to efficiently rank candidate molecules before oracle evaluations.

An analysis of collective behavior exhibited by AI agents on a social media platform, revealing statistical patterns similar to human communities, including heavy-tailed activity distributions and power-law scaling.

MoltGraph is a longitudinal temporal graph dataset designed for detecting coordinated-agent behavior in social platforms, capturing heterogeneous interactions and temporal drift to analyze visibility manipulation strategies.
A Bayesian framework that distinguishes between mean and variance causal graphs, enhancing interpretability in causal discovery from heteroscedastic data.
A sequence of relaxations for polynomial optimization problems that converges in finitely many steps under certain convexity conditions, facilitating the solution of non-convex objectives.

A design pattern in functional programming that defines how functions, operations, and side effects can be composed. Monads provide a way to chain operations while managing side effects like I/O or errors.
The process of generating revenue from a product, service, or asset. Common strategies include subscriptions, advertising, freemium models, micropayments, and licensing.

A popular open-source NoSQL document database that stores data in flexible, JSON-like BSON documents. MongoDB excels at handling unstructured data, horizontal scaling, and rapid development where schema flexibility is important.

The continuous observation of system performance, availability, and health through metrics, logs, and alerts. Monitoring tools like Prometheus, Grafana, and Datadog provide real-time visibility into application behavior and infrastructure status.
A set of vertices in a graph that, when any edge is removed, ensures that the distance between some two vertices in the set increases, used for monitoring networks against communication failures.
A technique to dynamically modify or extend code at runtime without changing the original source. While useful for quick fixes, monkey patching can make code harder to understand and maintain.

MonkeyTree is a system designed to mitigate network congestion in multi-tenant GPU clusters through job-migration based defragmentation, addressing limitations of traditional network-layer techniques.
This technique utilizes Vision-Language Models (VLMs) to estimate 3D object positions from monocular RGB images, enhancing human-robot interaction through natural language input.

A software architecture where all components of an application are tightly coupled and deployed as a single unit. Monolithic architectures are simpler to develop initially but can become difficult to scale.
A software development strategy where code for many projects is stored in a single version control repository. Monorepos simplify code sharing, dependency management, and cross-project changes.

This system allows developers to specify sequencing constraints on smart contract function calls, balancing expressivity with the tractability of block production by enforcing global priorities on transactions.

A theoretical framework that determines the necessity of coordination in organizational tasks based on whether tasks are non-monotonic, providing insights into resource allocation in multi-agent systems.

A method for testing the monotonicity of real-valued functions on directed acyclic graphs, providing lower bounds for query complexity against non-adaptive testers, enhancing understanding of function behavior in graph structures.

A novel algorithm that utilizes Monte Carlo methods for effective step-size selection in active noise control systems, enhancing convergence speed and adaptability to varying environmental conditions.

A broad class of computational algorithms that rely on repeated random sampling to obtain numerical results. Monte Carlo methods are used in optimization, numerical integration, and probabilistic analysis.

M.term: Monte Carlo Rendering to Diffusion Curves
M.definition: A method for generating vector graphics from noisy samples produced by a Monte Carlo renderer, allowing for robust extraction of editable vector images with shading information.

An ergodic streaming encoder for automatic speech recognition (ASR) designed for latency-critical applications, optimizing low time-to-first-token and high transcription accuracy on resource-constrained devices.

The observation that the number of transistors on a microchip doubles approximately every two years, while the cost of computers is halved. This trend has driven exponential growth in computing power.

M.term: More-Than-Human Representation in Crisis Resilience Planning
M.definition: An approach that incorporates non-human species and ecological systems into participatory crisis resilience planning, utilizing design probes such as conversational agents to facilitate discussions on non-human perspectives.
A class of best-response schemes that utilize Moreau smoothing to efficiently learn equilibria in nonsmooth and nonconvex games with expectation-valued objectives.
A post-training framework designed to align agentic language models for safe multi-step tool use, addressing challenges in sequential decision-making.

A novel end-to-end discrete audio tokenizer designed to enhance audio processing capabilities in large language models, overcoming limitations of fixed inductive biases found in previous architectures.
A framework that filters events triggered by instantaneous motion and ego-motion in real time, improving segmentation accuracy and reducing unnecessary event processing in event cameras.
A motion-centric, gradient-based data attribution framework that isolates temporal dynamics from static appearances in video generation, enabling efficient computation of motion-specific influences.
An open-source framework for scalable and synchronized video-audio generation, aiming to improve the quality and efficiency of audio-visual content creation.
A representation of permutations in compressed space that optimizes query time by utilizing long contiguously permuted intervals, significantly enhancing the performance of compressed text indexes.
MoveContinuousSkill is an optimized planning method for skill-based robot programming that enhances motion sequence efficiency by integrating motion sequence optimization into the execution process.
A framework that synthesizes diverse motion patterns from isolated audio events to create controlled training data for models focused on spatial audio understanding.
A hybrid Lagrangian/Eulerian method that eliminates particle-based quadrature during solve time, improving performance by treating particles as carriers of kinematic state and material history.

MPU is a privacy-preserving framework for machine unlearning in large language models, introducing a method for generating multiple perturbed model copies to facilitate secure unlearning without compromising client data privacy.
A privacy-preserving framework for machine unlearning that utilizes multiple perturbed copies of model instances to allow clients to execute unlearning without disclosing sensitive data.

Message Queuing Telemetry Transport — a lightweight messaging protocol designed for constrained devices and low-bandwidth networks. MQTT is widely used in IoT applications for device-to-cloud communication.
A motion correction system for MRI that utilizes inertial sensors to estimate orientation without integration, effectively eliminating drift and improving image quality during scans.

M.term: MRD Codes Construction via Circular-Shift Operations
M.definition: A method for constructing maximum rank distance codes that simplifies encoding by relying solely on circular-shift operations over finite fields, avoiding complex arithmetic associated with larger fields.

A multiscale transformer-based architecture designed for no-reference image quality assessment, featuring a dual-branch structure that captures fine and coarse details through spatial and channel attention mechanisms.
An OS-level scheduler that enhances GPU multitasking by proactively preparing working sets, addressing the limitations of high bandwidth memory capacity.
Problems that can be defined using Monadic Second-Order logic, with complexity lower bounds established for binary structures encoded by circuits under various restrictions.
MSVBench is a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating multi-shot video generation, featuring hierarchical scripts and reference images to assess long-form coherence and narrative appeal.

MTAVG-Bench is a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating multi-talker dialogue-centric audio-video generation, addressing challenges like identity drift and audio-visual misalignment in synthesized multi-participant dialogues.

MTL-VQA is a multi-task learning framework for no-reference video quality assessment that utilizes full-reference metrics as supervisory signals to learn perceptually meaningful features without human labels.
A multi-kernel adaptation framework for few-shot adaptation of Large Audio-Language Models, combining instruction-tuning and contrastive pretraining methods to enhance model performance on new tasks.

MulCovFuzz is a multi-component coverage-guided greybox fuzzer specifically designed for testing 5G protocols, which dynamically collects coverage information to enhance the robustness of security testing in complex mobile networks.

A security approach that integrates multiple protective measures, such as Web Application Firewalls, rate limiting, and dynamic blacklisting, to effectively mitigate low rate DDoS attacks in containerized environments.
An attention mechanism that employs multi-scale grid sampling to improve performance in various vision tasks, though it faces challenges with memory access patterns.
A framework for SQL injection detection that utilizes a multi-agent system to generate diverse requests and mediate responses, enhancing the context for improved detection performance.

A learning paradigm that aims to derive optimal policies from expert demonstrations in multi-agent environments, with a focus on understanding the exploitability of learned policies in relation to Nash equilibria.
An architecture that enables natural interaction between human planners and AI planners, facilitating iterative reasoning and elicitation processes through conversational explanations.
Trading systems that utilize large language models across multiple agents to generate trading strategies, with performance influenced by the communication structure among agents.

This framework integrates fine-tuned large language models (LLMs) with evidence retrieval and bias checks to enhance the reliability of clinical question answering, addressing challenges in verification and confidence signaling.
This framework facilitates trajectory design for UAV fleets in vehicular networks, allowing dynamic repositioning of aerial base stations to maintain connectivity in urban environments.

This model provides a comprehensive framework for studying affective polarization in social media by simulating partisan divisions and integrating various definitions of affective polarization for better interoperability across studies.
A generative-to-decision framework that combines Gaussian Hidden Markov Models with a diffusion generator to produce regime-conditioned scenarios for improved portfolio decisions under regime shifts.
A novel framework for coordinating the movement of multiple autonomous agents over mixed graphs, focusing on asynchronous settings and treating conflicts as soft constraints rather than hard ones.

A system that combines Control Barrier Functions to facilitate safe and efficient lane changes for Connected and Autonomous Vehicles by capturing multi-agent interactions through constructed interaction topologies.
An architectural design for psychotherapy chatbots that utilizes a multi-agent system to adhere to therapeutic protocols, enhancing the structure of dialogue in therapeutic settings.

A planning framework that formulates Signal Temporal Logic (STL) tasks as optimization problems, utilizing penalty functions and Block-Coordinate Gradient Descent to manage multi-agent decision-making complexity.
A problem in which a decision maker must choose between multiple options with uncertain rewards to maximize total payoff. It balances exploration of new options with exploitation of known good ones.
A computational framework for ensuring fairness in $k$-nearest neighbor ($k$-NN) queries across multiple attributes, imposing count constraints for proportional representation among protected groups.
The first public dataset for conversion rate prediction that features labels generated from multiple attribution mechanisms, facilitating the development of multi-attribution learning approaches.
An approach in integrated sensing and communication that utilizes frequency range 3 (FR3) for effective sensing across non-contiguous sub-bands, addressing dense multipath components.
A framework for interpreting multi-branch deep neural networks used in audio anti-spoofing, focusing on the decision dynamics of individual branches under various spoofing attacks.
A technique that utilizes pinching antennas for multi-cell interference management, significantly reducing power consumption in wireless communication systems.
A technique for improving speech emotion recognition in complex acoustic environments, utilizing a multi-channel approach that integrates deep learning methods for dereverberation and separation.

A strategy that uses cloud computing services from multiple providers to avoid vendor lock-in and optimize for cost, performance, and reliability. Multi-cloud architectures increase flexibility and resilience.

Multi-CoLoR is a framework for context-aware localization and reasoning across multi-language codebases, enhancing the ability of large language models to navigate complex repositories by considering organizational context and structural relationships.
A scheduling model that decomposes end-to-end timing constraints in autonomous driving systems into local relative deadlines for each sub-DAG, enhancing timing guarantees in complex systems.
A learning paradigm that addresses the challenges of training classifiers from heterogeneous data sources while exploiting shared structures to reduce sample complexity.

An extension of Markov Decision Processes that incorporates multiple probabilistic transition functions for different environments, allowing for more complex decision-making scenarios where the environment is not directly observable.

A security method requiring users to provide two or more verification factors to access an account — typically something you know (password), something you have (phone), and something you are (biometrics). MFA dramatically reduces unauthorized access.

This framework facilitates seamless cooperation among diverse devices in low-altitude wireless networks (LAWNs), enabling efficient execution of various network functions by assigning specific roles to each device.

A low-complexity framework that utilizes multi-head self-attention (MHSA) to directly estimate the signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) in user-centric non-terrestrial networks, reducing computational overhead.

M.term: Multi-Hit Cancer Driver Set Cover Problem (MHCDSCP)
M.definition: A binary classification problem that aims to identify gene combinations driving cancer by maximizing coverage of tumor samples while minimizing coverage of normal samples, formalized as a set cover problem.

A statistical model designed for analyzing multi-layer directed networks, accommodating distinct community structures for sender and receiver nodes. It facilitates the estimation of asymmetric community numbers through a novel goodness-of-fit test.

This framework integrates heterogeneous LLM-based query systems to facilitate semantic querying over multi-modal data, addressing the challenges posed by unstructured data types such as text, images, and videos.

A framework that integrates multiple sensing modalities and employs fusion learning techniques to enhance beamforming in mmWave communication for connected vehicles, addressing challenges posed by dynamic environments.
A novel approach that combines text and audio features to improve topic segmentation in spoken content, leveraging a fine-tuned text encoder and a Siamese audio encoder.

A heuristic designed to optimize the co-allocation of tasks and resources in multicore real-time systems, minimizing resource usage while ensuring schedulability under a preemptive EDF scheduling policy.

A framework that alternates between Speaker Recognition and Word Recognition passes to enhance transcription accuracy and speaker attribution in automatic speech recognition for medical conversations.
A computational framework for optimizing transport problems over multiple periods, featuring theoretical convergence rates and algorithmic improvements for financial applications.
A sentence transformer model utilized for generating semantic similarity scores to predict software vulnerabilities from unstructured attack descriptions in cybersecurity news.

MANDATE is a novel architecture designed for graph fraud detection that addresses the limitations of traditional graph neural networks by incorporating multi-scale neighborhood awareness to enhance global modeling capabilities.
A framework inspired by biological homeostasis that integrates regulation across multiple temporal scales to enhance the robustness and efficiency of artificial neural networks.
A robust framework for fact-checking that utilizes multiple sources and agents to retrieve accurate evidence, addressing the challenges of misinformation through advanced retrieval techniques.

This taxonomy categorizes multi-speaker conversational audio deepfakes into partial and full manipulations, addressing the emerging security threats posed by realistic and accessible audio deepfake technologies.

MSAF is a novel module designed for song aesthetics evaluation that utilizes bidirectional cross-attention mechanisms to enhance the understanding of song attributes and their impact on listener perception.

M.term: Multi-Task and Multi-Stage Query Rewriting Framework
M.definition: A framework that utilizes large language models (LLMs) for enhancing e-commerce search by rewriting user queries through multiple tasks and stages to improve relevance and conversion rates.

M.term: Multi-Task Deep Learning for Delivery Delay Prediction
M.definition: A multi-task deep learning model designed to predict delivery delay durations in complex logistics networks, effectively handling imbalanced data where delayed shipments are rare but critical.
An approach that emphasizes scaling the number of tasks rather than samples per task in model-based reinforcement learning, leveraging shared world models for effective online learning.
A software architecture where a single instance of software serves multiple customers or tenants. Each tenant's data is isolated and invisible to other tenants, while sharing the same infrastructure.
An enhanced version of the A* pathfinding algorithm that integrates multi-threading with Recast-based mesh generation and trajectory smoothing for scalable navigation in dynamic game environments.
A class of error-correcting codes derived from multi-twisted Reed-Solomon codes, characterized by their minimum distance properties and efficient decoding methods for correcting multiple errors.
A method for efficiently compressing multi-vector document representations in information retrieval, addressing the computational and storage costs associated with late interaction paradigms.
A technique for improving multilingual embeddings by aligning multiple translations of text across different languages, enhancing cross-lingual representation in natural language understanding tasks.
A dataset and benchmark for hierarchical activity recognition from long-form audio, featuring annotations at multiple semantic levels and supporting fine-grained understanding of human activities.

A network communication method where data is transmitted from one sender to multiple specific receivers simultaneously. Multicast is efficient for streaming media, software updates, and group communication.
An extension of omniprediction to multiclass settings, where the algorithm provides suboptimality bounds against a potentially infinite set of comparator predictors.
A directed acyclic graph framework designed to enhance digital forensic education by mapping the relationships between various skills and competencies necessary for cybersecurity professionals.
An innovative indexing technique for large-scale recommendation systems that integrates the learning of item embeddings and their indices, optimizing retrieval quality for newly created items.
M-DMCA is a novel algorithm for efficient node discovery in cognitive radio networks, featuring dual-channel selection and a three-way handshake mechanism to reduce rendezvous time.

MLMQ is a novel data structure designed to optimize the Single-Source Shortest Path (SSSP) problem on GPUs by distributing multiple queues across the GPU's multi-level parallelism and memory hierarchy, reducing synchronization overhead and improving adaptivity to diverse inputs.
A framework for safe robot grasping that employs a dual-patch optimization scheme to reconcile discrepancies between RGB and depth modalities.
Artificial intelligence systems that can process and integrate information from multiple types of input such as text, images, audio, and video. GPT-4V and Gemini are examples of multimodal models.
A reference-free data selection pipeline for accent adaptation in ASR systems that leverages multimodal consistency to improve training data quality without relying on labeled data.

dLLMs are a class of models that leverage diffusion processes in multimodal contexts, offering advantages such as bidirectional attention and parallel generation, positioning them as competitive alternatives to traditional autoregressive models.
A scalable framework that combines automated extraction of visual, audio, and textual features to analyze their joint contributions in short-form video content.
An integrated approach that combines structured credit variables, climate panel data, and unstructured textual narratives using architectures like LSTM and GRU for credit risk assessment.

This theory evaluates cross-modal reasoning abilities in multimodal large language models by addressing the shortcomings of current benchmarks that contain shortcut questions, ensuring reliable assessments of model performance.

Advanced language models capable of interpreting visual data and providing explanations in natural language, enhancing the analysis of graph data and aligning computational measures with human perception.
A sampling mechanism that integrates photometric, semantic, and geometric priors to enhance the robustness of hierarchical 3D Gaussian Splatting in sparse-view novel view synthesis.
A problem in graph theory where the goal is to determine if a graph contains a multipacking of a specified size, with implications for computational complexity.

A collaborative quantum protocol that allows three or more mutually distrustful users to establish a shared secret key, structured along axes of network architecture, quantum resources, and security models.

M.term: Multiparty Session Type with Mixed Choice
M.definition: A framework that allows transient inconsistencies in protocol states among distributed participants while ensuring eventual consistency, enhancing the robustness of multiparty communication protocols.

Multipath delay estimation involves leveraging channel state information from non-contiguous Wi-Fi bands to improve delay resolution for ranging and sensing applications, particularly in environments with frequency gaps.
A technique for Approximate k Nearest Neighbor (AKNN) search that involves building multiple sub-indexes separately to improve search efficiency in high-dimensional vector databases.

MERA is a tensor network framework that establishes a rigorous equivalence with paraunitary filter banks, enabling the learning of adaptive wavelets while enforcing exact orthogonality for efficient signal compression.

This design framework utilizes orthogonal time-frequency space (OTFS) modulation for integrated sensing and communication in vehicular networks, enhancing target localization and velocity estimation through Kalman filtering.
A programming technique that allows a single process to execute multiple threads concurrently, sharing the same memory space. Multithreading improves performance for I/O-bound and CPU-bound tasks.
An advanced model for analyzing complex multivariate event data that incorporates spatial information into the dynamics of event occurrence.

A zero-shot multi-agent system for vulnerability detection that utilizes an ensemble of agents to achieve high recall rates without the need for fine-tuning, surpassing traditional models in performance on benchmark datasets.
A novel framework that integrates the Muon optimizer into scalable generative recommendation systems, focusing on orthogonalized momentum updates to enhance training efficiency.
An open lexical dataset containing over 96,000 word-definition pairs in Arabic, created using a hybrid extraction pipeline to ensure accuracy and clarity.
An open-source, run-centric platform designed for the multimodal safety evaluation of large language models, integrating automatic cross-modal payload generation and multi-turn attack algorithms.

MuseAgent is an interactive multimodal agent that enhances large language models with structured symbolic representations from music scores and performance audio, enabling grounded reasoning in music understanding.
A concept describing the auditory similarity between two music fragments that differ in waveforms, with a method proposed for generating musical metamers using time-frequency scattering.
A manually annotated corpus designed to capture user intent behind music queries, categorizing musical descriptors to improve the effectiveness of recommendation systems.
A dataset that provides semantically rich natural language descriptions of music, aimed at improving alignment in music representation learning tasks.

A semantically rich dataset designed for music representation learning, capturing natural language descriptions of music to enhance alignment between text and audio for various retrieval and generation tasks.
A method of software testing where small modifications are made to a program's source code to evaluate the quality of existing tests. Good tests should detect and fail on these mutations.
This methodology tests the accuracy of LLM-generated code summaries by injecting targeted mutations into the code and verifying if the summary reflects the actual behavior of the code.
A mutual exclusion object used in concurrent programming to prevent multiple threads from simultaneously accessing a shared resource. Mutexes help avoid race conditions and ensure data consistency.
An automated method proposed to quantify competitive relationships among open-source software projects, helping to understand their survival dynamics in a competitive landscape.

MuxTune is a fine-tuning system that enables resource-efficient concurrent execution of multiple Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) tasks in multi-tenant datacenters, addressing GPU underutilization and communication delays.
Multi-View Subject-Consistent Video Generation (MV-S2V) synthesizes videos from multiple reference views, enhancing subject consistency in video generation beyond single-view constraints.

Model-View-Controller — an architectural pattern that separates an application into three interconnected components: the model (data and business logic), the view (user interface), and the controller (input handling). It promotes separation of concerns.

Multi-Version Concurrency Control — a database concurrency control method that maintains multiple versions of data to allow concurrent read and write operations without locking. PostgreSQL and MySQL InnoDB use MVCC.

Multi-View Visual-Semantic Representation (MViR) is a framework for fake news detection that captures multi-view visual-semantic features using pyramid dilated convolution and integrates these features through a dedicated fusion module.

Minimum Viable Product — the simplest version of a product with just enough features to satisfy early customers and provide feedback for future development. The MVP approach reduces risk and development time by validating ideas before full investment.

Model-View-ViewModel — an architectural pattern that facilitates separation of the development of the graphical user interface from business logic. It is particularly popular in WPF, iOS, and Android development.
A hybrid, weight-stationary Compute-in-Memory accelerator designed for efficient inference of large short-sequence Transformer models, utilizing ultra-dense Charge-Trap Transistors.
An open-source relational database management system that uses SQL for data management. MySQL is one of the most popular databases worldwide, powering web applications and enterprise systems.

N.definition: A contiguous sequence of N items from a given sample of text or speech used in natural language processing and text analysis. N-grams are foundational for language models and text classification.

The n-sided PL condition is a set of criteria that extends the Polyak-Łojasiewicz condition for finding Nash equilibria in nonconvex general-sum games. It facilitates the use of first-order gradient-based algorithms like block coordinate descent in these complex game scenarios.
A software architecture pattern that separates an application into multiple logical layers or tiers, each responsible for a specific aspect such as presentation, business logic, and data access.

A lossless compression system that integrates a transformer language model with ensemble context modeling and high-precision CDF coding, achieving superior compression results for natural language text.

A family of probabilistic classifiers based on applying Bayes' theorem with strong independence assumptions between features. Despite its simplicity, Naive Bayes performs well on text classification and spam filtering.
A domain name without the www prefix, such as example.com instead of www.example.com. Configuring naked domains requires A records or ALIAS records rather than standard CNAME records.

A technique used by compilers to encode additional information into function or variable names, such as namespace, type, or parameter information. Name mangling enables function overloading in compiled languages.
A subtask of natural language processing that identifies and classifies named entities in text into predefined categories such as persons, organizations, locations, and dates.
An extension of the traditional pipe concept that provides a named, persistent connection between processes for inter-process communication. Named pipes allow unrelated processes to exchange data.

A container that provides scope and organization for identifiers like variables, functions, and classes, preventing naming conflicts. Namespaces are used in programming languages, Kubernetes, XML, and DNS to create hierarchical, non-overlapping naming contexts.

Not a Number — a special floating-point value representing an undefined or unrepresentable numerical result. NaN is returned by operations like dividing zero by zero or taking the square root of a negative number.
A simple, user-friendly command-line text editor for Unix systems. Nano provides basic text editing with keyboard shortcuts displayed on screen, making it accessible to beginners.
A small-scale multitask multimodal language model that unifies emotional intelligence tasks through a cognitively inspired three-level hierarchy, bridging the gap between perception and interaction.
A benchmark dataset designed to partition questions based on the presence of answers in pre-training data, facilitating the understanding of knowledge encoding in large language models.

The manipulation of matter at the atomic and molecular scale, typically below 100 nanometers. In computing, nanotechnology enables smaller transistors, novel memory technologies, and quantum computing components.
A hierarchical framework for synthesizing soundtracks for long-form videos, utilizing emotion as a high-density compression of narrative logic.

A framework that integrates qualitative content analysis principles for annotating narratives in a structured manner, reducing errors by representing events as directed acyclic graphs (DAGs) with causal relations.
The process of refining a broad type to a more specific type based on runtime checks. In TypeScript, narrowing occurs through type guards, typeof checks, and control flow analysis.

NaSh is a natural language shell designed to leverage large language models while incorporating guardrails to help users recover from unintended outputs, aiming to enhance usability and safety in command-line interfaces.
A model for coalition formation among agents that allows for overlapping coalitions and operates under partial information, enhancing strategic collaboration in multi-agent systems.
A class of dynamics in game theory that converge to Nash equilibria, with implications for the complexity of computation in nondegenerate games.

The Nash-Sutcliffe loss is a negatively oriented counterpart to the Nash-Sutcliffe efficiency, defined as L_NS = 1 - NSE, providing a consistent measure for evaluating forecasts across multiple time series.

Network Address Translation — a method of mapping one IP address space into another by modifying network address information in packet headers. NAT enables multiple devices on a private network to share a single public IP address.

A managed network address translation service that enables instances in a private subnet to connect to the internet while preventing inbound connections. NAT gateways are essential for secure cloud architectures.
Techniques for establishing and maintaining network connections across devices behind NAT gateways. NAT traversal is essential for peer-to-peer applications, VoIP, and online gaming.
An estimate of the economic impact of carbon emissions on society, which varies with per capita income and population size, highlighting the net liabilities of carbon-intensive countries.

An application developed specifically for a particular operating system or platform using its native development tools and languages. Native apps offer the best performance and full access to device capabilities.
A module written in a compiled language like C or C++ that can be loaded and used by a higher-level runtime such as Node.js or Python. Native modules provide performance-critical functionality.

A lightweight, high-performance messaging system for cloud-native applications that supports publish-subscribe, request-reply, and queue-based messaging patterns. NATS is designed for simplicity and speed.
A database key formed from data that naturally exists in the real world, such as Social Security numbers or email addresses. Natural keys contrast with surrogate keys that are artificially generated.
A subfield of artificial intelligence that focuses on generating natural language text from structured data or other inputs. NLG is used in chatbots, report generation, and content creation.

A field of AI focused on enabling computers to understand, interpret, and generate human language. NLP powers applications like machine translation, sentiment analysis, chatbots, and voice assistants through techniques ranging from rule-based to deep learning.

A subtopic of natural language processing that focuses on machine reading comprehension — the ability to understand the meaning and intent behind human language, including context, sentiment, and nuance.

N.term: Natural Motion Trajectory for AMO Satellites
N.definition: A novel approach for station keeping of Areostationary Mars Orbit (AMO) satellites that employs a fuel-free natural motion trajectory, enhancing fuel and computational efficiency.

Natural privacy filters enable the exact composition of differentially private mechanisms with adaptively chosen privacy characteristics, accounting for the entire privacy profile of every query to enhance utility for a given privacy budget.
A sorting algorithm that orders strings containing numbers in the way a human would expect, so that 'item2' comes before 'item10'. Natural sort is preferred in user interfaces and file managers.
The system of menus, links, and interactive elements that allow users to move through a website or application. Effective navigation is intuitive, consistent, and helps users find content quickly.
A navigation mesh — a data structure used in game development and robotics that represents the walkable surfaces in an environment. NavMeshes enable AI agents to find paths through complex 3D spaces.

NE-Dreamer is a decoder-free model-based reinforcement learning agent that utilizes a temporal transformer to predict next-step encoder embeddings, optimizing temporal predictive alignment in representation space without requiring reconstruction losses.
An OS-centric abstraction for Near-Data Processing that facilitates the integration of disaggregated memory systems, aiming to overcome barriers to the adoption of this technology.

A concept in stable matching theory that allows for slight modifications of agent capacities to ensure desirable outcomes in matching settings, particularly when traditional stable matchings are not available.
A system that employs UAV swarms to create a movable antenna capable of near-field communication, utilizing a non-uniform spherical wave model to enhance aerial-to-ground link performance.

A set of communication protocols enabling two electronic devices to establish communication within a range of about 4 centimeters. NFC is used for contactless payments, access control, and data sharing.

N.term: Near-Field Dictionary Design for Hybrid MIMO
N.definition: A novel approach for channel estimation in near-field ultra-massive MIMO systems that utilizes a tailored grid design, optimizing measurement reconstruction for ground-located user equipment.

An algorithm that constructs suffix trees in near-real-time with double logarithmic worst-case guarantees on update time per letter, enabling efficient computation of string problems such as longest repeating suffix arrays and minimal unique substrings.
An algorithm that finds the closest data point to a given query point in a dataset. Approximate nearest neighbor search techniques like HNSW and Annoy enable efficient similarity search at scale.
A computational problem in election theory that determines if a nominated candidate can win against all possible nominations from other parties under various voting rules.
An extension of spectrum-preserving string sets that utilizes branching structures to efficiently represent strings with matching k-mer spectra, enhancing storage and traversal in genomics.
The first fuzzing framework specifically designed for nested virtualization, which systematically targets the complexities and attack surfaces introduced by nested hypervisors.
A class of Boolean functions characterized by a flat magnitude spectrum under the nega-Hadamard transform, with applications in cryptographic design due to their optimal spectral properties.
The practice of caching negative responses such as 404 errors or empty results to prevent repeated lookups for non-existent resources. Negative caching reduces load on backend systems.
A testing technique that verifies the system behaves correctly when receiving invalid, unexpected, or erroneous input. Negative testing ensures the application handles error conditions gracefully.
An optimization problem that involves assigning labels to vertices in a graph to maximize local rewards based on the closed neighborhood of each vertex.

NeighborMAE is a self-supervised learning framework that exploits spatial dependencies between neighboring Earth Observation images during Masked Image Modeling, enhancing representation learning by leveraging rich contextual information.
A native graph database management system that uses graph structures with nodes, relationships, and properties to represent and store data. Neo4j excels at queries involving connected data.
A modern, extensible fork of the Vim text editor that adds features like built-in LSP support, Lua scripting, and asynchronous plugin architecture while maintaining backward compatibility with Vim.
A multi-agent framework designed to convert neural radiance field (NeRF) research papers into executable code for Nerfstudio plugins, utilizing context-free grammar to ensure code correctness.
NESSiE, the NEceSsary SafEty benchmark, is a lightweight evaluation tool for large language models that identifies safety-relevant failures, serving as a necessary condition for model deployment.
A progressive Node.js framework for building efficient, reliable, and scalable server-side applications. Nest.js uses TypeScript by default and is inspired by Angular's modular architecture.
A function defined within the body of another function. Nested functions can access variables from their enclosing scope, enabling closures and encapsulation in languages that support them.

N.term: Nested Named Entity Recognition from Flat Annotations
N.definition: A method for learning nested named entity recognition using flat annotations, employing techniques such as string inclusions and entity corruption to enhance model performance despite limited nested resources.
A query within another SQL query, also known as a subquery. Nested queries allow complex data retrieval by using the results of one query as input to another.
A training methodology for human-AI teaming that allows agents to adaptively learn from each other, improving their ability to generalize across different contexts.
A collision-finding problem that demonstrates the superiority of adaptive queries over non-adaptive ones, revealing complexities within the PWPP class and its implications for Turing reductions.

A management tool that measures customer loyalty by asking customers how likely they are to recommend a product or service to others on a scale of 0 to 10. NPS categorizes respondents as promoters, passives, or detractors.
A framework for dynamic management of cache and backend storage devices in networked environments, optimizing I/O performance based on real-time network feedback.
A framework within the Linux kernel that provides packet filtering, network address translation, and connection tracking. Netfilter is the foundation for firewall tools like iptables and nftables.

A collection of open-source software tools developed by Netflix for building distributed systems. Components include Eureka for service discovery, Zuul for API gateway, and Hystrix for circuit breaking.
A comprehensive guide for optimizing the NetLogo platform to efficiently run large-scale agent-based models on cloud infrastructures, focusing on memory management and execution strategies.

A collection of interconnected devices that can communicate and share resources with each other. Networks range from local area networks connecting nearby devices to the global internet connecting billions.
A network access control list that acts as a stateless firewall for controlling traffic in and out of subnets in a virtual private cloud. Network ACLs provide an additional layer of security.

A method of mapping an IP address space into another by modifying network address information in the IP header of packets while they are in transit. NAT is widely used in home and enterprise networks.
The process of automating the configuration, management, and operations of a computer network using software tools. Network automation reduces human error and speeds up network provisioning.
A phenomenon where a product or service gains additional value as more people use it. Network effects are a key driver of growth for social media platforms and communication tools.
A network topology where all nodes are interconnected through a series of switches forming a mesh-like structure. Network fabrics provide high bandwidth and low latency in data centers.
The monitoring and analysis of computer network traffic for the purposes of information gathering, evidence collection, and intrusion detection. It is a sub-branch of digital forensics.
An approach to network architecture that uses virtualization technologies to manage core networking functions via software rather than proprietary hardware. NFV reduces costs and increases agility.
A hardware component that connects a computer to a network, providing a physical interface for network communication. Modern NICs support high-speed Ethernet and wireless connectivity.
The time delay experienced in transmitting data across a network from source to destination. Low latency is critical for real-time applications like video conferencing, gaming, and financial trading.

A load balancer that operates at the transport layer (Layer 4) of the OSI model to distribute incoming network traffic across multiple targets. It handles millions of requests per second with ultra-low latency.
The systematic tracking of a computer network's components and performance to detect slow or failing systems. Network monitoring tools alert administrators to outages and unusual patterns.
A Linux kernel feature that provides isolated network stacks with their own interfaces, routing tables, and firewall rules. Network namespaces are fundamental to container networking.

The ability to understand the internal state of a network from its external outputs including logs, metrics, and packet captures. Network observability goes beyond monitoring to provide deep analytical insights.
A set of rules and conventions that govern how data is transmitted and received across a network. Protocols like TCP/IP, HTTP, and FTP define communication standards between devices.
An extension of manifold Ricci curvature concepts to discrete structures such as graphs and hypergraphs, applicable in various fields including social networks and deep learning models.

Policies, practices, and tools designed to protect the integrity, confidentiality, and accessibility of computer networks and data. Network security includes firewalls, intrusion detection systems, VPNs, and network segmentation.
The practice of dividing a computer network into smaller subnetworks to improve security and performance. Segmentation limits the spread of attacks and helps enforce access control policies.
A form of virtual network architecture that allows multiple virtual networks to be created on top of a shared physical infrastructure. Network slicing is a key feature of 5G technology.
An endpoint for sending and receiving data across a computer network. Sockets provide the mechanism for inter-process communication using standard network protocols like TCP and UDP.
The arrangement of the elements of a communication network, including nodes and connecting lines. Common topologies include star, bus, ring, mesh, and hybrid configurations.

The process of combining hardware and software network resources into a single, software-based administrative entity. It decouples network services from the underlying hardware for greater flexibility and efficiency.
The practice of connecting computer systems and devices to share data and resources. Networking encompasses protocols, hardware, topologies, and the design of communication systems.

NeuEdge is a framework that integrates adaptive spiking neural networks with hardware-aware optimization techniques for efficient edge AI deployment, focusing on energy efficiency and low-latency inference.
A type of model that provides interpretable predictions by using a cardinally-minimal subset of input features, ensuring provable guarantees for explanations.

A research area focused on integrating algorithmic reasoning capabilities into neural architectures, particularly using graph neural networks to enhance their ability to learn and execute discrete algorithms.
An automated machine learning technique for finding optimal neural network architectures. NAS uses search algorithms to explore the design space of possible architectures for a given task.
Neural Audio Codecs are designed for compressing audio for storage and transmission, and are utilized in spoof detection by resynthesizing waveforms to simulate attacks in text-to-speech systems.

A flexible neural system for estimating budget shares in continuous allocation problems, incorporating state-dependent preferences and habit formation through an exponentially weighted moving average.

A flexible neural network architecture designed for estimating budget shares in continuous budget allocation, utilizing KL divergence minimization and incorporating habit formation through state-dependent preferences.

A framework for modeling overdispersed point process data using neural stochastic differential equations, facilitating efficient posterior inference and intensity estimation without relying on traditional MCMC methods.
A technique for reconstructing high-resolution material parameters in digital twins by leveraging neural networks to solve ill-posed physical inversion problems related to electromagnetic fields.

A lightweight screen-space post-processing technique that simulates the visual effect of tessellated geometry while rendering only low-polygon meshes by leveraging neural networks for contour refinement.

A paradigm that reformulates action prediction in Vision-Language-Action models from discrete waypoints to continuous action function regression, addressing issues of rigid sampling rates and quantization artifacts.

N.term: Neural Implicit Representations for SAR Imaging
N.definition: A technique using neural networks to model surface scattering in synthetic aperture radar imaging, improving the quality of reconstructed 3D imagery from incomplete data.
A sample-efficient method used to estimate single-site conditional probabilities in discrete denoising diffusion processes, enhancing generative modeling over discrete state spaces.

A neural-based technique for real-time rendering of diffuse global illumination that utilizes a compact pre-computed model to overcome aliasing artifacts and high memory consumption associated with traditional irradiance volume methods.
An approach to machine translation that uses neural networks to predict the likelihood of a sequence of words in the target language. NMT has largely replaced statistical machine translation methods.

A computing system inspired by biological neural networks, consisting of interconnected nodes (neurons) organized in layers that process information. Neural networks learn by adjusting connection weights during training, enabling pattern recognition and prediction.
A novel class of noise designed for neural stochastic differential equation frameworks, capable of capturing both long- and short-memory characteristics in time series data.

A hybrid framework that integrates neural networks with ordinary differential equations to model complex dynamical systems, such as antagonistic pneumatic artificial muscle dynamics, by embedding physical structure into the learning process.
A framework that generalizes Neural Cellular Automata to dynamic particle systems, allowing for continuous position updates and heterogeneous dynamics based on learnable neural rules.

N.term: Neural Quantum Support Vector Data Description (NQSVDD)
N.definition: NQSVDD is a classical-quantum hybrid framework for one-class classification that integrates a classical neural network with trainable quantum data encoding, enabling end-to-end optimized hierarchical representation learning.

A method for synthesizing novel views of complex 3D scenes by representing the scene as a continuous volumetric function using a neural network. NeRFs generate photorealistic images from limited input views.
Neural Routing Solvers leverage deep learning to address vehicle routing problems by learning implicit heuristic rules from data, thus reducing the reliance on traditional handcrafted heuristics.
Deep learning-based solvers for vehicle routing problems that learn implicit heuristic rules from data, reducing reliance on traditional handcrafted heuristics.

A unified Vision Language Model (VLM) for license plate recognition that integrates state classification and vehicle attribute extraction into a single forward pass, reducing latency and architectural complexity.
A neural network-based approach for computing Wasserstein geodesics between probability distributions, leveraging a minimax optimization framework.

A technique that applies the artistic style of one image to the content of another using convolutional neural networks. It enables creating images that combine the content and style of different sources.
A machine learning approach to formal reasoning that aims to automate the proof of Verification Conditions in program verification, addressing the limitations of existing Automated Theorem Provers.
A multi-view-based method for reconstructing high-fidelity 3D animal fur geometry using a strand-based representation, addressing challenges such as fine-scale details and view-dependent appearance.

A hybrid approach that combines data-driven methods with symbolic reasoning to enhance predictive maintenance systems, addressing limitations such as the need for large labeled datasets and lack of generalizability.

This pipeline integrates knowledge-graph embeddings, approximate nearest-neighbour search, and SPARQL-driven semantic filtering to provide advanced recommendations for cultural heritage knowledge graphs.
A proposed interdisciplinary field that integrates neuroscience insights into artificial intelligence development, focusing on areas such as embodiment, communication, and learning mechanisms.
A novel category of temporal neural networks designed for neuromorphic processing units, leveraging neuroscience insights to improve hardware efficiency and biological intelligence emulation.

The NeuroCognition benchmark evaluates large language models by focusing on foundational cognitive abilities through adapted neuropsychological tests, such as Raven's Progressive Matrices and Spatial Working Memory.

NeuroInv is a neurosymbolic framework for loop invariant generation that combines neural reasoning with Hoare logic to derive and refine invariants using backward-chaining weakest precondition reasoning.
A computing approach that uses electronic circuits to mimic the neuro-biological architectures present in the human brain. Neuromorphic chips are designed for energy-efficient AI processing.

NeuroPareto is an architecture designed for multi-objective optimization that combines rank-centric filtering and uncertainty disentanglement to efficiently navigate high-dimensional search spaces under computational constraints.

NeuroProlog is a neurosymbolic framework that enhances mathematical reasoning in large language models by compiling math word problems into executable Prolog programs, ensuring verifiable reasoning through a multi-task training strategy.

NeuroSCA (Neuro-Symbolic Constraint Abstraction) is a lightweight framework that enhances hybrid fuzzing for smart contracts by using LLMs to manage semantic constraints and reduce constraint pollution.

NeuroScaler is an AI-native, energy-efficient orchestrator for cloud and edge networks that focuses on energy and carbon constraints while managing multi-tier telemetry across various infrastructure components.
NeuroSkill is a proactive real-time agentic system that models human states of mind using biophysical and brain signals, enabling engagement on cognitive and affective levels.

A framework that integrates NeuroSymbolic abductive reasoning with active uncertainty minimization for generating structured radiology reports, enhancing fluency and factual consistency while addressing multi-hop clinical reasoning.

A neurosymbolic architecture that combines an optimized BERT model with logic tensor networks (LTN) to enhance explainability and effectiveness in detecting advanced persistent threats (APTs) under conditions of extreme class imbalance.

An innovative architecture that combines an optimized BERT model with logic tensor networks to enhance the detection of advanced persistent threats (APTs) in environments with extreme class imbalance.
A framework designed to audit the internal consistency of vision-language model-generated reports, combining neural and symbolic reasoning to ensure logical coherence in clinical reasoning tasks.
A multi-agent coaching system utilizing large language models to facilitate double-empathy communication, particularly for neurotypical users interacting with autistic partners.
A technique for reducing the size of trained neural networks by removing redundant weights or neurons that contribute minimally to the output. Pruning reduces model size and inference latency.
A NeRF-driven architecture designed for high-fidelity novel view synthesis and dense 3D reconstruction, capable of providing accurate poses and reliable depth from casually captured views.
A production function that allows for elasticity values less than one under specific parameter restrictions, providing insights into production efficiency.

A software analytics platform that provides real-time monitoring and performance management for web and mobile applications. New Relic offers application performance monitoring, infrastructure monitoring, and log management.
A reinforcement learning approach that incorporates news sentiment scores and raw market data to make end-to-end trading decisions, enhancing responsiveness to market events.

A React-based web framework that enables server-side rendering, static site generation, and full-stack development. Next.js provides built-in routing, API routes, and performance optimization features.
NextAds is a personalized video advertising framework that addresses the limitations of static ad inventories by enabling dynamic ad selection based on user context and preferences.

NEXUS is a framework that achieves bit-exact equivalence between Artificial Neural Networks and Spiking Neural Networks through neuromorphic gate circuits, ensuring mathematically identical outputs without approximation.

Non-Fungible Token — a unique digital identifier recorded on a blockchain that certifies ownership and authenticity of a specific digital asset. NFTs are used for digital art, collectibles, and gaming items.

A high-performance web server, reverse proxy, and load balancer known for its stability, low resource consumption, and ability to handle massive concurrent connections. Nginx serves a significant portion of the world's busiest websites.
A dynamic web and application server designed to run multiple languages and frameworks. NGINX Unit supports configuration changes without service disruption and manages application processes.
A precompute-reuse architecture for vector multiplication that reformulates multiplication as a structured operation to reduce power consumption and improve efficiency in AI acceleration tasks.
An automated build of a software project that runs every night using the latest source code. Nightly builds catch integration issues early and provide daily snapshots for testing.

A framework for formalizing natural-language descriptions of formal languages, enabling educational systems to assess and explain the accuracy of these descriptions against their formal representations.

NiMark is a non-intrusive watermarking framework designed to protect against screen-shooting attacks by embedding logical verification keys without altering the original content, thus maintaining fidelity.
The National Institute of Standards and Technology — a U.S. federal agency that develops technology standards and guidelines including cybersecurity frameworks used worldwide for risk management.
A powerful package manager for Linux and other Unix systems that makes package management reliable and reproducible. Nix ensures that installing or upgrading one package cannot break other packages.
A system designed for efficient and transparent temporal multiplexing of consumer GPUs, enabling better resource allocation for large machine learning workloads on single-user machines.

Natural language to SQL translation agents that leverage large language models to enable non-expert users to query relational databases, though they may struggle with complex datasets due to misconceptions about data intent.

Natural Language Processing — a branch of artificial intelligence that enables computers to understand, interpret, and generate human language. Applications include chatbots, translation, sentiment analysis, and text summarization.
Network Mapper — an open-source tool for network discovery and security auditing. Nmap scans networks to identify hosts, services, operating systems, and potential vulnerabilities.

Development platforms that allow users to create applications through visual interfaces and configuration rather than traditional programming. No-code tools democratize software creation for non-technical users.
An efficient algorithm designed to compute no-wait schedules for data streams in Time-Sensitive Networking, specifically tailored for daisy-chain topologies.
A fundamental unit of a data structure such as a linked list, tree, or graph. In networking, a node refers to any device connected to a network that can send, receive, or forward information.
A Kubernetes scheduling constraint that attracts pods to nodes with matching labels. Node affinity rules can be required (hard) or preferred (soft), providing flexible workload placement control.

A Prometheus exporter that collects hardware and operating system metrics from Linux hosts, including CPU, memory, disk, and network statistics. Node Exporter is essential for infrastructure monitoring.
A visual representation of nodes connected by edges that depicts relationships between entities. Node graphs are used in social network analysis, recommendation systems, and knowledge representation.

A JavaScript runtime built on Chrome's V8 engine that enables server-side JavaScript execution. Node.js uses an event-driven, non-blocking I/O model that makes it efficient for building scalable network applications, APIs, and real-time services.

A regularization technique that adds random noise to inputs, weights, or gradients during neural network training to improve generalization. Noise injection prevents overfitting by making the model robust to perturbations.

N.term: Noise Mitigation for Digital Visible Light Communication
N.definition: Techniques developed to reduce optical noise in Digital Visible Light Communication systems, improving signal integrity and reducing bit error rates.
The process of removing unwanted random variations from data or signals to improve quality and accuracy. Noise reduction techniques are used in image processing, audio processing, and data cleaning.
A method for performing inference from differentially private sufficient statistics in exponential families, which allows for uncertainty quantification while maintaining privacy.

A network architecture that utilizes Non-Orthogonal Multiple Access (NOMA) to enhance mobile edge computing (MEC) capabilities across multiple base stations, specifically designed for delay-sensitive and computation-intensive IoT applications.
A novel framework for non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) in fog radio access networks (F-RANs), optimizing user association, cache placement, and power allocation to minimize transmission delays.

Non-additive discrepancy generalizes classical discrepancy theory to non-additive functions, particularly in the context of fair division, and explores coverage functions in sparse settings to achieve comparable bounds.
An I/O processing model that allows other processing to continue before a transmission has finished. Non-blocking I/O is essential for building high-performance, concurrent applications.
An efficient batch machine teaching model that avoids collusion among learners, enhancing the effectiveness of teaching algorithms in graph-based contexts.

A decentralized optimization technique that utilizes the energy superposition property of wireless channels to enable low-latency and bandwidth-efficient consensus-based algorithms without requiring channel state information.
A property of algorithms or systems whose behavior is not predictable from the same set of inputs. Non-determinism arises from concurrency, randomness, or external inputs.
A class of sampling designs that achieve minimal expected star discrepancy by utilizing non-equal volume partitions, improving upon traditional jittered sampling methods.
A concept in multi-objective optimization that identifies Pareto front points that avoid extreme trade-offs, enhancing decision-making efficiency.
Specifications that describe the system's operational qualities rather than specific behaviors. Examples include performance, scalability, security, reliability, and maintainability requirements.
A robust framework for non-linear Principal Component Analysis that combines the interpretability of traditional PCA with the flexibility of neural networks, optimized using Evolution Strategies.

A control strategy for extremum seeking in multi-dimensional systems that utilizes sinusoidal dither signals to approximate the gradient of an objective function over a sphere, enhancing optimization performance.
A hardware interrupt that cannot be ignored or disabled by the processor, typically reserved for critical events like hardware failures or watchdog timer expiry. NMIs demand immediate attention.

N.term: Non-negative Matrix Factorization with Random Effects (NMF-RE)
N.definition: An extension of non-negative matrix factorization that incorporates random effects to model heterogeneity and control complexity in parts-based representations.
A database that does not use the tabular schema of rows and columns found in relational databases. Non-relational databases are designed for flexible data models and horizontal scaling.
A method for allocating resources in an online setting where demand is unpredictable, requiring only one historical sample per period to adapt to changing conditions.
Computer memory that retains stored information even when power is removed. Examples include flash memory, ROM, and newer technologies like Intel Optane that bridge the gap between DRAM and storage.

A number used once — a random or pseudo-random value that is used only once in a cryptographic communication to prevent replay attacks. Nonces are essential in authentication protocols, blockchain mining, and secure API communications.
A coordination mechanism for managing virtual queues in aviation that utilizes correlated equilibria to provide incentive-compatible recommendations for aircraft pushback decisions.
A novel approach for tagged MRI that addresses the challenges of motion tracking by using physics and deep generative priors to disentangle anatomy, tags, and motion.
A no-operation instruction that performs no action and is used as a placeholder. Noops maintain code structure when an operation is syntactically required but no action is needed.

A concept where IT environments are so automated and abstracted from the underlying infrastructure that operations teams are no longer needed to manage them. NoOps leverages cloud and serverless technologies.

In databases, the process of organizing data to reduce redundancy and improve integrity through a series of normal forms. In data science, normalization scales numerical features to a standard range, ensuring fair comparison across different measurements.
Specific stages of database normalization, including First Normal Form through Fifth Normal Form. Each form addresses specific types of data redundancy and update anomalies.

N.term: Normalized Action Reward-Guided Consistency Policy Optimization
N.definition: A method for optimizing policies in high-frequency decision-making tasks using large language models, focusing on aligning learned policies with frequent numerical state updates.
A single metric that best captures the core value a product delivers to customers. The North Star metric guides a company's long-term growth strategy and aligns teams around a common goal.

A category of databases that store data in formats other than traditional relational tables, including documents, key-value pairs, graphs, and wide columns. NoSQL databases like MongoDB, Redis, and Cassandra excel at handling unstructured data and horizontal scaling.
An attack that exploits vulnerabilities in NoSQL databases by injecting malicious code through user input. Similar to SQL injection but targets document-oriented and key-value databases.
A type of NoSQL database that stores data in columns rather than rows, optimized for queries over large datasets. Cassandra and HBase are wide-column stores suited for time-series and IoT data.
A database constraint that ensures a column cannot contain null values. NOT NULL constraints enforce data integrity by requiring that every row has a valid value for the specified column.

The practice of recording information for later reference, supported by digital tools like Notion, Obsidian, and Roam Research. Modern note-taking apps support linking, search, and multimedia content.
An interactive computing environment that allows users to combine code execution, rich text, mathematics, plots, and media. Jupyter Notebooks are widely used in data science and machine learning.

A plugin or add-on that enhances the functionality of computational notebooks like Jupyter. Extensions can add features like table of contents, code formatting, variable inspectors, and collaboration tools.
A managed service that provides message delivery from publishers to subscribers. Cloud notification services like AWS SNS and Google Cloud Pub/Sub enable decoupled communication between components.
A framework for pair-free video editing that utilizes sparse control and dense synthesis to overcome the limitations of requiring large-scale paired datasets.
A real-time scheduling framework for serving agentic vision-language models on a single GPU, optimizing latency and throughput through adaptive resource partitioning.

NovaLAD is a CPU-optimized document extraction pipeline that transforms unstructured documents into structured representations, utilizing concurrent YOLO models for element and layout detection to enhance generative AI applications.

N.term: Novel Data Storage Approaches for NWP
N.definition: Innovative storage solutions aimed at improving performance limitations of traditional POSIX file systems in high-performance computing and AI applications, particularly for operational Numerical Weather Prediction.

N.term: Novel Transform for NMR Envelope Extraction
N.definition: A new method for envelope extraction in nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) that allows for accurate results with fewer sampling points, challenging traditional scaling definitions.
A framework that allows for the construction of deterministic Turing Machines for NP-complete problems, bridging theoretical proofs with practical implementations.

A class of computational problems that are at least as hard as the hardest problems in NP. No known polynomial-time algorithm exists for NP-hard problems, and they often require approximation algorithms.

Node Package Manager — the default package manager for Node.js and the world's largest software registry. npm enables developers to share and reuse JavaScript packages, managing dependencies and scripts for millions of open-source projects.
The default public registry for Node.js packages where developers publish and download JavaScript modules. The npm registry hosts over two million packages and serves billions of downloads monthly.
Custom scripts defined in a Node.js project's package.json file that automate common development tasks like building, testing, and deploying. NPM scripts provide a simple task runner mechanism.
Neural Processing Unit — a specialized processor designed to accelerate neural network computations. NPUs are optimized for the matrix operations common in machine learning inference.

A noise-sensitive homomorphic encrypted database query engine that enables secure query processing on encrypted data while addressing challenges such as cipher-text expansion and computational overhead.

Network Time Protocol — a networking protocol for clock synchronization between computer systems over packet-switched networks. NTP ensures all machines in a distributed system agree on the current time.
A measurement framework developed to assess the robustness of the NTP Pool against monopoly attacks, focusing on the availability and accuracy of network time synchronization.
The package manager for .NET that enables developers to create, share, and consume useful code packages. NuGet hosts thousands of packages for the .NET ecosystem.
An operator that returns the right-hand operand when the left-hand operand is null or undefined. Null coalescing operators (?? in JavaScript and C#) simplify default value assignments.
A design pattern that uses a special object to represent the absence of a value instead of using null references. The null object implements the expected interface but performs no operations.
A runtime error that occurs when a program attempts to use a reference that points to no location in memory. Null pointer exceptions are among the most common bugs in languages like Java and C++.
A programming language feature that prevents null reference errors by distinguishing between nullable and non-nullable types at compile time. Languages like Kotlin and Dart have built-in null safety.

A type modifier that indicates a variable can hold either a value of its declared type or a null/nil reference. Nullable types help prevent null reference errors, and many languages now include null safety features.
A branch of pure mathematics devoted to the study of integers and integer-valued functions. In computer science, number theory is fundamental to cryptography, hashing, and random number generation.
The study of algorithms that use numerical approximation for mathematical analysis problems. Numerical methods are essential when exact solutions are impossible or impractical to compute.

Algorithms for solving mathematical problems using numerical approximation rather than exact symbolic computation. Common applications include root-finding, optimization, interpolation, and differential equation solving.
The degree of exactness with which a number is represented in a computing system. Limited numerical precision can cause rounding errors in floating-point arithmetic, affecting scientific computing.
A property of algorithms that ensures small errors in input data do not cause large errors in the output. Numerically stable algorithms are crucial for scientific computing and machine learning.
A fundamental Python library for scientific computing that provides support for large multidimensional arrays and matrices along with a collection of mathematical functions to operate on them.

A hierarchical multi-agent framework designed for personalized nutrition intervention, which utilizes a parallel-then-sequential reasoning topology to manage complex patient profiles with multimorbidity.
A framework built on top of Vue.js that enables server-side rendering, static site generation, and full-stack development. Nuxt.js provides a structured way to build Vue.js applications.
A parallel computing platform and API model created by NVIDIA that allows software developers to use NVIDIA GPUs for general-purpose processing. CUDA is widely used for deep learning training.
A production-grade architecture for deploying AI weather forecasting models at a significantly lower cost than traditional radar systems, aimed at improving early warning systems in Africa.

nvidia-pcm is a platform configuration manager for NVIDIA's OpenBMC that enables a single firmware image to adapt to multiple hardware variants by querying hardware identity data and exporting platform-specific configurations.

NVIDIA's high-bandwidth interconnect technology for linking GPUs together or connecting GPUs to CPUs. NVLink provides significantly faster data transfer than PCIe, enabling efficient multi-GPU computing.

Non-Volatile Memory Express — a host controller interface and storage protocol for accessing non-volatile storage media attached via PCI Express bus. NVMe provides significantly faster data transfer than SATA.
Nyrkiö is a change detection service integrated with MooBench that enables the detection of performance changes in software projects hosted on GitHub, enhancing performance measurement capabilities.

O.definition: Origin-Aware Mandatory Access Control (OAMAC) is a kernel-level enforcement model that enhances security by considering the execution origin of processes, thereby reducing the attack surface in operating systems.

An open standard for access delegation that allows users to grant third-party applications limited access to their accounts without sharing passwords. OAuth 2.0 is the industry standard for authorization, used by Google, Facebook, GitHub, and most web platforms.
A consolidation of OAuth 2.0 best practices and security recommendations into a single specification. OAuth 2.1 deprecates insecure flows like implicit grant and requires PKCE for public clients.

OBASE is an object-based address-space engineering approach designed to improve memory tiering by dynamically reorganizing virtual memory to cluster hot objects, thereby reducing the overprovisioning of DRAM in datacenters.
The deliberate act of making source code or data difficult to understand. Code obfuscation protects intellectual property and makes reverse engineering more challenging.

An instance of a class that bundles data (attributes) and behavior (methods) into a single entity. Objects are the fundamental building blocks of object-oriented programming and model real-world entities.
A computer vision technique that identifies and locates objects within images or video frames by drawing bounding boxes around them. Popular architectures include YOLO, SSD, and Faster R-CNN.
