Rudder is a software module designed for dynamic prefetching in distributed Graph Neural Network (GNN) training, utilizing Large Language Model (LLM) agents to adaptively optimize data fetching based on changing graph conditions.
A software module for distributed Graph Neural Network training that dynamically adapts prefetching strategies based on the changing conditions of graph data and training parameters.

A software system that executes business rules defined as condition-action pairs against data to derive conclusions or trigger actions. Rule engines separate business logic from application code for easier maintenance.
A deep reinforcement learning-based framework that unifies design rules in 3D floorplanning, automating compliance with complex hardware design rules to reduce manual adjustments.

A documented set of standardized procedures for routine operations and incident response. Runbooks provide step-by-step instructions that enable operators to handle situations consistently and efficiently.

The period during which a program is executing, or the environment that provides services to a running program. Runtime systems manage memory allocation, garbage collection, and other execution services.

A dynamic analysis technique that monitors a running system to check whether its behavior conforms to a formal specification. Runtime verification complements static analysis and testing for ensuring system correctness.

A systems programming language that focuses on safety, speed, and concurrency without a garbage collector. Rust's ownership system prevents memory errors at compile time and is increasingly used for systems software.

A benchmark consisting of 500 real-world, repository-level software engineering tasks specifically designed for evaluating automated issue resolution in Rust programming, addressing the need for large-scale testing in the Rust ecosystem.
RustCompCert is a verified compiler for a subset of Rust that ensures semantics preservation and memory safety through verifying compilation techniques, enhancing the reliability of Rust programs.

RustyDL is a program logic specifically designed for the Rust programming language, enabling auto-interactive, deductive verification directly at the source code level, which enhances the verification of complex functional properties.

RVCBench is a benchmarking framework designed to evaluate the robustness of voice cloning models across various audio generation scenarios, particularly under challenging conditions like noisy references and imperfect prompts.

RVR (Retrieve-Verify-Retrieve) is a multi-round retrieval framework that enhances question answering by iteratively refining document sets through verification and re-querying to maximize answer coverage.
A kernel engine designed for real-time detection and analysis of in-memory threats, addressing challenges posed by fileless execution techniques used by malware.

Reactive Extensions for JavaScript — a library for composing asynchronous and event-based programs using observable sequences. RxJS provides operators for transforming, filtering, and combining streams of data.

S.definition: A generalization of the Hamiltonian cycle problem that requires visiting each vertex of a graph with specific hop constraints between consecutive vertices, introducing new complexity considerations.
An ultra-low bitrate sound effect compression model that leverages diffusion autoencoders and offline quantization techniques to achieve high-quality audio compression down to 0.096 kbps.

A dataset and evaluation framework designed to infer speaking voice character attributes in literature, enhancing synthetic audiobook narration by assigning plausible voices to fictional characters based on their attributes.

Amazon Simple Storage Service — a scalable object storage service offered by AWS that provides high availability, durability, and security. S3 is widely used for backup, archiving, content distribution, and data lake storage.
A self-supervised learning framework for mean-opinion-score (MOS) prediction that employs spectral augmentation to enhance performance across multi-rate speech samples.
An agentic AI framework designed for end-to-end formal verification through multi-agent collaboration, addressing challenges in robustness and reliability in complex tasks.

Software as a Service — a software distribution model where applications are hosted in the cloud and accessed via the internet on a subscription basis. SaaS eliminates installation and maintenance, with examples including Slack, Salesforce, and Google Workspace.
A specialized Deep Neural Network architecture developed to accurately model the SABR framework for interest rate volatility, addressing limitations in traditional analytical approximations.
A new metric designed to assess the transferability of pre-trained large language models across different domains by leveraging spatial and contextual features without additional training.
A safety-generalizable large language model designed to enhance safety in task planning for robotic systems, capable of adapting to novel safety constraints across multiple domains.
A system-level co-design for managing KV-cache sharing in large language model inference that mitigates timing side-channel vulnerabilities while optimizing cache management.
A machine-learning-based scanner designed to detect malicious Python pickle files, providing a lightweight alternative to complex policy generation and code instrumentation.

A framework that formalizes safety decision-making in vision-language models through a structured protocol involving a planner and responder, enhancing multimodal safety by integrating user intent and visual evidence.
A hierarchical visual navigation framework that enhances safety and generalization in mobile robots by integrating learned guidance with safety-constrained control.
A contrastive dataset containing pairs of safe and unsafe prompts used to train models for improved safety in generative tasks, particularly in Text-to-Image generation.

A design pattern for managing distributed transactions by breaking them into a sequence of local transactions, each with a compensating action for rollback. Sagas maintain data consistency across microservices without distributed locks.
A framework for retrieval-augmented question answering that constructs a chunk-level graph to facilitate multi-hop evidence retrieval across heterogeneous data sources.

A two-layer decision architecture that integrates Large Language Models with fuzzy Control Barrier Function verification and graph-structured knowledge retrieval to enhance UAV decision-making under dynamic conditions.

A trainable low-bit attention mechanism that quantizes multiple attention matrix multiplications while maintaining fine-tuning performance, aiming to bridge the performance gap with full-precision attention.
A framework that leverages semantic consistency to reweight samples in recommendation systems, effectively reducing noise from implicit feedback and improving prediction accuracy.

SAILOR is an energy-efficient, ultra-lightweight RISC-V architecture designed for IoT security, balancing compact area and integrated cryptographic support to mitigate performance and security trade-offs in constrained environments.

Stylometry-Assisted LLM Analysis (SALA) is a method that combines quantitative stylometric features with large language model reasoning to enhance authorship attribution while assessing deanonymization risks in textual data.

Random data added to a password before hashing to ensure that identical passwords produce different hash values. Salting defends against rainbow table attacks and makes brute-force attacks more expensive.

Samaritan AI agents are designed to function as either unconditional fair proposers or strict receivers within a bipartite hybrid population model, particularly in contexts like hiring and negotiation, emphasizing the importance of fairness in asymmetric social structures.

A security mechanism in web browsers that restricts how a document or script loaded from one origin can interact with resources from another origin. It prevents malicious scripts from accessing sensitive data across domains.

Security Assertion Markup Language — an open standard for exchanging authentication and authorization data between parties, particularly between an identity provider and a service provider. SAML enables single sign-on across web applications.

The process of selecting a representative subset of data from a larger dataset for analysis. Sampling techniques include random, stratified, and systematic sampling, each suited to different analytical goals.

An open benchmark designed to evaluate the capabilities of Large Language Models to escape from isolated sandbox environments, implemented as a Capture the Flag evaluation using a nested sandbox architecture.
A security mechanism that isolates running programs in a restricted environment to limit their access to system resources. Sandboxes are used to test untrusted code and contain potential malware.

Low-degree sandwiching polynomials are used to approximate target functions while providing bounds on function values, enhancing learning under distribution shifts and contamination. This method improves degree bounds for fundamental function classes.

The process of cleaning user input to remove or escape potentially dangerous characters that could be used in injection attacks. Input sanitization is a critical defense against XSS, SQL injection, and other vulnerabilities.
A non-parametric method for constructing smooth and arbitrage-free option price surfaces, utilizing linear programming for model calibration to market quotes.
SARA is a memory allocation strategy designed for mixed-criticality systems, addressing memory competition issues in edge devices to prevent performance degradation and missed deadlines.

The Sequential Adaptive Residual Quantization (SARQ) framework dynamically allocates code lengths based on item path entropy to improve retrieval efficiency and generalization in semantic ID-based generative retrieval systems.

Secure Access Service Edge — a network architecture that combines wide-area networking capabilities with cloud-native security functions like SWG, CASB, FWaaS, and ZTNA into a single cloud-delivered service model.

Syntactically Awesome Style Sheets — a preprocessor scripting language that is interpreted or compiled into CSS. Sass extends CSS with variables, nesting, mixins, and functions for more maintainable stylesheets.
A novel architecture for spoofing-robust automatic speaker verification that employs score-aware gated attention to dynamically modulate speaker embeddings based on countermeasure scores.

A software tool that determines whether a Boolean satisfiability problem has a solution. SAT solvers are used in formal verification, automated planning, cryptanalysis, and electronic design automation.
A locality-centered technique for selective token attention in transformers, reducing computational complexity by narrowing the attention scope to relevant tokens.
An approach to designing satellite constellations for global quantum networks, focusing on optimizing satellite orbital parameters to maximize entanglement generation rates.

This approach enables efficient deployment of UAV-mounted Base Stations (UAV-BSs) in dynamic environments without the need for extensive training, focusing on user satisfaction and adaptability to heterogeneous distributions.
A programming technique that enhances cache performance in high-performance attention kernels by reducing L2 cache misses, validated on NVIDIA GB10 hardware.
A framework that accelerates kernel memory access from userspace by reducing the overhead of traditional system call interfaces.

An automated code generation technique that creates the basic structure and boilerplate code for a new project, component, or feature. Scaffolding tools speed up development by providing a standardized starting point.

The ability of a system to handle increased workload by adding resources, either vertically by adding more power to existing machines or horizontally by adding more machines. Scalability is a key requirement for modern applications.
A framework that integrates hardware and algorithm design to overcome the limitations of traditional graphics pipelines in generative game engines, facilitating high-resolution neural simulations.

A method for automated test generation that leverages large language models to improve coverage rates by eliminating covered code and enhancing the generation of unit tests for complex software methods.
An extension of Gaussian processes designed to handle multiple correlated functional tasks and functional covariates, enabling principled uncertainty quantification in complex mechanical systems.
A method for attitude estimation using only scalar measurements from inertial sensors, incorporating gyroscope bias compensation for improved stability.
A theoretical framework connecting wavelet transforms with spiking neural networks, utilizing leaky integrate-and-fire neurons to implement discrete mother wavelets.

The ability to increase or decrease computing resources to handle changing workload demands. Vertical scaling (scaling up) adds more power to existing machines, while horizontal scaling (scaling out) adds more machines to distribute the load.

S.term: Scaling Law for Bandwidth Under Quantization
S.definition: A derived law that relates the bit depth of Analog to Digital Converters (ADCs) to the effective bandwidth of signals with $1/f^α$ power spectra, showing that each additional bit can approximately double the bandwidth under certain conditions.

A theoretical exploration of scaling laws that govern the trade-off between model quality and training costs in high-dimensional linear regression, particularly focusing on the effects of quantization.
A methodology for assessing the security exposure of IoT devices by analyzing scan data to identify population-level risks based on network configurations.
A structural parameter for directed acyclic graphs (DAGs) that respects edge directions, introduced to study parameterized complexity in graph problems, particularly in phylogenetic networks.

A spatial context attention mechanism designed for progressive Gaussian splatting, enhancing the efficiency of storage and transmission of 3D scene representations by optimizing the bitrate of Gaussian attributes.
A novel preprocessing method proposed for auditory attention decoding that enhances the performance of electroencephalography-based systems by capturing relevant features in auditory signals.

A type of data visualization that uses dots to represent the values of two different numeric variables, with each dot's position indicating values on each axis. Scatterplots reveal correlations and patterns in data.

A robustness evaluation method for large language models (LLMs) that assesses their sensitivity to variations in natural language descriptions of coding tasks, particularly in the context of code generation.

A novel simulation paradigm that allows for the ex-ante evaluation of urban traffic regulation policies by modeling both direct and indirect impacts on mobility systems, leveraging advances in data availability and computational power.
A pilot-free aggregation primitive for over-the-air federated distillation that provides phase-invariant estimates of weighted soft-label averages while mitigating noise-energy offsets.

S.term: SCENE (Semantic-aware Codec Enhancement with Neural Embeddings)
S.definition: A lightweight pre-processing framework that enhances perceptual fidelity in video by integrating semantic embeddings to mitigate compression artifacts from standard codecs.
A hierarchical agentic framework that generates simulation-ready indoor environments from natural language prompts, addressing the need for diverse and complex indoor spaces for robotic training.
An autonomous driving benchmark that incorporates multi-objective specifications and formal environment models to evaluate the performance of autonomous vehicles in complex traffic scenarios.
A generalization of matrix norms used in optimization problems, particularly for finding low-rank solutions in linear inverse problems.

SchedCP is a framework that enables autonomous Large Language Model (LLM) agents to optimize Linux schedulers by decoupling the semantic reasoning from execution, addressing the semantic gap in operating system scheduling.

A system component that determines the order and timing of task execution, allocating CPU time and resources to processes or threads. Schedulers are fundamental to operating systems and job orchestration systems.

The process of assigning computing resources and time slots to tasks, processes, or jobs. Scheduling algorithms optimize resource utilization, throughput, and response times in operating systems and distributed systems.

The formal structure that defines the organization of data in a database, API, or data format. Schemas specify tables, columns, data types, relationships, and constraints, serving as a blueprint that ensures data consistency and integrity.
The process of updating a database schema from one version to another while preserving existing data. Migration scripts track incremental changes and allow rollbacks if issues arise.
The process of verifying that data conforms to a predefined structure or schema, ensuring data integrity and consistency. Schema validation is used with JSON Schema, XML Schema, and database schemas.

SciDataCopilot is an agentic data preparation framework designed to facilitate the use of raw experimental data in AI-driven scientific discovery, addressing challenges related to data heterogeneity and domain expertise.
A unified open infrastructure that integrates 293 million scholarly papers from eight sources using DOI normalization while preserving source-level schemas, built on DuckDB and Parquet files.

The use of advanced computing capabilities to solve complex mathematical and scientific problems. It encompasses numerical simulation, optimization, and data analysis using tools like NumPy, SciPy, and MATLAB.

System for Cross-domain Identity Management — an open standard for automating the exchange of user identity information between identity domains and IT systems. SCIM simplifies user provisioning and deprovisioning across services.
The region of a program where a variable or function is accessible. Scope rules determine visibility and lifetime of identifiers and include block scope, function scope, and global scope.

A logical framework that introduces a segment modality and syntactic restrictions on data comparisons, providing characterizations for languages recognized by nondeterministic register automata with guessing.

ScrapeGraphAI-100k is a large-scale dataset created for LLM-based web information extraction, containing 93,695 examples collected from real-world extraction events across diverse domains and languages.
A stochastic optimization scheme that utilizes scattering transform coefficients with random paths to improve the efficiency of perceptual quality assessment in machine learning tasks.

An assistive technology application that converts on-screen content into speech or Braille output for visually impaired users. Screen readers interpret HTML semantics and ARIA attributes to navigate web content.
An innovative transparent antenna array integrated into the screens of mobile devices, designed to enhance performance in ultra-high frequency bands and mitigate user-induced blockage issues.
A fixed-vocabulary tokenization method for digital ink that decomposes pen movement into unit pixel steps, enabling efficient representation and compression of handwritten text.

A programming language designed for integrating and communicating with other programming languages, often used for automating tasks. Scripting languages like Python, JavaScript, and Bash are typically interpreted rather than compiled.

An agile framework for managing and completing complex projects through iterative development in time-boxed sprints (usually 2 weeks). Scrum defines roles (Product Owner, Scrum Master), ceremonies (standup, retrospective), and artifacts (backlog, sprint board).

Software-Defined Wide Area Network — an approach to network management that uses software-defined networking principles to manage and optimize WAN connections. SD-WAN improves performance and reduces costs for distributed enterprises.
SDA2E is a Sparse Dual Adversarial Attention-based AutoEncoder designed to refine decision boundaries in anomaly detection tasks, particularly for imbalanced datasets.

A federated prompt learning approach that addresses local-global discrepancies in multi-party settings by refining subspaces and controlling divergence, allowing for heterogeneous client data adaptation.

Software Development Kit — a collection of tools, libraries, documentation, and code samples that enables developers to build applications for a specific platform or service. SDKs abstract complex APIs into convenient, language-specific interfaces.

Software Development Life Cycle — a structured process for planning, creating, testing, and deploying software systems. SDLC frameworks include waterfall, agile, and iterative models that guide development from concept to maintenance.

Software-Defined Networking — an approach to network management that enables dynamic, programmatically efficient network configuration through decoupling the control plane from the data plane. SDN improves network agility and management.
A reference framework for estimating the carbon footprint of LLM inference, focusing on prompt-level carbon measurement to promote sustainability in AI practices.
SEAnet is a deep learning architecture designed for data series similarity search, utilizing Deep Embedding Approximation (DEA) techniques to improve performance on high-frequency and noisy datasets.

The process of finding and retrieving information from a collection of data, whether on the web, within databases, or across file systems. Search systems use indexing, ranking algorithms, and natural language processing.

A software system that indexes web content and retrieves relevant results in response to user queries. Search engines use crawling, indexing, and ranking algorithms to organize and present information.

A data structure optimized for fast retrieval of documents or records matching search queries. Search indexes store inverted mappings from terms to their locations, enabling sub-second full-text search.
A framework for path-centric reward shaping in agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation training, enhancing sample efficiency and addressing sparse outcome rewards.

A technique identified in the Adam optimizer that normalizes the second moment of gradients, providing a theoretical basis for its superior convergence properties compared to stochastic gradient descent (SGD).
A novel method for identifying groups in overlapping multi-agent swarms by analyzing agent trajectories to infer latent group structures without ground-truth supervision.

A novel closed-form expression derived for the achievable secrecy rate in multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) visible light communication (VLC) systems, considering peak and average intensity constraints.

The practice of securely storing, distributing, and managing sensitive credentials like API keys, passwords, certificates, and tokens. Secret management tools like HashiCorp Vault prevent secrets from being exposed in code or logs.

The tools and processes for securely handling sensitive configuration data such as database passwords, API keys, and encryption keys throughout the software development lifecycle. Solutions include Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, and SOPS.

Secure aggregation is a technique used in federated learning to protect client privacy by concealing individual updates during the model training process. It ensures that sensitive information is not leaked through inference attacks.

S.term: Secure and Interoperable Architecture for EHR Access Control
S.definition: A comprehensive architecture leveraging blockchain and IPFS for secure access control and sharing of electronic health records, ensuring compliance with privacy regulations.
A security standard that ensures a device boots using only software trusted by the manufacturer. Secure Boot verifies the digital signatures of boot loaders and operating system files.

The practice of writing software that is resistant to security vulnerabilities by following security guidelines and best practices. Secure coding prevents common attacks like injection, XSS, and buffer overflows.

A framework designed to enhance the security of deep neural network (DNN) accelerators by minimizing hardware resource demands and off-chip memory access overheads, ensuring data confidentiality and computational integrity.
A framework designed to enhance security in Robot Operating System 2 (ROS 2) environments, which is vulnerable to supply chain attacks that can exfiltrate sensitive data.

A cryptographic network protocol for operating network services securely over an unsecured network. SSH provides encrypted communication for remote command-line access, file transfer, and port forwarding.

The practice of protecting systems, networks, and data from unauthorized access, attacks, and damage. Information security encompasses confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data across all technology layers.

HTTP response headers that provide security directives to web browsers, including Content-Security-Policy, Strict-Transport-Security, X-Frame-Options, and X-Content-Type-Options. Security headers mitigate common web vulnerabilities.

A system that combines security information management and security event management to provide real-time analysis of security alerts generated by applications and network hardware. SIEM tools centralize log management and threat detection.

The process of evaluating a system's security mechanisms to identify vulnerabilities, weaknesses, and potential attack vectors. Security testing includes penetration testing, vulnerability scanning, and code review.
A physical or digital device used to prove a user's identity electronically. Security tokens can be hardware devices, software-based tokens, or one-time passwords used in multi-factor authentication.

A framework that automates the transformation of static anime illustrations into manipulatable 2.5D models by decomposing a single image into fully inpainted, semantically distinct layers with inferred drawing orders.

Initial data loaded into a database to populate it with the minimum information needed for an application to function or for testing purposes. Seed data scripts ensure consistent development and staging environments.
A tree data structure used for storing intervals or segments, allowing efficient querying of which segments contain a given point. Segment trees support range queries and updates in logarithmic time.

A runtime error that occurs when a program attempts to access a memory location that it is not allowed to access. Segfaults typically result from null pointer dereferences, buffer overflows, or stack overflows.
A framework that enhances the Segment Anything Model 2 (SAM2) for extracting high-fidelity contours from Scanning Electron Microscope images using a few-shot learning approach.
SELEBI is a signal-adaptive phase vocoder algorithm that reduces percussion smearing artifacts in time-stretched audio signals while maintaining stability and quality.
Quantitative theorems that establish necessary internal structures for artificial agents to achieve low average-case regret in decision-making tasks under uncertainty.
These theorems provide a quantitative framework for understanding the necessary internal structures required for artificial agents to achieve low average-case regret in uncertain environments.
A method that enhances uncertainty sets by identifying calibration examples that are exchangeable with test examples, particularly in interventional contexts.

SSA is an innovative attention mechanism that replaces traditional self-attention with a closed-form operator derived from the Kuramoto model, allowing tokens to be represented as oscillators with learnable frequencies and phases.
An open-source framework for automating web browser interactions, widely used for testing web applications. Selenium supports multiple programming languages and browsers for end-to-end testing.
An approach for processing probabilistic skyline queries in distributed edge computing environments that adapts to dynamic conditions, improving efficiency and reducing latency.
A phenomenon observed in large language models where the confidence of the model's responses changes systematically during multi-turn conversations, influenced by prior outputs.

A framework for scalable distributed submodular bandit optimization that enables agents to coordinate under realistic communication constraints, focusing on one-hop communication and minimal message sizes.

A technique that reduces uncertainty in the output of black-box AI systems by generating multiple outputs for the same input and assessing their consistency, thus providing a reliability level for the system's predictions.
A technique for dimensional aspect-based sentiment analysis that enhances prediction reliability by executing a LoRA-adapted large language model multiple times and retaining only consensus tuples.

S.term: Self-dual Stacked Quantum Low-Density Parity-Check Codes
S.definition: These codes facilitate fault-tolerant quantum computation by allowing the implementation of transversal Clifford gates, constructed by stacking non-self-dual qLDPC codes to enhance encoding rates and distances.
SERP is a replanning approach for embodied navigation that allows robots to learn from recent experiences and evolve their action models in real-time, enhancing resilience in complex environments.

The Self-Healing Router is a fault-tolerant orchestration architecture for LLM agents that prioritizes routing decisions over reasoning, utilizing parallel health monitors to manage tool outages and risk signals.
A system that can detect failures and automatically recover without human intervention. Self-healing mechanisms include automatic restarts, failover, and scaling to maintain service availability.

The practice of running and maintaining your own server infrastructure to host applications and services rather than relying on third-party cloud providers. Self-hosting gives full control over data and configuration.
A technique for enhancing reasoning capabilities in vision language models by refining reasoning paths through self-improvement, particularly in the context of visual question answering.
This method enhances spiking neurons by generating an internal prediction current based on input-output history, improving training efficiency and performance in Spiking Neural Networks.
A defense mechanism against backdoor attacks in Multimodal Diffusion Language Models, aimed at mitigating vulnerabilities while maintaining model performance on clean inputs.
A technique for constructing graph instances that reveal the algorithmic hardness of the clique problem by ensuring that both instances with and without a $k$-clique share identical properties.
An algorithm designed for leader election in anonymous networks that achieves self-stabilization with low memory complexity, addressing challenges in distributed systems.
An inference-time re-ranking method for diffusion planners that penalizes dynamically inconsistent plans, enhancing the robustness of offline reinforcement learning.

This technique employs unsupervised domain adaptation to improve visual 3D pose estimation of drone racing gates by enforcing geometric consistency, utilizing real image sequences annotated with drone odometry.

A machine learning approach where the model generates its own labels from the input data, creating supervisory signals from the data itself. It bridges the gap between supervised and unsupervised learning.

A paradigm that leverages vast amounts of unlabeled data to train models for speaker recognition, addressing the challenges of dependence on human-annotated data and improving generalization under unseen conditions.

S.term: Self-Supervised Learning for Non-Linear Inverse Problems
S.definition: A novel approach extending self-supervised learning to recover audio and images from clipped measurements, addressing challenges in training without ground truth references.

A technique that leverages self-supervised learning to extract speaker characteristics from speech data, analyzing how different dimensions of feature vectors represent attributes like pitch and gender.
An abstraction used for complex multi-query analytics over property graphs, which describes the graph schema and operations to improve the efficiency of natural language graph query generation.

A communication paradigm that focuses on conveying the intended meaning of messages rather than just the bit-wise transmission of data, addressing the challenges of semantic representation and extraction.

Semantic Communication (SemCom) is a paradigm shift in wireless communication that focuses on transmitting task-relevant semantics instead of mere symbols, enhancing efficiency but also introducing new vulnerabilities that require AI-driven defenses.
A model for collaborative data structures that enables explicit, local-first conflict resolution in asynchronous work environments, avoiding reliance on centralized coordination.

Two novel unsupervised metrics designed to evaluate the faithfulness of Large Language Models (LLMs) by analyzing information transformations and entropy measures in the context of question-answering tasks.
A framework designed for satellite-terrestrial networks that prioritizes task-relevant information over exact bit recovery, enhancing communication efficiency in low SNR conditions.

A failure mode in large language models (LLMs) where the model disregards locally defined semantics in favor of globally learned conventions, leading to incorrect reasoning in contexts with redefined terms.
An advanced approach to Flowchart Question Answering that enhances semantic and logic relationship understanding between nodes, improving the accuracy of answers derived from flowcharts.

Semantic Scheduling is an advanced scheduling approach that utilizes semantic analysis of processes to make context-aware scheduling decisions, prioritizing tasks based on their urgency and importance rather than traditional metrics.

A search technique that aims to understand the meaning and intent behind a query rather than just matching keywords. Semantic search uses natural language processing and knowledge graphs to deliver more relevant results.
A framework for object navigation that utilizes a persistent memory structure to consolidate past observations, enhancing performance in lifelong navigation tasks.

A versioning scheme using a three-part number format (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH) where each part conveys meaning about the nature of changes. SemVer helps developers understand the impact of updating dependencies.

An extension of the World Wide Web that adds machine-readable metadata to web content, enabling software agents to understand and process information meaningfully. It uses standards like RDF, OWL, and SPARQL.
A tree-structured memory module designed for conversational AI that allows agents to access and update structured memory efficiently, outperforming traditional flat memory approaches.

A paradigm for sixth generation (6G) networks that utilizes both specialist and large models to enhance the perception and utilization of channel semantics, improving performance in massive MIMO systems.
An unsupervised video summarization method that leverages semantic information to enhance keyframe selection, improving the stability and coherence of generated video summaries.

This technique addresses the limitations of traditional viewport prediction in 360-degree video streaming by utilizing semantic understanding to anticipate user attention shifts, thereby reducing rebuffering stalls during high engagement moments.
An advanced mixed-format quantization method for video diffusion transformers that optimizes per-block quantization formats to maintain semantic and temporal coherence.
A generative vocoder that synthesizes waveforms directly from semantic latents, addressing limitations of traditional Variational Autoencoders in audio generation.
A synchronization primitive used to control access to a shared resource by multiple threads. Semaphores maintain a count and allow threads to signal and wait, preventing race conditions.
A self-evolving hierarchical multi-agent system designed for industrial IoT predictive maintenance, which adapts to changing operational conditions while maintaining interpretability and efficiency.

Feed-Forward Semantic 3D Gaussian Splatting, a framework for reconstructing generalizable semantic fields from sparse image inputs using a dual-branch architecture to extract color and semantic features.
An advanced channel estimation algorithm for XL-MIMO systems that operates in the near-field region, adapting to the unique characteristics of spherical wavefronts for improved performance.

S.term: Semi-Markov Framework for Predictive Runtime Analysis
S.definition: A hybrid method that combines runtime tracing with probabilistic modeling to estimate worst-case execution times in real-time systems, enhancing temporal constraint detection.
A method developed for Image-Text Sentiment Analysis that balances intra-modal and inter-modal sentiment relationships using a two-step strategy for improved representation quality.
Graphs whose vertices are points in real space, with adjacency defined by semialgebraic predicates, allowing for compact adjacency labeling schemes through polynomial partitioning methods.

A material with electrical conductivity between a conductor and an insulator that forms the foundation of modern electronics. Semiconductors like silicon are used to manufacture integrated circuits, transistors, and diodes.
A technique used to convexify nonconvex structures in pricing models, particularly in discrete and nonlinear markets, allowing for efficient pricing through dual variable analysis.
SemLoco is a Reinforcement Learning framework designed to enable legged robots to avoid obstacles in cluttered environments by integrating high-level semantic understanding with low-level control.

SemPipes is a declarative programming model that integrates LLM-powered semantic data operators into tabular machine learning pipelines, allowing for natural language specifications of data transformations.
A data preprocessing system designed to optimize cache partitioning and data sampling for multimedia ML model training, reducing bottlenecks in the data ingestion pipeline.

The SC3 closed loop integrates sensing, communication, computing, and control processes in industrial automation, enhancing decision-making while addressing security vulnerabilities in wireless communications.

This automated system detects and analyzes potential sensitive information leaks from publicly accessible URLs, employing lexical filtering and dynamic rendering techniques to quantify the extent of such exposures.

A device that detects and responds to physical input from the environment such as light, heat, motion, moisture, or pressure. Sensors convert physical phenomena into electrical signals for processing by computers.

The use of natural language processing and text analysis to identify and extract subjective information from text. Sentiment analysis determines whether the expressed opinion is positive, negative, or neutral.

Search Engine Optimization — the practice of improving a website's visibility in organic search engine results through content quality, keyword optimization, technical performance, and link building. SEO drives organic traffic to websites.

SEP-YOLO is a novel framework designed for transparent object instance segmentation that integrates a dual-domain collaborative mechanism and a Frequency Domain Detail Enhancement Module to improve segmentation accuracy in challenging conditions.
A form of logic used for reasoning about programs that manipulate pointers and mutable data, providing a framework for verification in the context of heap memory.

A design principle that advocates dividing a program into distinct sections, each addressing a separate concern or responsibility. This principle improves modularity, maintainability, and reusability of code.
A novel lossless data compression method utilizing discrete latent transformers and reinforcement learning to optimize data storage and transmission without losing integrity.
A UML interaction diagram that shows how objects interact in a particular sequence of time. Sequence diagrams illustrate the order of messages exchanged between objects to carry out functionality.

S.term: Sequence-to-Sequence Learning for CNC Axis Reconstruction
S.definition: A machine learning approach that reconstructs CNC axis positions from accelerometer data, addressing security risks in machining process monitoring.
A series of transformer-based models designed for specific tasks in music source restoration, including music source separation, denoising, and dereverberation.

A scheduling variant for belief propagation decoders in quantum low-density parity-check codes that processes check nodes in a fixed order to stabilize message updates and improve error correction performance.
A method for continuous value prediction that utilizes regression techniques to handle complex and long-tailed data distributions, improving performance in recommendation systems.
A modeling language designed for automatically verifying the serializability of concurrent programs, ensuring that concurrent executions are equivalent to some serial execution.

A method of transmitting data where bits are sent sequentially one at a time over a single communication channel. Serial protocols like UART, SPI, and I2C are fundamental in embedded systems and hardware interfaces.
The process of converting an object's state into a format that can be stored or transmitted and reconstructed later. Common serialization formats include JSON, XML, Protocol Buffers, and MessagePack.

A computer or software system that provides resources, data, or services to other computers (clients) over a network. Servers handle requests for web pages, files, email, databases, and other networked services.

The process of securing a server by reducing its attack surface through disabling unnecessary services, applying patches, configuring firewalls, and enforcing strict access controls. Hardening is a fundamental security practice.

A server push technology enabling a client to receive automatic updates from a server via an HTTP connection. SSE is simpler than WebSockets for scenarios where only the server needs to push data to the client.
The process of rendering web pages on the server before sending them to the client browser. SSR improves initial page load time and search engine optimization compared to client-side rendering.

A cloud computing execution model where the cloud provider dynamically manages server allocation and scaling. Developers write functions that run in response to events without provisioning servers, paying only for actual compute time consumed.

A cloud execution model where the cloud provider dynamically manages the allocation and provisioning of servers. Developers write functions that run in response to events without managing infrastructure.

The automatic detection of devices and services on a network, enabling microservices to find and communicate with each other. Service discovery is essential in dynamic container orchestration environments.

A commitment between a service provider and a client that defines the expected level of service, including uptime guarantees, response times, and remedies for breaches. SLAs establish accountability in cloud services.
A quantitative measure of some aspect of the level of service being provided, such as request latency, error rate, or throughput. SLIs are the foundation for defining SLOs and SLAs.
A target value or range of values for a service level that is measured by a service level indicator. SLOs set the reliability targets that a team aims to meet.
The set of practices and processes for designing, delivering, managing, and improving IT services to meet business needs. ITIL is the most widely adopted framework for IT service management.

A dedicated infrastructure layer for handling service-to-service communication in microservices architectures. Service meshes like Istio and Linkerd provide traffic management, security, and observability.

A script that runs in the background of a web browser, separate from the web page, enabling features like offline support, push notifications, and background sync. Service workers are key to Progressive Web Apps.

A Java programming language class used to extend the capabilities of a server, typically a web server. Servlets handle HTTP requests and responses and form the foundation of Java web application development.

A mechanism for maintaining state between a client and server across multiple HTTP requests. Sessions store user-specific data server-side, identified by a session ID transmitted via cookies or URL parameters.
A web storage mechanism that stores data for the duration of a browser session, with data cleared when the tab or browser is closed. Unlike localStorage, sessionStorage is scoped to a single tab.
A trustless and scalable reward settlement protocol for federated learning on permissionless blockchains, designed to minimize economic friction in decentralized environments.

SEval-NAS is a search-agnostic evaluation mechanism for neural architecture search that converts architectures into strings and embeds them as vectors to predict performance metrics, facilitating flexible evaluation across different hardware.
An efficient zero-shot visual language navigation framework that combines slow and fast cognitive collaboration to reduce latency and computation costs in navigation tasks.

A comprehensive dataset designed for evaluating the perceptual quality of Singing Face Generation, addressing the lack of singing face datasets and the challenges posed by the domain gap between singing and talking.
SSH File Transfer Protocol — a secure file transfer protocol that provides file access, transfer, and management over a reliable data stream. SFTP uses SSH encryption to protect data in transit.

SGAgent is a suggestion-guided framework for repository-level software repair that integrates large language models to analyze, localize bugs, and generate patches, addressing the reasoning gap in existing repair approaches.

SGMA (Semantic-Guided Modality-Aware Segmentation) addresses Incomplete Multimodal Semantic Segmentation (IMSS) by integrating semantic guidance to improve segmentation performance despite missing modalities.
Spectrogram-Guided Phonetic Alignment, a four-stage pipeline that enhances phonetic alignment for audio language models, improving the accuracy of Shapley value explanations.

Secure Hash Algorithm — a family of cryptographic hash functions published by NIST that produce fixed-size hash values from input data. SHA-256 and SHA-3 are widely used for data integrity verification and digital signatures.

A program that runs on a graphics processing unit to calculate rendering effects such as lighting, shadows, and color. Shaders are written in specialized languages like GLSL or HLSL for real-time graphics.
A web standard that provides encapsulation for JavaScript, CSS, and templating in a web component. Shadow DOM keeps the component's internal structure hidden from the rest of the page.

A copy of an object that duplicates only the top-level structure, with nested objects still referencing the same memory locations as the original. Understanding shallow versus deep copying is important for avoiding unintended side effects.
A variant of physics-informed neural networks that employs shallow architectures for solving nonlinear PDEs, optimized using the Levenberg-Marquardt algorithm for efficient parameter tuning.
A derivative-informed neural operator framework designed for shape optimization under uncertainty, enhancing the efficiency of PDE-constrained shape optimization by encoding geometric variability.

A methodology that separates the short-term energy (gain) and normalized structure (shape) of audio signals in neural audio codecs, enhancing robustness to global signal level variations and improving bitrate-distortion performance.
A framework for continuum robots that enables geometry-aware control by encoding robot shapes from visual inputs, enhancing interaction with complex environments.
A scalable image-conditioned 3D editing framework that addresses the trade-off between visual controllability, geometric consistency, and scalability in 3D asset manipulation.
A horizontal partition of data in a database or search engine where each shard is held on a separate server to distribute the load. Sharding is a key strategy for scaling databases horizontally.
A novel mechanism for concurrent stack implementations that combines sharding and fetch&increment strategies to enhance performance and reduce contention.

A database partitioning technique that splits large datasets across multiple database instances called shards. Each shard holds a subset of the data, enabling horizontal scaling and improved query performance.

A memory region that can be simultaneously accessed by multiple processes for high-speed inter-process communication. Shared memory eliminates the overhead of data copying between processes but requires synchronization.

Shared program state is a programming abstraction that facilitates interoperability between natural language code and formal programming languages, streamlining the integration of LLMs with traditional coding practices.
An innovative approach for efficient multi-LLM serving that enables cross-model sharing of decode execution, optimizing resource utilization in disaggregated settings.
A video generation framework that facilitates multi-agent shared world modeling, incorporating diverse scenes and interactive trajectories to enhance collaborative video generation.
A framework for stock selection and portfolio optimization that utilizes the Sharpe ratio to identify stocks with strong risk-adjusted performance while considering liquidity constraints.

A mathematical modeling framework that extends traditional neural networks to handle complex relationships in data, showing superior performance in biomedical applications compared to conventional graph neural networks.

A discrete gauge-theoretic framework that replaces the traditional single-global-dictionary approach with a collection of local semantic charts, each representing a context with its own feature space and information-geometric metric.
The character sequence #! at the beginning of a script file that specifies the interpreter to use for execution. For example, #!/usr/bin/env python3 tells the system to run the script with Python 3.

A command-line interface that provides access to the operating system's services, allowing users to execute commands, run scripts, and manage files. Common shells include Bash, Zsh, Fish, and PowerShell.
A script written for a command-line interpreter that automates sequences of commands. Shell scripts are used for system administration, deployment automation, and task scheduling.
A precision-aware 1D feature-driven CNN accelerator designed for low-energy acoustic detection in UAVs, utilizing a shared multi-precision datapath.
A practice of moving testing earlier in the software development lifecycle to find and fix defects sooner. Shift-left testing reduces the cost of fixing bugs and improves overall software quality.

A shift-tree is a new data structure used in dynamic edge coloring that tracks multiple possible recolorings of a graph, enabling efficient maintenance of proper edge colorings with minimal recoloring during edge updates.
The Sequential Hierarchical Integration Network (SHINE) is an architecture for reconstructing speech-silence sequences from MEG signals, addressing challenges in cognitive neuroscience.
A framework that reformulates ship trajectory prediction as a text-to-text generation problem, utilizing dynamic prompts to guide large language models in predicting ship movements.

A leading e-commerce platform that enables businesses to create online stores with customizable themes, payment processing, and inventory management. Shopify provides APIs and a Liquid templating language for developers.
A technique for identifying short races in observed execution traces, where a race type R is detected within a specified window size w, allowing efficient monitoring of concurrent programs.

A monitoring framework designed to detect short races in observed traces, specifically for race types such as happens-before and sync-preserving races. The framework includes efficient detection algorithms that optimize space usage.
A technique for enhancing time-domain cochlear transmission line models by correcting for short-wave effects, improving the simulation of basilar-membrane displacement.
An algorithm that finds the path between two vertices in a graph such that the sum of the weights of the edges is minimized. Dijkstra's algorithm and Bellman-Ford are classic examples.

The shuffle model of differential privacy (Shuffle-DP) enhances privacy protection by locally perturbing client updates and globally anonymizing them through shuffling, although it introduces vulnerabilities to adversarial attacks.

A novel 'Intent-to-Operation' framework that allows users to express interaction intents through natural eye-hand motions in Virtual Reality, reducing learning costs and improving interaction error tolerance.

An observable change in state that occurs during the execution of a function beyond returning a value, such as modifying a global variable, writing to a file, or making a network request. Minimizing side effects improves code predictability.

A design pattern where a helper component is deployed alongside a primary application to provide supporting features. In Kubernetes, sidecar containers handle logging, monitoring, or security for the main container.

SIDSense is an edge AI framework designed for database-free operation of TV White Space networks, aimed at enhancing disaster-resilient connectivity in vulnerable regions by ensuring compliance and operational continuity.

Security Information and Event Management — a solution that aggregates and analyzes security data from across an organization's infrastructure to detect threats, manage incidents, and ensure compliance. SIEM systems provide real-time monitoring and alerting.
A pretraining method for vision-language models that aligns medical imaging data with textual reports using contrastive learning techniques to improve retrieval and classification tasks.
A Semantic-Grounded Instruction-Driven Generative Multi-Task Recommender designed to adapt to evolving trends and diverse recommendation tasks by grounding item entities in general semantics.

A hardware-aware heterogeneous quantization method for edge deep neural network inference that optimally allocates different bitwidths to layers based on their robustness, improving resource usage and accuracy.
A scalable in-generation watermarking technique for AI-generated videos that enables blind extraction, ensuring content protection without degrading video quality.
Signal First-Order Logic (SFO) is a formalism for expressing real-time specifications over hybrid systems, enhanced with a robustness-based quantitative semantics for richer property evaluation.
The analysis, modification, and synthesis of signals such as sound, images, and sensor data. Digital signal processing is fundamental to telecommunications, audio engineering, and image processing.

A numerical approach for solving multi-dimensional linear-quadratic stochastic control problems by transforming them into deterministic convex quadratic polynomial optimization using linear signature functionals.
A compact binary structure representing a graph, consisting of a rooted binary tree and 2-colored edges, used to compute shortest-path trees efficiently.
A variant of stochastic gradient descent that utilizes sign information for updates, exhibiting unique scaling laws under specific feature decay conditions.
A theoretical framework analyzing the performance of SignSGD in linear regression under power-law random features, identifying unique effects such as drift-normalization and noise reshaping.
A security risk in large language model systems where adversarial instructions embedded in automatically generated URL previews can lead to unintended data exfiltration without user awareness.
An ultra-low power wearable system designed for EMG-based silent speech recognition, enabling noise-robust and privacy-preserving human-computer interfaces.
An adhesive-free, solvent-reversible bonding technique for integrating silicone with glossy paper, enabling robust interfaces for soft robotics applications.

A scalable benchmark environment for evaluating distributed coordination in multi-agent systems, focusing on the Communication-Reasoning Gap across various algorithmic tasks and communication complexity levels.
A system that simulates A/B testing using persona-conditioned AI agents, allowing for rapid design evaluation without reliance on real user traffic, thus speeding up the iteration process.

Single Instruction, Multiple Data — a parallel computing architecture where a single instruction operates on multiple data points simultaneously. SIMD is used in graphics processing, scientific computing, and multimedia applications.

A mathematical measure of how alike two data objects are, used in clustering, recommendation systems, and information retrieval. Common similarity metrics include cosine similarity, Euclidean distance, and Jaccard index.

SIMPLE-ICS is a virtualized industrial testbed designed for realistic end-to-end emulation of Advanced Persistent Threats (APTs) across integrated IT, operational technology (OT), and Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) networks, facilitating comprehensive cybersecurity research.

Simplicial Normal Form (SNF) is a concept derived from topological relational theory that characterizes the acyclicity of functional dependencies in relational schema design using homological invariants.

An extension of homotopy type theory that incorporates directed morphisms, allowing for the composition of types known as Segal types, which enhances the expressiveness of type systems in formal proofs.
SimRecon is a compositional scene reconstruction framework that utilizes a 'Perception-Generation-Simulation' pipeline to create object-centric representations from real-world video inputs.
A data augmentation strategy that uses simulated ATFs to improve own voice detection in hearing aids, allowing for effective machine learning without complex hardware setups.
A probabilistic optimization algorithm inspired by the annealing process in metallurgy. It explores the solution space by accepting worse solutions with decreasing probability to escape local optima.

The use of computer models to replicate the behavior of real-world systems or processes over time. Simulations are used in engineering, physics, economics, and game development for testing and prediction.
The Simulation-Based Inference framework utilizes neural networks for parameter estimation in large-scale agent-based models, addressing computational constraints in exploring parameter spaces.

A novel approach that frames augmented reading as a simulation-based optimization problem, utilizing resource-rational models to systematically evaluate text user interfaces and improve comprehension.

S.term: Simulation-based Policies with Asymmetric Real-world Residuals (SPARR)
S.definition: A hybrid approach combining simulation-trained policies with real-world residuals to improve robotic assembly performance by bridging the sim-to-real gap.

A software tool that imitates the behavior of a hardware device, operating system, or environment for testing and development purposes. Simulators allow developers to test applications without physical hardware.

An agent powered by large language models that generates and optimizes deep neural network (DNN) accelerator simulators from natural language descriptions, utilizing techniques like In-Context Learning and Chain-of-Thought reasoning.
A web application that loads a single HTML page and dynamically updates content as the user interacts with the app. SPAs provide a fluid user experience similar to desktop applications.
A component in a system whose failure would cause the entire system to stop functioning. Eliminating single points of failure through redundancy is essential for building highly available systems.

An authentication mechanism that allows users to access multiple applications with one set of login credentials. SSO reduces password fatigue, simplifies user management, and can improve security when combined with MFA.
QCL is a technique that utilizes quantum circuits to model the dynamics of volatility time series, providing a novel approach to capturing volatility asymmetry in financial data.

S.term: Single-Source Negative Weight Shortest Paths Algorithm
S.definition: A randomized algorithm that solves the single-source shortest paths problem on directed graphs with negative weights, achieving nearly linear time complexity for dense graphs.

S.term: Single-Type Binary Variables in Unit Commitment
S.definition: A formulation of the unit commitment problem that utilizes a single type of binary variable to improve the efficiency of power production scheduling.

A creational design pattern that restricts the instantiation of a class to a single instance and provides a global point of access to it. Singletons are used for managing shared resources like database connections or configuration.

A creational design pattern that restricts a class to a single instance and provides a global point of access to that instance. Singletons are used for managing shared resources like database connections.
A Bayesian framework that characterizes the geometry of the loss landscape in machine learning, linking local learning coefficients to generalization properties.
An optimization technique for tree-structure indexes in CXL-based heterogeneous memory, enhancing performance by adapting to hardware characteristics and memory semantics.

Session Initiation Protocol — a signaling protocol used for initiating, maintaining, and terminating communication sessions for voice, video, and messaging applications. SIP is the foundation of most VoIP systems.

SIREN is an AI-driven framework that enables voice-driven perception for UAV-assisted networks, facilitating the integration of voice communications into automated network management for emergency response.
A framework for single-image reflection removal that generates a synthetic dataset through path tracing, leveraging a Large Multimodal Model to enhance reflection removal accuracy.
SIRUP is a diffusion-based virtual upmixer for steering vectors, enabling highly-directive spatialization from first-order ambisonics data using a latent diffusion model architecture.

A discipline that applies software engineering practices to infrastructure and operations to create scalable and reliable systems. SRE teams use error budgets, SLOs, and automation to balance reliability and velocity.

A novel architecture for a Rydberg atomic receiver that utilizes six-wave mixing to enable wideband radio frequency-to-optical quantum transduction, overcoming bandwidth limitations of conventional electromagnetically induced transparency.

SkeFi is a framework for cross-modal knowledge transfer in wireless skeleton-based action recognition, utilizing non-invasive sensors like LiDAR and mmWave to improve performance in challenging environments.
A novel framework for generating editable dynamic 3D Gaussians from monocular video input, using a hierarchical articulated representation to separate rigid and non-rigid motion.

In the Sketch&Patch++ framework, Gaussians are categorized into Sketch Gaussians, which capture high-frequency features like edges, and Patch Gaussians, which represent broader, smoother regions, enabling efficient structure-aware 3D Gaussian representation.

Sketch&Patch++ is a hybrid representation framework that categorizes Gaussian functions into Sketch Gaussians for high-frequency features and Patch Gaussians for low-frequency regions, mimicking traditional artistic techniques in 3D representation.
A framework that transforms 2D storyboard sketches into coherent 3D multi-human animations with fine-grained control over various motion parameters, utilizing a sketch-driven diffusion prior.
A predictive model for touch pointing success rates that accounts for the influence of screen edges on tapping accuracy, particularly for targets placed near screen boundaries.

This framework utilizes skill-based autonomous agents powered by Large Language Models to extract high-fidelity datasets from unstructured scientific literature, automating the curation process and reducing human error.
A benchmark designed to evaluate the ability of LLM agents to learn and reuse higher-level tool compositions, focusing on their capacity to manage long-horizon workflows with recurring structures.

SkillFortify is a formal analysis framework designed to enhance the security of agentic AI skill ecosystems by providing guarantees against supply chain attacks, moving beyond heuristic-based detection methods.
A benchmark that evaluates the vulnerability of large language model (LLM) agents to prompt injection attacks through skill files, highlighting the risks associated with extending agent capabilities.

S.term: Skin in the Game Experimental Design
S.definition: A novel experimental design where decision-makers incur direct costs when making choices for others, revealing differences in risk and time preferences compared to self-interested decisions.

A large-scale dataset designed for skin tone classification that addresses biases in existing datasets by providing granular, annotated examples across a spectrum of skin tones, facilitating fairer machine learning applications.
A learned, compact representation for skinning weights in 3D models, utilizing a FSQ-CVAE to optimize the rigging process by capturing intrinsic sparsity.

A probabilistic data structure that allows fast search, insertion, and deletion operations by maintaining multiple layers of linked lists. Skip lists provide average-case logarithmic time complexity similar to balanced trees.

A high-fidelity 3D head reconstruction method that utilizes multi-view normal prediction to generate detailed head geometries quickly, overcoming limitations of traditional photogrammetry and optimization-based methods.

A scalable 3D photonic in-memory tensor core architecture designed for real-time AI inference, overcoming limitations of conventional photonic architectures through innovative design in topology and wavelength routing.

Service Level Agreement — a contract between a service provider and a customer that defines the expected level of service including uptime guarantees, response times, and remedies for failures. SLAs establish accountability and expectations.
A framework for managing inference tasks across heterogeneous computing environments (on-device, RAN-edge, cloud) while ensuring service level agreements (SLAs) are met in real-time AI applications.

SLAM-LLM is an open-source multimodal large language model framework designed to support speech, language, audio, and music processing, facilitating the development of customized models for diverse audio-language tasks.
A novel framework for lip-to-speech synthesis that utilizes a hierarchical subspace latent diffusion model to directly map visual lip movements to a continuous latent space.

Service Level Indicator — a quantitative measure of some aspect of the level of service provided, such as request latency, error rate, or throughput. SLIs are the metrics used to evaluate whether SLOs are being met.
A method for reconstructing latent states and nonlinear restoring forces in multidimensional systems by utilizing periodic multisine excitation and relaxed sensing requirements.

Service Level Objective — a target value or range for a service level measured by an SLI. SLOs define the reliability targets that a team commits to and guide decisions about feature development versus reliability work.
A category of language models that are resource-efficient and suitable for on-device deployment, particularly evaluated for their effectiveness in human-robot interaction scenarios.
An approach that utilizes small language models to drive auto-parallelization in compilers for heterogeneous systems, improving performance over traditional heuristic-based methods.
This concept involves designing robust information strategies in multi-agent systems that guide agents to achieve perfect coordination through a simple threshold rule based on their utilities.
A transformer-based surrogate model for scalable mesh-free aerodynamic simulations that predicts physical quantities directly from raw geometries, avoiding the computational cost of mesh generation.
A self-executing contract with the terms of the agreement directly written into code on a blockchain. Smart contracts automatically enforce and execute agreements without intermediaries.

Tools designed to detect vulnerabilities in smart contracts, which have been evaluated primarily on synthetic datasets, raising concerns about their effectiveness in real-world applications and the trust developers place in them.

A residence equipped with internet-connected devices that enable remote monitoring and management of appliances, lighting, heating, and security systems. Smart home technology relies on IoT protocols and voice assistants.

A SmartNIC is a network interface card designed to offload data processing tasks such as decoding and query execution, significantly reducing the computational load on CPUs and improving query performance in data lakes.

SMDIM is a diffusing strategy for efficient long-sequence diffusion modeling in symbolic music generation, combining global structure construction with light local refinement to reduce training and inference costs.

A preliminary level of testing that verifies the most critical functions of a software application work correctly. Smoke tests are run after each build to quickly identify major issues before deeper testing.
An algorithmic framework that generates datasets for rapid recalibration of option pricing models, integrating with supervised machine learning models for efficient valuation.
A method for estimating quantile parameters using a generalized method of moments framework that incorporates non-linearities and dependent data, enhancing computational tractability and consistency.
A model for cross-domain recommendation that addresses the user cold-start problem by leveraging correlations between items across different domains.
A property of neural networks that use smooth activation functions, allowing them to achieve optimal approximation and estimation rates by increasing width alone.
A self-attention-based multi-scale object detection algorithm specifically designed for snap detection in automated assembly, enhancing robustness and precision in challenging visual scenarios.
Short Message Service — a text messaging service component of phone, web, or mobile communication systems that allows the exchange of short text messages between fixed line or mobile phone devices.

Simple Mail Transfer Protocol — the standard protocol for sending email messages between servers. SMTP handles the routing and delivery of outgoing mail and works alongside POP3 or IMAP for receiving messages.

A testing technique that captures the output of a component or function and compares it against a stored reference snapshot. If the output changes unexpectedly, the test fails, helping catch unintended regressions.
An adaptive mixed-precision training framework for large language models that optimizes subbyte precision during pretraining, enhancing model quality and training efficiency.

Simple Network Management Protocol — an internet standard protocol for collecting and organizing information about managed devices on IP networks. SNMP enables network administrators to monitor and manage network performance.

A distributed unique ID generation scheme originally developed by Twitter that produces roughly time-ordered 64-bit identifiers. Snowflake IDs encode a timestamp, machine ID, and sequence number for uniqueness without coordination.

Simple Object Access Protocol — a messaging protocol for exchanging structured information in web services using XML. SOAP provides a standardized way to communicate across different platforms and languages.
A learning-based approach for efficient design space exploration in High-Level Synthesis (HLS), aimed at identifying optimal hardware architectures under constraints.
An agent-based model that simulates wealth and income distributions, focusing on the effects of consumption on wealth inequality through a dimensionless parameter.

The psychological manipulation of people into performing actions or divulging confidential information for malicious purposes. Phishing, pretexting, and baiting are common social engineering techniques.

Online platforms and applications that enable users to create and share content and participate in social networking. Social media platforms are central to digital marketing, customer engagement, and brand building.
A game-theoretic framework that adjusts decision-making in multi-agent systems by balancing individual objectives with group welfare through a social weight parameter.
An endpoint for sending or receiving data across a computer network. Sockets provide the mechanism for inter-process communication and are the foundation of network programming.
A domain-specific language developed for analyzing hardware documentation, enabling more accurate descriptions of hardware semantics and security properties.

Semantic-Oriented Distributional Alignment is a novel paradigm for generative recommendation that utilizes probability distributions over multi-layer codebooks, enhancing information retention and joint optimization between tokenizers and generative recommenders.
A reinforcement learning algorithm designed for continuous action spaces, utilized for optimizing cryptocurrency portfolio management by maximizing cumulative returns.
An advanced symbol detection technique that balances performance and complexity in MIMO systems by effectively mitigating inter-user interference.
A physics-inspired learning technique for non-line-of-sight imaging that reconstructs scenes from indirect measurements, utilizing shadows cast by hidden objects.
A differentiable reparameterization layer that integrates hard constraints into deep learning, addressing gradient saturation issues by mapping predictions onto constraint boundaries.

The high-level structure of a software system, defining the components, their relationships, and the principles governing their design and evolution. Architecture decisions shape the system's quality attributes.
A formal inventory of all components, libraries, and dependencies used in a software application. SBOMs help organizations track vulnerabilities, manage licenses, and maintain supply chain security.
A practice that identifies open-source components in a codebase and detects known vulnerabilities, licensing issues, and outdated dependencies. SCA tools help manage supply chain security risks.
A novel standard for diskless booting that allows computers to boot from any location without the need for local storage, simplifying the user experience.

The structured process of planning, creating, testing, and deploying software systems. SDLC frameworks like Agile, Waterfall, and Spiral define phases and deliverables to manage software projects effectively.

The systematic application of engineering principles to the design, development, testing, and maintenance of software. Software engineering encompasses methodologies, tools, and best practices for building reliable systems.
This proposed architecture aims to create an ecosystem of reusable and interoperable NLP tools for requirements engineering, addressing the challenges of tool comparison and long-term sustainability.

The process of evaluating software to find defects and verify it meets specified requirements. Testing encompasses unit testing, integration testing, system testing, acceptance testing, and various other methodologies.

An approach to networking that uses software-based controllers to manage network traffic, separating the control plane from the data plane. SDN enables more flexible and programmable network management.
A testbed designed to quantify the resilience of Wi-Fi networks against deauthentication attacks by evaluating various wireless security configurations.

SOLAR is a recommendation system that employs SVD-optimized lifelong attention, reducing the computational complexity of attention mechanisms in Transformers while preserving the softmax mechanism for effective credit assignment.

An acronym for five object-oriented design principles — Single Responsibility, Open-Closed, Liskov Substitution, Interface Segregation, and Dependency Inversion — that promote maintainable, scalable, and flexible software design.
Five design principles for writing maintainable and scalable object-oriented code: Single Responsibility, Open-Closed, Liskov Substitution, Interface Segregation, and Dependency Inversion.
A method for providing explainable justifications in mission planning by deriving explanations directly from the optimization model, ensuring faithfulness and causal attribution.
A topology-aware tokenization method that combines vector quantization with Self-Organizing Maps, enabling discrete generative models to maintain semantic structure in token space.

Sonar-TS is a neuro-symbolic framework designed for Natural Language Querying of Time Series Databases, utilizing a Search-Then-Verify pipeline to improve the retrieval of meaningful events and intervals from extensive temporal records.
SongSong is a music generation model that restores ancient Chinese SongCi by predicting melody and generating singing voice and accompaniment, showcasing advancements in music generation.
A dynamically-updating benchmark for open Lean tasks sourced from real-world formalization projects, aimed at improving the usability and capability of AI provers in formal mathematics.

A data structure that maintains elements in sorted order while providing efficient insertion, deletion, and lookup operations. Redis sorted sets are commonly used for leaderboards and priority queues.

The process of arranging data elements in a specific order, typically ascending or descending. Sorting is one of the most fundamental operations in computer science with algorithms like quicksort, mergesort, and heapsort.

An algorithm that puts elements of a list into a predetermined order. Common sorting algorithms include quicksort, mergesort, heapsort, and timsort, each with different time and space complexity trade-offs.
